conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の国際(こくさい)会議(かいぎ)ともなると、世界中(せかいじゅう)から著名(ちょめい)な研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)が集(あつ)まりますね。
question.romaji: Konkai no kokusai kaigi to mo naru to, sekaijuu kara chomei na kenkyuusha ga atsumarimasu ne.
question.arti: When it comes to an international conference of this caliber, renowned researchers from around the world gather, don't they.

choices.jepang: そうですね。ですから、発表(はっぴょう)の準備(じゅんび)はそれほど気(き)にしなくてもよいでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Desu kara, happyou no junbi wa sorehodo ki ni shinakute mo yoi deshou.
choices.arti: That's true. Therefore, there is no need to worry too much about presentation preparations.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。それだけに、発表(はっぴょう)の準備(じゅんび)も万全(ばんぜん)を期(き)さなければなりません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Sore dake ni, happyou no junbi mo banzen wo ki sa nakereba narimasen.
choices.arti: You are absolutely right, Professor. Precisely because of that, the presentation preparations must be flawless.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'You are absolutely right... the presentation preparations must be flawless' because it responds with awareness of the high responsibility at an international conference, matching the nuance of 「ともなると」 which implies significant consequences. Meanwhile, 'there is no need to worry too much about presentation preparations' downplays the importance of preparation, contradicting the serious situation described.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、言語(げんご)の壁(かべ)をものともせず、堂々(どうどう)と英語(えいご)で基調(きちょう)講演(こうえん)をされるご予定(よてい)でしょうか。
question.romaji: Sensei, gengo no kabe wo mono to mo sezu, doudou to eigo de kichou kouen wo sareru go yotei deshou ka.
question.arti: Professor, do you plan to deliver the keynote speech boldly in English without being deterred by the language barrier?

choices.jepang: いや、言語(げんご)の問題(もんだい)はやはり懸念(けねん)されますので、通訳(つうやく)を依頼(いらい)するつもりです。
choices.romaji: Iya, gengo no mondai wa yahari kenen saremasu no de, tsuuyaku wo irai suru tsumori desu.
choices.arti: No, language remains a concern, so I plan to request an interpreter.

choices.jepang: ええ、多少(たしょう)の不安(ふあん)はありますが、長年(ながねん)の研究(けんきゅう)の成果(せいか)を世界(せかい)に伝(つた)えるべく、英語(えいご)で臨(のぞ)む所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Ee, tashoo no fuan wa arimasu ga, naganen no kenkyuu no seika wo sekai ni tsutaeru beku, eigo de nozomu shozonde su.
choices.arti: Yes, I have some apprehension, but with the determination to convey years of research findings to the world, I intend to present in English.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'I have some apprehension, but I intend to present in English' because it demonstrates strong determination to face obstacles, matching the spirit of 「をものともせず」. Meanwhile, 'language remains a concern, so I will request an interpreter' effectively surrenders to the language barrier, contrary to the nuance of courage in the question.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 参加者(さんかしゃ)たちは、新(あたら)しい発見(はっけん)を聞(き)かんばかりに、会場(かいじょう)に詰(つ)めかけておりました。
question.romaji: Sankasha-tachi wa, atarashii hakken wo kikan bakari ni, kaijou ni tsumekakete orimashita.
question.arti: The participants crowded into the room as if eager to hear the new discovery immediately.

choices.jepang: 参加者(さんかしゃ)があまり関心(かんしん)を示(しめ)さなかったのは、発表(はっぴょう)の内容(ないよう)が平凡(へいぼん)だったからでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Sankasha ga amari kanshin wo shimesanakatta no wa, happyou no naiyou ga heibon datta kara deshou ka.
choices.arti: Did the participants show little interest because the presentation content was mediocre?

choices.jepang: それほど皆(みな)さんの期待(きたい)が高(たか)かったということですね。発表(はっぴょう)の反響(はんきょう)はいかがでしたか。
choices.romaji: Sore hodo minna-san no kitai ga takakatta to iu koto desu ne. Happyou no hankyou wa ikaga deshita ka.
choices.arti: That means everyone's expectations were indeed very high. How was the response to the presentation?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'everyone's expectations were indeed very high' because it captures the nuance of high expectations from 「んばかりに」 which depicts the great enthusiasm of participants. Meanwhile, 'the participants showed little interest because the content was mediocre' contradicts the context -- participants were so enthusiastic they crowded in, far from being disinterested.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 完璧(かんぺき)な発表(はっぴょう)とはいかないまでも、主要(しゅよう)な論点(ろんてん)はしっかりと伝(つた)えることができたと思(おも)います。
question.romaji: Kanpeki na happyou to wa ikanai made mo, shuuyou na ronten wa shikkari to tsutaeru koto ga dekita to omoimasu.
question.arti: Although it may not have been a perfect presentation, I believe the key points were conveyed effectively.

choices.jepang: 謙虚(けんきょ)なお言葉(ことば)ですが、聴衆(ちょうしゅう)の反応(はんのう)を見(み)る限(かぎ)り、十分(じゅうぶん)に成功(せいこう)されたご様子(ようす)でした。
choices.romaji: Kenkyo na okotoba desu ga, chyoushuu no hannou wo miru kagiri, juubun ni seikou sareta go yousu deshita.
choices.arti: Those are humble words, but judging from the audience's reaction, the presentation appears to have been very successful.

choices.jepang: そのご謙遜(けんそん)こそが先生(せんせい)のお人柄(ひとがら)ですね。しかし、核心(かくしん)をついた論点(ろんてん)は国際(こくさい)社会(しゃかい)に確(たし)かな一石(いっせき)を投(な)じたはずです。
choices.romaji: Sono go kenson koso ga sensei no ohitogara desu ne. Shikashi, kakushin wo tsuita ronten wa kokusai shakai ni tashika na isseki wo naje ta hazu desu.
choices.arti: That humility is a reflection of your character, Professor. However, the points that struck at the core of the issues have surely made waves in the international community.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that humility reflects your character... the points striking at the core must have made an impact' because it appreciates the humility while acknowledging the real impact. Meanwhile, 'humble words, the presentation appears very successful' although positive, only validates in general terms without capturing the depth implied by 「ないまでも」.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: あの研究(けんきゅう)発表(はっぴょう)は、国際(こくさい)学会(がっかい)での議論(ぎろん)を活発(かっぱつ)にせずにはおかないほどの内容(ないよう)でしたね。
question.romaji: Ano kenkyuu happyou wa, kokusai gakkai de no giron wo kappatsu ni sezu ni wa okana i hodo no naiyou deshita ne.
question.arti: That research presentation had content that will undoubtedly invigorate discussions in international academic forums.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かに。しかし、それほどの反響(はんきょう)があったにもかかわらず、参加者(さんかしゃ)たちは早々(そうそう)に退席(たいせき)してしまいました。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni. Shikashi, sore hodo no hankyou ga atta ni mo kakawarazu, sankasha-tachi wa sousou ni taiseki shite shimaimashita.
choices.arti: Indeed. However, despite such a significant response, the participants quickly left the venue.

choices.jepang: まさにそうですね。閉会後(へいかいご)も廊下(ろうか)で議論(ぎろん)が続(つづ)いていたのは、その証左(しょうさ)と言(い)えるでしょう。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sou desu ne. Heikai go mo rouka de giron ga tsuzuite ita no wa, sono shousya to ieru deshou.
choices.arti: That is absolutely right. The discussions that continued in the hallway after the event are proof of that.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the discussions that continued in the hallway after the event are proof of that' because it provides concrete evidence that the presentation stimulated discussion, consistent with 「ずにはおかない」. Meanwhile, 'the participants quickly left the venue' contradicts the context -- if the presentation was that impressive, participants should have been eager to discuss.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、台風(たいふう)の影響(えいきょう)で、最終日(さいしゅうび)のシンポジウムは中止(ちゅうし)を余儀(よぎ)なくされてしまいましたね。
question.romaji: Sensei, taifuu no eikyou de, saishuu bi no shinpojiumu wa chuushi wo yogi naku sa rete shimaimashita ne.
question.arti: Professor, due to the typhoon, the symposium on the final day was forced to be cancelled, wasn't it.

choices.jepang: 残念(ざんねん)ながら、そうなりました。自然(しぜん)の猛威(もうい)の前(まえ)には、いかなる計画(けいかく)も無力(むりょく)であることを改(あらた)めて痛感(つうかん)しました。
choices.romaji: Zannen nagara, sou narimashita. Shizen no moui no mae ni wa, ikanaru keikaku mo muryoku de aru koto wo aratamete tsuukan shimashita.
choices.arti: Unfortunately, that is the case. I was once again reminded of how powerless all plans are in the face of nature's fury.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に遺憾(いかん)なことでした。ただ、オンラインでの継続(けいぞく)開催(かいさい)という代替(だいたい)案(あん)が迅速(じんそく)に実現(じつげん)し、討議(とうぎ)を滞(とどこお)りなく進(すす)めることができました。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni ikan na koto deshita. Tada, onrain de no keizoku kaisa i to iu daita ian ga jinsoku ni jitsugen shi, tougi wo todokoori naku susumeru koto ga dekimashita.
choices.arti: It was truly regrettable. However, the alternative of holding it online was realized swiftly, and the discussions proceeded smoothly.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the alternative of holding it online was realized swiftly, and the discussions proceeded smoothly' because it demonstrates a professional response with a concrete solution to the compulsion of 「を余儀なくされる」. Meanwhile, 'all plans are powerless in the face of nature' merely resigns to the situation without showing constructive steps.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: オンライン形式(けいしき)であれ対面(たいめん)であれ、研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)同士(どうし)が真剣(しんけん)に議論(ぎろん)を戦(たたか)わせる場(ば)は、学問(がくもん)の発展(はってん)にとって不可欠(ふかけつ)です。
question.romaji: Onrain keishiki de are taimen de are, kenkyuusha doushi ga shinken ni giron wo tatakawaseru ba wa, gakumon no hatten ni totte fukaketsu desu.
question.arti: Whether online or in-person, a venue where researchers engage in serious intellectual debate is absolutely essential for the advancement of science.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。今後(こんご)は全(すべ)ての学会(がっかい)をオンラインに移行(いこう)すべきだと思(おも)います。対面(たいめん)開催(かいさい)はもはや時代遅(じだいおく)れです。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Kongo wa subete no gakkai wo onrain ni ikou subeki da to omoimasu. Taimen kaisai wa mohaya jidaiokure desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Going forward, all conferences should shift to online. In-person events are outdated.

choices.jepang: 仰(おっしゃ)る通(とお)りです。ただ、対面(たいめん)ならではの即興(そっきょう)的(てき)な議論(ぎろん)の深(ふか)まりは、オンラインでは再現(さいげん)しがたい部分(ぶぶん)もございます。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Tada, taimen nara de wa no sokkyouteki na giron no fukamari wa, onrain de wa saigen shigatai bubun mo gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. However, the spontaneous deepening of discussions that can only occur in face-to-face meetings has aspects that are difficult to replicate online.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the spontaneous deepening of discussions that can only occur face-to-face has aspects difficult to replicate online' because it appropriately uses 「ならではの」 to highlight the uniqueness of in-person meetings. Meanwhile, 'all conferences should shift to online' is too extreme and disregards the value of direct interaction mentioned in the question.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の会議(かいぎ)で、ドイツの研究(けんきゅう)チームが提唱(ていしょう)した新(あたら)しい理論(りろん)的(てき)枠組(わくぐ)みは、従来(じゅうらい)の学説(がくせつ)を覆(くつがえ)さんばかりの衝撃(しょうげき)でしたね。
question.romaji: Konkai no kaigi de, Doitsu no kenkyuu chiimu ga teishou shita atarashii rironteki wakugumi wa, juurai no gakusetsu wo kutsugaesan bakari no shougeki deshita ne.
question.arti: At this conference, the new theoretical framework proposed by the German research team was astonishing, as if it would overturn the prevailing theories.

choices.jepang: あの発表(はっぴょう)は注目(ちゅうもく)に値(あたい)しますが、従来(じゅうらい)の学説(がくせつ)はすでに十分(じゅうぶん)に確立(かくりつ)されておりますので、新(あたら)しい理論(りろん)を受(う)け入(い)れる必要(ひつよう)はないかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Ano happyou wa chuumoku ni atai shimasu ga, juurai no gakusetsu wa sudeni juubun ni kakuritsu sarete orimasu no de, atarashii riron wo ukeireru hitsuyou wa nai ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: That presentation certainly deserves attention, but the prevailing theories are well-established, so I believe there is no need to accept the new theory.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かに画期的(かっきてき)でした。ただ、あの理論(りろん)が既存(きそん)の研究(けんきゅう)蓄積(ちくせき)と矛盾(むじゅん)する箇所(かしょ)については、さらなる実証(じっしょう)が求(もと)められるでしょう。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni kakkiteki deshita. Tada, ano riron ga kison no kenkyuu chikuseki to mujun suru kasho ni tsuite wa, saranaru jisshou ga motomerareru deshou.
choices.arti: It is indeed revolutionary. However, for the parts where the theory contradicts the existing body of research, further empirical verification is required.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'it is indeed revolutionary, but further empirical verification is required for the contradicting parts' because it demonstrates an open yet critical academic attitude. Meanwhile, 'there is no need to accept the new theory' shows a closed-minded attitude contradicting scientific progress -- new theories must be evaluated based on evidence, not rejected outright.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: この分野(ぶんや)をおいて他(ほか)に、人類(じんるい)の未来(みらい)を左右(さゆう)する研究(けんきゅう)領域(りょういき)はないと言(い)っても過言(かごん)ではありません。
question.romaji: Kono bunya wo oite hoka ni, jinrui no mirai wo sayuu suru kenkyuu ryouiki wa nai to itte mo kagonde wa arimasen.
question.arti: It is no exaggeration to say that there is no other research field that determines the future of humanity as much as this one.

choices.jepang: 先生(せんせい)のそのお言葉(ことば)に、若手(わかて)研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)として身(み)の引(ひ)き締(し)まる思(おも)いでございます。この分野(ぶんや)に携(たずさ)われることを誇(ほこ)りに存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Sensei no sono okotoba ni, wakate kenkyuusha to shite mi no hikishimaru omoi de gozaimasu. Kono bunya ni tazusawareru koto wo hokori ni zonjimasu.
choices.arti: With those words, Professor, as a young researcher I feel inspired and motivated. I am proud to be involved in this field.

choices.jepang: いささか大袈裟(おおげさ)ではないでしょうか。他(ほか)の分野(ぶんや)にも同等(どうとう)の重要性(じゅうようせい)があると思(おも)われますが。
choices.romaji: Isasaka oogesa de wa nai deshou ka. Hoka no bunya ni mo doutou no juuyousei ga aru to omowaremasu ga.
choices.arti: Isn't that somewhat of an exaggeration? I believe other fields hold equal importance.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'as a young researcher I feel motivated and proud to be involved in this field' because it responds with appropriate respect and enthusiasm to the supervisor's statement using 「をおいて他に〜ない」. Meanwhile, 'isn't that somewhat of an exaggeration' is contextually inappropriate as it directly questions a senior without supporting arguments.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 会議(かいぎ)の成果(せいか)を論文(ろんぶん)として発表(はっぴょう)するにせよ、報告書(ほうこくしょ)にまとめるにせよ、何(なん)らかの形(かたち)で記録(きろく)に残(のこ)す必要(ひつよう)がありますね。
question.romaji: Kaigi no seika wo ronbun to shite happyou suru ni seyo, houkokusho ni matomeru ni seyo, nanraka no katachi de kiroku ni nokosu hitsuyou ga arimasu ne.
question.arti: Whether we publish the conference results as a paper or compile them as a report, we need to document them in some form.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の通(とお)りです。国際(こくさい)学術誌(がくじゅつし)への投稿(とうこう)を視野(しや)に入(い)れつつ、まずは共同(きょうどう)研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)との議論(ぎろん)を経(へ)て方針(ほうしん)を固(かた)めましょう。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no toori desu. Kokusai gakujutsushi he no toukou wo shiya ni iretsu tsu, mazuwa kyoudou kenkyuusha to no giron wo hete houshin wo katamemashou.
choices.arti: Absolutely. While considering submission to an international academic journal, let us establish our direction after discussing with our collaborating researchers first.

choices.jepang: 論文(ろんぶん)の執筆(しっぴつ)は時間(じかん)がかかりますから、口頭(こうとう)発表(はっぴょう)だけで十分(じゅうぶん)ではないでしょうか。記録(きろく)に残(のこ)す必要性(ひつようせい)は低(ひく)いと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Ronbun no shippitsu wa jikan ga kakarimasu kara, koutou happyou dake de juubun de wa nai deshou ka. Kiroku ni nokosu hitsuyousei wa hikui to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Writing a paper takes time, so wouldn't an oral presentation suffice? I believe the need for documentation is low.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'let us establish our direction after discussing with collaborating researchers' because it demonstrates a well-considered academic plan with concrete steps. Meanwhile, 'an oral presentation is sufficient, documentation need is low' contradicts scientific research principles -- written publication is essential for academic contributions to be verified and referenced.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 若手(わかて)研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)たちが堂々(どうどう)と発表(はっぴょう)する姿(すがた)を見(み)て、感動(かんどう)を禁(きん)じ得(え)ませんでした。彼(かれ)らの成長(せいちょう)は目覚(めざ)ましいですね。
question.romaji: Wakate kenkyuusha-tachi ga doudou to happyou suru sugata wo mite, kandou wo kinji emasendeshita. Karera no seichou wa mezamashii desu ne.
question.arti: Watching the young researchers present so courageously, I could not contain my deep emotion. Their growth is truly remarkable.

choices.jepang: 若手(わかて)の成長(せいちょう)は確(たし)かですが、彼(かれ)らの発表(はっぴょう)にはまだまだ改善(かいぜん)の余地(よち)が多(おお)く、手放(てばな)しで褒(ほ)めるべきではないと考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Wakate no seichou wa tashika desu ga, karera no happyou ni wa madamada kaizen no yochi ga ooku, tebanashi de homeru beki de wa nai to kangaemasu.
choices.arti: The growth of the young researchers is certain, but their presentations still have much room for improvement, so they should not be praised without reservation.

choices.jepang: 先生(せんせい)のご指導(しどう)の賜物(たまもの)です。先生(せんせい)が築(きず)かれた学術(がくじゅつ)的(てき)基盤(きばん)なくしては、彼(かれ)らの飛躍(ひやく)もあり得(え)なかったことでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sensei no go shidou no tamamono desu. Sensei ga kizukareta gakujutsuteki kiban naku shite wa, karera no hiyaku mo arienakatta koto deshou.
choices.arti: That is thanks to your guidance, Professor. Without the academic foundation you built, their remarkable progress would never have been possible.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that is thanks to your guidance; without the academic foundation you built, their progress would never have been possible' because it responds with sincere gratitude. The pattern 「感動を禁じ得ませんでした」 means 'could not contain one's deep emotion.' Meanwhile, 'they should not be praised without reservation' is overly critical and fails to appreciate the emotional moment.

conversation.speaker: 鈴木(すずき)助教(じょきょう)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、来年度(らいねんど)の会議(かいぎ)は東京(とうきょう)開催(かいさい)と伺(うかが)いましたが、主催(しゅさい)側(がわ)として万全(ばんぜん)の態勢(たいせい)を整(ととの)えるべく、早(はや)めに準備(じゅんび)に取(と)り掛(か)かりましょう。
question.romaji: Sensei, rainendo no kaigi wa Toukyou kaisai to ukagaimashita ga, shusaigawa to shite banzen no taisei wo totonoeru beku, hayame ni junbi ni torikakarimashou.
question.arti: Professor, I hear next year's conference will be held in Tokyo. Let us begin preparations promptly to be fully ready as the hosting party.

choices.jepang: ええ、東京(とうきょう)開催(かいさい)ともなると、日本(にほん)の学術(がくじゅつ)水準(すいじゅん)が問(と)われることになります。世界(せかい)に恥(は)じぬ運営(うんえい)を目指(めざ)して、全力(ぜんりょく)を尽(つ)くしましょう。
choices.romaji: Ee, Toukyou kaisai to mo naru to, Nihon no gakujutsu suijun ga towareru koto ni narimasu. Sekai ni hajinu un'ei wo mezashite, zenryoku wo tsukushimashou.
choices.arti: Yes, when it comes to hosting in Tokyo, Japan's academic standards will be put to the test. Let us devote all our efforts to management that will not bring shame on the world stage.

choices.jepang: 東京(とうきょう)開催(かいさい)は名誉(めいよ)なことですが、準備(じゅんび)は委員会(いいんかい)に任(まか)せておけば問題(もんだい)ないでしょう。我々(われわれ)は研究(けんきゅう)に専念(せんねん)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Toukyou kaisai wa meiyo na koto desu ga, junbi wa iinkai ni makasete okeba mondai nai deshou. Wareware wa kenkyuu ni sennen subeki desu.
choices.arti: Hosting in Tokyo is certainly an honor, but leaving preparations to the committee alone should be fine. We should focus on research.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'when it comes to hosting in Tokyo, Japan's academic standards will be tested; let us devote all efforts' because it shows a high sense of responsibility as the host, matching 「ともなると」. Meanwhile, 'leave preparations to the committee' shows an irresponsible attitude -- as host academics, active involvement is a moral obligation.

conversation.speaker: 田中(たなか)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の経験(けいけん)を通(つう)じて、国際(こくさい)学術(がくじゅつ)交流(こうりゅう)の重要性(じゅうようせい)を改(あらた)めて痛感(つうかん)しました。学問(がくもん)の発展(はってん)を願(ねが)ってやみません。
question.romaji: Konkai no keiken wo tsuujite, kokusai gakujutsu kouryuu no juuyousei wo aratamete tsuukan shimashita. Gakumon no hatten wo negatte yamimasen.
question.arti: Through this experience, I have once again realized the importance of international academic exchange. I ceaselessly hope for the advancement of science.

choices.jepang: 先生(せんせい)のその熱意(ねつい)こそが、次世代(じせだい)の研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)を育(そだ)てる原動力(げんどうりょく)です。私(わたくし)もその志(こころざし)を受(う)け継(つ)いでまいります。
choices.romaji: Sensei no sono netsui koso ga, jisedai no kenkyuusha wo sodateru gendouryoku desu. Watakushi mo sono kokorozashi wo uketsui de mairimasu.
choices.arti: Your enthusiasm, Professor, is the driving force for nurturing the next generation of researchers. I too shall carry on that resolve.

choices.jepang: 国際(こくさい)交流(こうりゅう)は大切(たいせつ)ですが、国内(こくない)の研究(けんきゅう)基盤(きばん)を固(かた)めることのほうが先決(せんけつ)だと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Kokusai kouryuu wa taisetsu desu ga, kokunai no kenkyuu kiban wo katameru koto no hou ga senketsu da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: International exchange is important, but I believe strengthening the domestic research foundation should take priority.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'your enthusiasm is the driving force for nurturing the next generation; I too shall carry on that resolve' because it responds to 「てやまない」 (ceaselessly hope) with a commitment to inheriting the same spirit. Meanwhile, 'strengthening the domestic foundation should take priority' disregards the emotional and reflective context of the professor expressing his deepest hopes.

conversation.speaker: 弁護士(べんごし)・伊藤(いとう)

question.jepang: 裁判(さいばん)ともなると、一言(ひとこと)一句(いっく)が判決(はんけつ)を左右(さゆう)しかねませんので、慎重(しんちょう)に言葉(ことば)を選(えら)んでください。
question.romaji: Saiban to mo naru to, hitokoto ikku ga hanketsu wo sayuu shi kane masen no de, shinchou ni kotoba wo erande kudasai.
question.arti: When it comes to a trial, every single word can influence the verdict, so please choose your words carefully.

choices.jepang: 承知(しょうち)しました。証言(しょうげん)に際(さい)しては、事実(じじつ)のみを簡潔(かんけつ)に述(の)べるよう努(つと)めます。
choices.romaji: Shouchi shimashita. Shougen ni saishite wa, jijitsu nomi wo kanketsu ni noberu you tsutomemasu.
choices.arti: Understood. When giving testimony, I will endeavor to state only the facts concisely.

choices.jepang: わかりました。裁判(さいばん)の場(ば)では自由(じゆう)に感情(かんじょう)を表現(ひょうげん)することが大切(たいせつ)だと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Wakarimashita. Saiban no ba de wa jiyuu ni kanjou wo hyougen suru koto ga taisetsu da to omoimasu.
choices.arti: I understand. I believe freely expressing emotions in court is important.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'when giving testimony, I will endeavor to state only the facts concisely' because it shows awareness of the gravity of a trial, matching 「ともなると」. Meanwhile, 'freely expressing emotions in court is important' contradicts principles of sound testimony -- in court, facts take precedence over emotions.

conversation.speaker: 検察官(けんさつかん)・山田(やまだ)

question.jepang: 被告(ひこく)は、周囲(しゅうい)の批判(ひはん)をものともせず、最後(さいご)まで無実(むじつ)を主張(しゅちょう)し続(つづ)けました。
question.romaji: Hikoku wa, shuui no hihan wo mono to mo sezu, saigo made mujitsu wo shuchou shi tsuzukemashita.
question.arti: The defendant, undeterred by criticism from those around him, continued asserting his innocence until the very end.

choices.jepang: しかしながら、その一貫(いっかん)した主張(しゅちょう)が、後(のち)に新(あたら)たな証拠(しょうこ)と結(むす)びつき、無罪(むざい)判決(はんけつ)を導(みちび)く一因(いちいん)となりました。
choices.romaji: Shikashi nagara, sono ikkan shita shuchou ga, nochi ni aratana shouko to musubitsuki, muz ai hanketsu wo michibiku ichiin to narimashita.
choices.arti: Nevertheless, that consistent claim later connected with new evidence and became one of the factors leading to a not-guilty verdict.

choices.jepang: その頑固(がんこ)な姿勢(しせい)こそが、かえって陪審員(ばいしんいん)の心証(しんしょう)を悪化(あっか)させた可能性(かのうせい)がございます。
choices.romaji: Sono ganko na shisei koso ga, kaette baishin'in no shinshoo wo akka saseta kanousei ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: That stubborn attitude is precisely what may have worsened the impression on the jury.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that consistent claim later connected with new evidence and led to a not-guilty verdict' because it validates that the defendant's steadfastness ultimately proved meaningful, matching the positive spirit of 「をものともせず」. Meanwhile, 'that stubborn attitude worsened the jury's impression' frames steadfastness as negative, obscuring the courage contained in the expression.

conversation.speaker: 弁護士(べんごし)・伊藤(いとう)

question.jepang: 証人(しょうにん)は、泣(な)かんばかりの表情(ひょうじょう)で、あの夜(よる)の出来事(できごと)を詳細(しょうさい)に語(かた)りました。
question.romaji: Shounin wa, nakan bakari no hyoujou de, ano yoru no dekigoto wo shousai ni katarimashita.
question.arti: The witness, with an expression as if about to cry, recounted the events of that night in detail.

choices.jepang: その真(しん)に迫(せま)った証言(しょうげん)は、法廷(ほうてい)にいた全員(ぜんいん)の心(こころ)を動(うご)かしたに違(ちが)いありません。
choices.romaji: Sono shin ni sematta shougen wa, houtei ni ita zen'in no kokoro wo ugokashita ni chigai arimasen.
choices.arti: That testimony, so close to the truth, must have moved the hearts of everyone present in the courtroom.

choices.jepang: 証人(しょうにん)が感情的(かんじょうてき)になりすぎたため、その証言(しょうげん)の信憑性(しんぴょうせい)は低(ひく)いと判断(はんだん)されました。
choices.romaji: Shounin ga kanjouteki ni nari sugita tame, sono shougen no shinpyousei wa hikui to handan saremashita.
choices.arti: Because the witness was too emotional, the credibility of the testimony was deemed low.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the testimony so close to the truth must have moved everyone in the courtroom' because it captures the strong emotional impact from 「んばかりに」. Meanwhile, 'the witness was too emotional so credibility was low' reverses the meaning -- an expression full of feeling gives an authentic impression, not a weakened credibility.

conversation.speaker: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)・松本(まつもと)

question.jepang: 完全(かんぜん)な証明(しょうめい)とはいかないまでも、状況(じょうきょう)証拠(しょうこ)の積(つ)み重(かさ)ねが、有罪(ゆうざい)の心証(しんしょう)を形成(けいせい)するに足(た)りると判断(はんだん)いたします。
question.romaji: Kanzen na shoumei to wa ikanai made mo, joukyou shouko no tsumikasane ga, yuuzai no shinshoo wo keisei suru ni tariru to handan itashimasu.
question.arti: Although it may not amount to perfect proof, I rule that the accumulation of circumstantial evidence is sufficient to form a conviction of guilt.

choices.jepang: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)のご判断(はんだん)に異議(いぎ)を申(もう)し立(た)てます。状況(じょうきょう)証拠(しょうこ)のみでは、合理的(ごうりてき)な疑(うたが)いを排除(はいじょ)するに至(いた)らないと考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Saibancho no go handan ni igi wo moushitate masu. Joukyou shouko nomi de wa, gouriteki na utagai wo haijo suru ni itaranai to kangaemasu.
choices.arti: I raise an objection to Your Honor's judgment. I contend that circumstantial evidence alone is insufficient to eliminate reasonable doubt.

choices.jepang: 弁護(べんご)側(がわ)といたしましては、その判断(はんだん)に対(たい)し直(ただ)ちに不服申(ふふくもう)し立(た)てを行(おこな)い、上訴(じょうそ)する権利(けんり)を行使(こうし)いたします。
choices.romaji: Bengo gawa to itashimashite wa, sono handan ni tai shi tadachi ni fufuku moushitate wo okonai, jousso suru kenri wo koushi itashimasu.
choices.arti: On behalf of the defense, we will immediately file an appeal and exercise our right to appeal to a higher court.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'we will immediately file an appeal and exercise our right to appeal' because it responds with procedurally appropriate action -- using the correct legal channels after a verdict is rendered. Meanwhile, 'I raise an objection, circumstantial evidence alone is insufficient' although sounding legal, directly objecting as the judge delivers a verdict is procedurally less appropriate.

conversation.speaker: 検察官(けんさつかん)・山田(やまだ)

question.jepang: この判決(はんけつ)は、社会(しゃかい)全体(ぜんたい)に警鐘(けいしょう)を鳴(な)らさずにはおかないものと確信(かくしん)しております。
question.romaji: Kono hanketsu wa, shakai zentai ni keishou wo narasa zu ni wa okana i mono to kakushin shite orimasu.
question.arti: I am convinced that this verdict will undoubtedly sound an alarm bell for all of society.

choices.jepang: ご見解(けんかい)はわかりますが、一(いち)判決(はんけつ)が社会(しゃかい)に与(あた)える影響(えいきょう)を過大評価(かだいひょうか)すべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Go kenkai wa wakarimasu ga, ichi hanketsu ga shakai ni ataeru eikyou wo kadai hyouka sube ki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: I understand your view, but shouldn't we refrain from overestimating the impact of a single verdict on society?

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。この判決(はんけつ)を契機(けいき)に、関連(かんれん)する法律(ほうりつ)の見直(みなお)しが進(すす)むことを期待(きたい)しております。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Kono hanketsu wo keiki ni, kanren suru houritsu no minaoshi ga susumu koto wo kitai shite orimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. I hope that with this verdict as a catalyst, the review of related laws will advance.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'shouldn't we refrain from overestimating the impact of a single verdict' because it provides a critical yet balanced perspective in professional debate. Meanwhile, 'I hope the review of related laws will advance' merely agrees without contributing substantive legal discussion.

conversation.speaker: 弁護士(べんごし)・伊藤(いとう)

question.jepang: 依頼人(いらいにん)は、新(あたら)たな証拠(しょうこ)が見(み)つかるまで、拘留(こうりゅう)の継続(けいぞく)を余儀(よぎ)なくされております。
question.romaji: Irainin wa, aratana shouko ga mitsukaru made, kouryu no keizoku wo yogi naku sarete orimasu.
question.arti: My client is forced to remain in detention until new evidence is found.

choices.jepang: その状況(じょうきょう)は誠(まこと)に遺憾(いかん)です。証拠(しょうこ)収集(しゅうしゅう)のために、私(わたくし)どもも全力(ぜんりょく)を尽(つ)くす所存(しょぞん)でございます。
choices.romaji: Sono joukyou wa makoto ni ikan desu. Shouko shuushuu no tame ni, watakushi domo mo zenryoku wo tsukusu shozonde gozaimasu.
choices.arti: That situation is truly regrettable. We too are determined to devote all our capabilities to evidence gathering.

choices.jepang: 依頼人(いらいにん)が拘留(こうりゅう)されているのは、本人(ほんにん)の不注意(ふちゅうい)によるものですから、致(いた)し方(かた)ないことでしょう。
choices.romaji: Irainin ga kouryu sarete iru no wa, honnin no fuchuui ni yoru mono desu kara, itashikata nai koto deshou.
choices.arti: The client is detained due to his own carelessness, so it simply cannot be helped.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that situation is truly regrettable; we are determined to devote all capabilities to evidence gathering' because it demonstrates empathy and professional commitment. Meanwhile, 'the client is detained due to his own carelessness' is highly inappropriate -- a lawyer would never blame their own client before other parties, as this violates professional legal ethics.

conversation.speaker: 検察官(けんさつかん)・山田(やまだ)

question.jepang: 裁判官(さいばんかん)であれ検察官(けんさつかん)であれ、法(ほう)の番人(ばんにん)たるもの、私情(しじょう)を排(はい)して公正(こうせい)に職務(しょくむ)を遂行(すいこう)しなければなりません。
question.romaji: Saibankan de are kensatsukan de are, hou no bannin taru mono, shijou wo hai shite kousei ni shokumu wo suikou shinakereba narimasen.
question.arti: Whether judge or prosecutor, as guardians of the law, one must carry out duties impartially by setting aside personal feelings.

choices.jepang: 全(まった)く同感(どうかん)です。ただ、公正(こうせい)さを追求(ついきゅう)するあまり、被害者(ひがいしゃ)の苦痛(くつう)に対(たい)する共感(きょうかん)まで失(うしな)ってはならないと考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Mattaku doukan desu. Tada, kousei sa wo tsuikyuu suru amari, higaisha no kutsuu ni taisuru kyoukan made ushinatte wa naranai to kangaemasu.
choices.arti: I fully agree. However, I believe we must not lose empathy for the suffering of victims in the excessive pursuit of justice.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)はもっともですが、現実(げんじつ)の裁判(さいばん)においては、私情(しじょう)を完全(かんぜん)に排除(はいじょ)することは不可能(ふかのう)です。むしろ感情(かんじょう)を活用(かつよう)すべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki wa mottomo desu ga, genjitsu no saiban ni oite wa, shijou wo kanzen ni haijo suru koto wa fukanou desu. Mushiro kanjou wo katsuyou subeki deshou.
choices.arti: Your point is valid, but in actual trials, completely eliminating personal feelings is impossible. Rather, we should utilize emotions.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'we must not lose empathy for victims' suffering in the excessive pursuit of justice' because it adds an important nuance -- justice and empathy must go hand in hand. Meanwhile, 'we should utilize emotions' contradicts the principle in 「であれ」 which emphasizes that all guardians of the law must be impartial.

conversation.speaker: 弁護士(べんごし)・伊藤(いとう)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の事件(じけん)は、法律(ほうりつ)の専門家(せんもんか)たりとも容易(ようい)に解釈(かいしゃく)できない複雑(ふくざつ)な法的(ほうてき)論点(ろんてん)を含(ふく)んでおります。
question.romaji: Konkai no jiken wa, houritsu no senmonka tari to mo youi ni kaishaku dekinai fukuzatsu na houteki ronten wo fukunde orimasu.
question.arti: This case contains legal points so complex that even legal experts cannot easily interpret them.

choices.jepang: 複雑(ふくざつ)な論点(ろんてん)は避(さ)けて、より単純(たんじゅん)な法的(ほうてき)根拠(こんきょ)に基(もと)づいて弁論(べんろん)を展開(てんかい)すべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Fukuzatsu na ronten wa sakete, yori tanjun na houteki konkyo ni motozuite benron wo tenkai subeki deshou.
choices.arti: It would be better to avoid the complex points and develop the defense based on simpler legal grounds.

choices.jepang: だからこそ、判例(はんれい)研究(けんきゅう)を徹底(てってい)し、先例(せんれい)に即(そく)した論理(ろんり)構成(こうせい)を組(く)み立(た)てる必要(ひつよう)がございます。
choices.romaji: Dakara koso, hanrei kenkyuu wo tettei shi, senrei ni sokushita ronri kousei wo kumitateru hitsuyou ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Precisely because of that, it is necessary to conduct thorough jurisprudence studies and construct logical arguments based on precedent.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'it is necessary to conduct thorough jurisprudence studies and construct logical arguments based on precedent' because it shows high legal professionalism in facing complexity. Meanwhile, 'avoid the complex points' shows an unprofessional evasive attitude -- lawyers must confront complexity, not avoid it.

conversation.speaker: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)・松本(まつもと)

question.jepang: 法治国家(ほうちこっか)において、冤罪(えんざい)は許(ゆる)すまじき不正義(ふせいぎ)です。司法(しほう)制度(せいど)の信頼(しんらい)を守(まも)るため、慎重(しんちょう)な審理(しんり)を心(こころ)がけてまいります。
question.romaji: Houchi kokka ni oite, enzai wa yurusu majiki fuseigi desu. Shihou seido no shinrai wo mamoru tame, shinchou na shinri wo kokorogakete mairimasu.
question.arti: In a state governed by law, a wrongful conviction is an injustice that must never be tolerated. To maintain trust in the judicial system, I will strive for a thorough examination process.

choices.jepang: 冤罪(えんざい)の防止(ぼうし)は確(たし)かに重要(じゅうよう)ですが、それに固執(こしつ)するあまり、有罪者(ゆうざいしゃ)を逃(に)がしてしまうことも同様(どうよう)に問題(もんだい)です。
choices.romaji: Enzai no boushi wa tashika ni juuyou desu ga, sore ni koshitsu suru amari, yuuzaisha wo nigashite shimau koto mo douyou ni mondai desu.
choices.arti: Preventing wrongful convictions is important, but being too fixated on that to the point of acquitting the guilty is equally problematic.

choices.jepang: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)のその姿勢(しせい)を弁護(べんご)側(がわ)として高(たか)く評価(ひょうか)いたします。被告(ひこく)の権利(けんり)保護(ほご)にも十全(じゅうぜん)のご配慮(はいりょ)をお願(ねが)い申(もう)し上(あ)げます。
choices.romaji: Saibanchou no sono shisei wo bengo gawa to shite takaku hyouka itashimasu. Hikoku no kenri hogo ni mo juuzen no go hairyo wo onegai moushi agemasu.
choices.arti: On behalf of the defense, we deeply appreciate that stance. We also request full attention to the protection of the defendant's rights.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'we deeply appreciate that stance; we also request attention to protecting the defendant's rights' because it appropriately responds to the judge's use of 「まじき」 (must never be tolerated) and reinforces rights protection. Meanwhile, 'being too fixated on preventing wrongful convictions to acquit the guilty' undermines the important principle just established by the judge.

conversation.speaker: 検察官(けんさつかん)・山田(やまだ)

question.jepang: 本件(ほんけん)の被害者(ひがいしゃ)遺族(いぞく)は、真相(しんそう)の解明(かいめい)を願(ねが)ってやまないとのことです。我々(われわれ)はその思(おも)いに応(こた)える義務(ぎむ)があります。
question.romaji: Honken no higaisha izoku wa, shinsou no kaimei wo negatte yamanai to no koto desu. Wareware wa sono omoi ni kotaeru gimu ga arimasu.
question.arti: The victim's family ceaselessly hopes that the truth will be revealed. We have an obligation to answer that hope.

choices.jepang: 遺族(いぞく)の気持(きも)ちは理解(りかい)できますが、感情(かんじょう)に流(なが)されてはなりません。司法(しほう)は遺族(いぞく)のためだけにあるわけではございません。
choices.romaji: Izoku no kimochi wa rikai dekimasu ga, kanjou ni nagasarete wa narimasen. Shihou wa izoku no tame dake ni aru wake de wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: The family's feelings are understandable, but we must not be swayed by emotions. The judiciary exists not only for victims' families.

choices.jepang: その通(とお)りです。ただし、真相(しんそう)解明(かいめい)への熱意(ねつい)が、証拠(しょうこ)の恣意的(しいてき)な解釈(かいしゃく)につながることのないよう、細心(さいしん)の注意(ちゅうい)が必要(ひつよう)です。
choices.romaji: Sono toori desu. Tadashi, shinsou kaimei he no netsui ga, shouko no shiiteki na kaishaku ni tsunagaru koto no nai you, saishin no chuui ga hitsuyou desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. However, extreme care is required to ensure that the zeal for uncovering the truth does not lead to arbitrary interpretation of evidence.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'extreme care is required so that the zeal for truth does not lead to arbitrary interpretation of evidence' because it maintains a balance between empathy and legal objectivity. Meanwhile, 'the judiciary exists not only for victims' families' although principally correct, lacks sensitivity to the context where the family ceaselessly hopes for truth.

conversation.speaker: 弁護士(べんごし)・伊藤(いとう)

question.jepang: 控訴審(こうそしん)においては、新(あたら)たな鑑定(かんてい)結果(けっか)かたがた、事実(じじつ)認定(にんてい)の誤(あやま)りを指摘(してき)する方針(ほうしん)でおります。
question.romaji: Kousoshin ni oite wa, aratana kantei kekka katagata, jijitsu nintei no ayamari wo shiteki suru houshin de orimasu.
question.arti: In the appeals trial, we plan to demonstrate errors in fact-finding while also presenting new expert evaluations.

choices.jepang: 新(あたら)たな鑑定(かんてい)結果(けっか)は、一審(いっしん)の判断(はんだん)を覆(くつがえ)す決定的(けっていてき)な要素(ようそ)となり得(う)るのでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Aratana kantei kekka wa, isshin no handan wo kutsugaesu ketteiteki na youso to nari uru no deshou ka.
choices.arti: Could the new expert evaluation become a decisive element for overturning the first instance verdict?

choices.jepang: 控訴審(こうそしん)での逆転(ぎゃくてん)は極(きわ)めて稀(まれ)ですから、早(はや)めに和解(わかい)の道(みち)を探(さぐ)るべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Kousoshin de no gyakuten wa kiwamete mare desu kara, hayame ni wakai no michi wo saguru beki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Since reversals at the appellate level are extremely rare, wouldn't it be better to seek mediation immediately?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'could the new expert evaluation become a decisive element for overturning the verdict' because it shows professional interest in the proposed legal strategy. Meanwhile, 'it would be better to seek mediation immediately' gives up before trying, contradicting a lawyer's duty to advocate for their client.

conversation.speaker: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)・松本(まつもと)

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)の審理(しんり)をもちまして結審(けっしん)とし、判決(はんけつ)は来月(らいげつ)言(い)い渡(わた)すこととします。双方(そうほう)とも法廷(ほうてい)の秩序(ちつじょ)を守(まも)り、品位(ひんい)ある態度(たいど)を最後(さいご)まで維持(いじ)してください。
question.romaji: Honjitsu no shinri wo mochimashite kesshin to shi, hanketsu wa raigetsu iiwatasu koto to shimasu. Souhou tomo houtei no chitsujo wo mamori, hini aru taido wo saigo made iji shite kudasai.
question.arti: With today's examination, this case is declared concluded, and the verdict will be read next month. Both parties are requested to maintain court order and preserve a dignified attitude until the end.

choices.jepang: 畏(かしこ)まりました。弁護(べんご)側(がわ)としましては、公正(こうせい)なる判決(はんけつ)が下(くだ)されることを切(せつ)に願(ねが)っております。
choices.romaji: Kashikomarimashita. Bengo gawa to shimashite wa, kousei naru hanketsu ga kudasareru koto wo setsu ni negatte orimasu.
choices.arti: Very well, Your Honor. The defense deeply hopes that a just verdict will be rendered.

choices.jepang: 裁判長(さいばんちょう)、判決(はんけつ)を来月(らいげつ)まで待(ま)つのは長(なが)すぎます。被告(ひこく)の精神的(せいしんてき)負担(ふたん)を考慮(こうりょ)して、もっと早(はや)く結論(けつろん)を出(だ)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Saibanchou, hanketsu wo raigetsu made matsu no wa nagasugimasu. Hikoku no seishinteki futan wo kouryo shite, motto hayaku ketsuron wo dasu beki desu.
choices.arti: Your Honor, waiting until next month for the verdict is too long. Considering the mental burden on the defendant, the conclusion should be reached sooner.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'we deeply hope that a just verdict will be rendered' because it responds respectfully and professionally to the judge's decision. Meanwhile, 'the verdict should be reached sooner' shows disrespect for the court's authority in determining the schedule -- the judge needs sufficient time to carefully deliberate the verdict.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 博士(はくし)論文(ろんぶん)ともなると、先行(せんこう)研究(けんきゅう)との差異(さい)を明確(めいかく)に示(しめ)すことが不可欠(ふかけつ)です。
question.romaji: Hakushi ronbun to mo naru to, senkou kenkyuu to no sai wo meikaku ni shimesu koto ga fukaketsu desu.
question.arti: When it comes to a doctoral dissertation, demonstrating a clear distinction from prior research is absolutely essential.

choices.jepang: 肝(きも)に銘(めい)じます。第(だい)二章(にしょう)において、先行(せんこう)研究(けんきゅう)との相違点(そういてん)をより詳細(しょうさい)に論(ろん)じるよう改稿(かいこう)いたします。
choices.romaji: Kimo ni meijimasu. Dai ni shou ni oite, senkou kenkyuu to no soui ten wo yori shousai ni ronjiru you kaikou itashimasu.
choices.arti: I will take that to heart. I will revise Chapter Two to discuss the distinctions from prior research in greater detail.

choices.jepang: 承知(しょうち)しました。ただ、先行(せんこう)研究(けんきゅう)との差異(さい)は序論(じょろん)に一文(いちぶん)程度(ていど)記載(きさい)すれば十分(じゅうぶん)かと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Shouchi shimashita. Tada, senkou kenkyuu to no sai wa joron ni ichifun teido kisai sureba juubun ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: I understand. However, I believe a single sentence in the introduction is sufficient to note the distinctions from prior research.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'I will revise Chapter Two to discuss distinctions from prior research in greater detail' because it demonstrates understanding of the high standards of a doctoral dissertation, matching 「ともなると」. Meanwhile, 'a single sentence in the introduction is sufficient' trivializes the supervisor's advice -- a single sentence is clearly insufficient for a dissertation requiring in-depth differentiation from the literature.

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、私(わたくし)は指導委員会(しどういいんかい)の厳(きび)しい批判(ひはん)をものともせず、三(さん)年間(ねんかん)この研究(けんきゅう)を続(つづ)けてまいりました。
question.romaji: Sensei, watakushi wa shidou iinkai no kibishii hihan wo mono to mo sezu, san nen kan kono kenkyuu wo tsuzukete mairimashita.
question.arti: Professor, I have continued this research for three years without being deterred by harsh criticism from the supervising committee.

choices.jepang: その不屈(ふくつ)の精神(せいしん)こそが、独創的(どくそうてき)な研究(けんきゅう)を成(な)し遂(と)げる原動力(げんどうりょく)となるのです。その姿勢(しせい)を貫(つらぬ)いてください。
choices.romaji: Sono fukutsu no seishin koso ga, dokusouteki na kenkyuu wo nashitogeru gendouryoku to naru no desu. Sono shisei wo tsuranuite kudasai.
choices.arti: That unwavering spirit is the driving force for completing original research. Maintain that attitude.

choices.jepang: その粘(ねば)り強(づよ)さは称賛(しょうさん)に値(あたい)します。しかし、批判(ひはん)を真摯(しんし)に受(う)け止(と)め、論(ろん)文(ぶん)の質(しつ)を高(たか)めることも学術(がくじゅつ)的(てき)成長(せいちょう)には欠(か)かせません。
choices.romaji: Sono nebari zuyosa wa shousan ni atai shimasu. Shikashi, hihan wo shinshi ni uketo me, ronbun no shitsu wo takameru koto mo gakujutsuteki seichou ni wa kakasemasen.
choices.arti: That perseverance is commendable. However, earnestly accepting criticism and improving the thesis quality is also inseparable from academic growth.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that unwavering spirit is the driving force for completing original research; maintain that attitude' because the supervisor appreciates the student's steadfastness, matching the positive spirit of 「をものともせず」. Meanwhile, 'accepting criticism is also important' although sounding wise, its nuance seems to question the student's steadfastness, contradicting the context.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 審査委員(しんさいいん)たちは、その斬新(ざんしん)な仮説(かせつ)を今(いま)すぐにでも検証(けんしょう)せんばかりの熱意(ねつい)を見(み)せておりました。
question.romaji: Shinsa iin-tachi wa, sono zansin na kasetsu wo ima sugu ni demo kenshou sen bakari no netsui wo misete orimashita.
question.arti: The examiners showed enthusiasm as if they were about to immediately verify the new hypothesis.

choices.jepang: それは励(はげ)みになります。その熱意(ねつい)に応(こた)えられるよう、実験(じっけん)データの補強(ほきょう)に取(と)り組(く)んでまいります。
choices.romaji: Sore wa hagemi ni narimasu. Sono netsui ni kotaerareru you, jikken deeta no hokyou ni tori kumde mairimasu.
choices.arti: That gives me motivation. To meet that enthusiasm, I will strive to strengthen the experimental data.

choices.jepang: 審査委員(しんさいいん)の熱意(ねつい)は理解(りかい)できますが、仮説(かせつ)の検証(けんしょう)は私(わたくし)の管轄外(かんかつがい)ですので、関与(かんよ)する必要(ひつよう)はないと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Shinsa iin no netsui wa rikaI dekimasu ga, kasetsu no kenshou wa watakushi no kankatsu gai desu no de, kanyo suru hitsuyou wa nai to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I understand the examiners' enthusiasm, but verifying hypotheses is beyond my authority, so I believe there is no need to get involved.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that gives me motivation; I will strive to strengthen the experimental data' because it responds to the examiners' enthusiasm with a constructive action plan. Meanwhile, 'verifying hypotheses is beyond my authority' is highly inappropriate -- hypothesis verification is the core of a dissertation, and a doctoral student cannot abdicate that responsibility.

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 理想(りそう)的(てき)な実験(じっけん)環境(かんきょう)とはいかないまでも、現有(げんゆう)の設備(せつび)を最大限(さいだいげん)に活用(かつよう)して結果(けっか)を出(だ)そうと努(つと)めております。
question.romaji: Risouteki na jikken kankyou to wa ikanai made mo, gen'yuu no setsubi wo saidaigen ni katsuyou shite kekka wo daso to tsutomete orimasu.
question.arti: Although experimental conditions may not have been ideal, I strove to produce results by maximizing the use of available facilities.

choices.jepang: その姿勢(しせい)は申(もう)し分(ぶん)ありません。加(くわ)えて、他大学(ただいがく)との共同(きょうどう)研究(けんきゅう)という選択肢(せんたくし)も視野(しや)に入(い)れてはいかがでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Sono shisei wa moushiibun arimasen. Kuwa ete, tadaigaku to no kyoudou kenkyuu to iu sentakushi mo shiya ni irete wa ikaga deshou ka.
choices.arti: That attitude is admirable. Additionally, how about also considering collaborative research with other universities?

choices.jepang: その創意工夫(そういくふう)の姿勢(しせい)は高(たか)く評価(ひょうか)します。制約(せいやく)の中(なか)での研究(けんきゅう)こそが、真(しん)の学術(がくじゅつ)的(てき)力量(りきりょう)を示(しめ)すものです。
choices.romaji: Sono soui kufu no shisei wa takaku hyouka shimasu. Seiyaku no naka de no kenkyuu koso ga, shin no gakujutsuteki rikiryou wo shimesu mono desu.
choices.arti: I deeply appreciate that creative attitude. Research under constraints is what truly demonstrates one's academic capabilities.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that attitude is admirable; how about also considering collaborative research with other universities' because it provides appreciation along with concrete advice for overcoming facility limitations. Meanwhile, 'I deeply appreciate that creative attitude' although positive, only praises without offering useful forward-looking guidance for the student.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: この研究(けんきゅう)成果(せいか)は、関連(かんれん)する学術(がくじゅつ)分野(ぶんや)全体(ぜんたい)に影響(えいきょう)を与(あた)えずにはおかないでしょう。
question.romaji: Kono kenkyuu seika wa, kanren suru gakujutsu bunya zentai ni eikyou wo ataez u ni wa okana i deshou.
question.arti: These research findings will undoubtedly have an impact across all related academic fields.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。ただ、影響(えいきょう)の範囲(はんい)は限定的(げんていてき)であると謙虚(けんきょ)に認(みと)め、過度(かど)な期待(きたい)は持(も)たないようにしたいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Tada, eikyou no hani wa genteiteki de aru to kenkyo ni mitome, kada na kitai wa motanai you ni shitai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. However, I would like to humbly acknowledge that the scope of impact is limited and try not to have excessive expectations.

choices.jepang: 先生(せんせい)にそう言(い)っていただけると、三(さん)年間(ねんかん)の苦労(くろう)が報(むく)われた思(おも)いです。より多(おお)くの研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)に届(とど)くよう、論文(ろんぶん)投稿(とうこう)先(さき)も慎重(しんちょう)に選(えら)びます。
choices.romaji: Sensei ni sou itte itadakeru to, san nen kan no kurou ga mukawareta omoi desu. Yori ooku no kenkyuusha ni todoku you, ronbun toukou saki mo shinchou ni erabimasu.
choices.arti: With those words from you, Professor, I feel the three years of struggle have been rewarded. I will also carefully select journals for submission to reach more researchers.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the three years of struggle have been rewarded; I will carefully select journals for submission' because it responds to the supervisor's praise with gratitude and a strategic plan. Meanwhile, 'the scope of impact is limited, I don't want excessive expectations' contradicts the supervisor's statement about the research having a significant impact -- downplaying one's own research when the supervisor is praising it is a disproportionate response.

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、データの不備(ふび)が判明(はんめい)し、論文(ろんぶん)の全面(ぜんめん)改稿(かいこう)を余儀(よぎ)なくされることになりました。
question.romaji: Sensei, deeta no fubi ga hanmei shi, ronbun no zenmen kaikou wo yogi naku sareru koto ni narimashita.
question.arti: Professor, deficiencies in the data were discovered, and I was forced to completely revise the thesis.

choices.jepang: それは大変(たいへん)でしたね。しかし、不備(ふび)を早期(そうき)に発見(はっけん)できたことは不幸(ふこう)中(ちゅう)の幸(さいわ)いです。今後(こんご)の方針(ほうしん)を一緒(いっしょ)に考(かんが)えましょう。
choices.romaji: Sore wa taihen deshita ne. Shikashi, fubi wo souki ni hakken dekita koto wa fukou chuu no saiwai desu. Kongo no houshin wo issho ni kangaemashou.
choices.arti: That is indeed difficult. However, discovering the deficiencies early is a blessing in disguise. Let us think about the next steps together.

choices.jepang: それは辛(つら)い状況(じょうきょう)ですね。ただ、この経験(けいけん)が研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)としての厳密(げんみつ)さを鍛(きた)える礎(いしずえ)となることを信(しん)じ、共(とも)に乗(の)り越(こ)えましょう。
choices.romaji: Sore wa tsurai joukyou desu ne. Tada, kono keiken ga kenkyuusha to shite no genmitsu sa wo kitaeru ishizue to naru koto wo shinji, tomo ni nori koemashou.
choices.arti: That is indeed a difficult situation. However, trust that this experience will forge the foundation of meticulousness as a researcher, and let us overcome it together.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'this experience will forge the foundation of meticulousness as a researcher; let us overcome it together' because it provides emotional support along with a positive long-term perspective. Meanwhile, 'a blessing in disguise; let us think about next steps' although well-intentioned, the phrase 'blessing in disguise' could feel dismissive of the difficulty the student is experiencing with a forced total revision.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 論文(ろんぶん)の方法論(ほうほうろん)について言(い)えば、質的(しつてき)研究(けんきゅう)にせよ量的(りょうてき)研究(けんきゅう)にせよ、一貫(いっかん)した分析(ぶんせき)手法(しゅほう)を採用(さいよう)することが肝要(かんよう)です。
question.romaji: Ronbun no houhouron ni tsuite ieba, shitsuteki kenkyuu ni seyo ryouteki kenkyuu ni seyo, ikkan shita bunseki shuhou wo saiyou suru koto ga kanyou desu.
question.arti: Regarding thesis methodology, whether qualitative or quantitative research, adopting a consistent analytical method is of paramount importance.

choices.jepang: ご指導(しどう)に従(したが)い、混合(こんごう)研究法(けんきゅうほう)を採用(さいよう)しつつも、各(かく)段階(だんかい)での分析(ぶんせき)基準(きじゅん)の統一(とういつ)を図(はか)ってまいります。
choices.romaji: Go shidou ni shitagat, kongou kenkyuuhou wo saiyou shi tsutsu mo, kaku dankai de no bunseki kijun no touitsu wo hakatte mairimasu.
choices.arti: Following your guidance, Professor, while adopting a mixed research method, I will strive to unify analytical standards at each stage.

choices.jepang: 方法論(ほうほうろん)の一貫性(いっかんせい)よりも、結果(けっか)の面白(おもしろ)さのほうが重要(じゅうよう)ではないでしょうか。読者(どくしゃ)を惹(ひ)きつける内容(ないよう)こそが優先(ゆうせん)されるべきです。
choices.romaji: Houhouron no ikkansei yori mo, kekka no omoshirosa no hou ga juuyou de wa nai deshou ka. Dokusha wo hikitsukeru naiyou koso ga yuusen sareru beki desu.
choices.arti: Isn't the excitement of results more important than methodological consistency? Content that engages readers should be prioritized.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'while adopting a mixed research method, I will strive to unify analytical standards at each stage' because it demonstrates understanding of the importance of methodological consistency, matching 「にせよ〜にせよ」. Meanwhile, 'excitement of results is more important than methodological consistency' is gravely misguided in an academic context -- without consistent methodology, research results have no scientific validity, no matter how 'interesting.'

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、査読者(さどくしゃ)からの指摘(してき)を受(う)けまして、結論(けつろん)部分(ぶぶん)の論理(ろんり)展開(てんかい)を大幅(おおはば)に書(か)き直(なお)す所存(しょぞん)です。ご確認(かくにん)かたがた、ご助言(じょげん)をいただけますでしょうか。
question.romaji: Sensei, sadokusha kara no shiteki wo ukemashite, ketsuron bubun no ronri tenkai wo oohaba ni kakinaosu shozon desu. Go kakunin katagata, go jogen wo itadakemasu deshou ka.
question.arti: Professor, after receiving the reviewer's comments, I intend to substantially rewrite the logical development in the conclusion section. Could you provide advice and review it?

choices.jepang: 査読者(さどくしゃ)の意見(いけん)は参考(さんこう)程度(ていど)にとどめ、自分(じぶん)の論理(ろんり)に自信(じしん)を持(も)つべきです。大幅(おおはば)な書(か)き直(なお)しは不要(ふよう)でしょう。
choices.romaji: Sadokusha no iken wa sankou teido ni todome, jibun no ronri ni jishin wo motsu beki desu. Oohaba na kakinaoshi wa fuyou deshou.
choices.arti: It would be better to treat the reviewer's opinions merely as references and trust your own logic. A substantial rewrite is unnecessary.

choices.jepang: もちろんです。改稿案(かいこうあん)が出来上(できあ)がり次第(しだい)、拝見(はいけん)しましょう。査読者(さどくしゃ)の指摘(してき)を真摯(しんし)に受(う)け止(と)める姿勢(しせい)は研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)として模範的(もはんてき)です。
choices.romaji: Mochiron desu. Kaikouan ga dekiagari shidai, haiken shimashou. Sadokusha no shiteki wo shinshi ni uketomeru shisei wa kenkyuusha to shite mohanteki desu.
choices.arti: Of course. As soon as the revised draft is complete, I will review it. Your attitude of earnestly accepting reviewer comments is exemplary as a researcher.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'as soon as the revised draft is complete, I will review it; your attitude of earnestly accepting reviewer comments is exemplary' because it supports a healthy peer review process. Meanwhile, 'treat reviewer opinions merely as references; a substantial rewrite is unnecessary' contradicts academic principles -- peer review is a fundamental quality mechanism in scholarly publication.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 論文(ろんぶん)審査(しんさ)においては、研究(けんきゅう)の独自性(どくじせい)のみならず、社会(しゃかい)的(てき)意義(いぎ)も問(と)われることを忘(わす)れてはなりません。あなたの研究(けんきゅう)は、その点(てん)においても申(もう)し分(ぶん)ないものです。
question.romaji: Ronbun shinsa ni oite wa, kenkyuu no dokujisei nomi narazu, shakaiteki igi mo towareru koto wo wasurete wa narimasen. Anata no kenkyuu wa, sono ten ni oite mo moushi bun nai mono desu.
question.arti: In the thesis evaluation, remember that not only research originality but also social significance will be examined. Your research is exemplary in that regard as well.

choices.jepang: 身(み)に余(あま)るお言葉(ことば)でございます。社会(しゃかい)実装(じっそう)への道筋(みちすじ)も含(ふく)め、実践的(じっせんてき)な提言(ていげん)を論文(ろんぶん)の最終章(さいしゅうしょう)に盛(も)り込(こ)む所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Mi ni amaru okotoba de gozaimasu. Shakai jissou he no michisuji mo fukume, jissenteki na teigen wo ronbun no saishuu shou ni morikomi shozon desu.
choices.arti: That is too much praise. Including a roadmap for social implementation, I intend to include practical recommendations in the final chapter of the thesis.

choices.jepang: ありがとうございます。ただ、基礎(きそ)研究(けんきゅう)の意義(いぎ)は社会(しゃかい)への直接的(ちょくせつてき)な貢献(こうけん)とは異(こと)なりますので、社会的(しゃかいてき)意義(いぎ)を強調(きょうちょう)する必要(ひつよう)はないかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Arigatou gozaimasu. Tada, kiso kenkyuu no igi wa shakai he no chokusetsuteki na kouken to wa kotonarimasu no de, shakaiteki igi wo kyouchou suru hitsuyou wa nai ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Thank you. However, the significance of basic research differs from direct contributions to society, so I believe there is no need to emphasize social significance.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'I intend to include practical recommendations and a roadmap for social implementation in the final chapter' because it responds to the supervisor's advice with a concrete plan. Meanwhile, 'there is no need to emphasize social significance' rejects the supervisor's advice -- in the context of a dissertation, social significance is often an important evaluation factor.

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)のご指導(しどう)なくしては、この研究(けんきゅう)の完成(かんせい)はあり得(え)ませんでした。心(こころ)より感謝(かんしゃ)申(もう)し上(あ)げます。学位(がくい)取得後(しゅとくご)も、この分野(ぶんや)の発展(はってん)に寄与(きよ)すべく研鑽(けんさん)を積(つ)んでまいります。
question.romaji: Sensei no go shidou naku shite wa, kono kenkyuu no kansei wa ariemasendeshita. Kokoro yori kansha moushi agemasu. Gakui shutokugo mo, kono bunya no hatten ni kiyo subeku kensan wo tsunde mairimasu.
question.arti: Without your guidance, Professor, completing this research would have been impossible. I express my deepest gratitude. Even after obtaining my degree, I will continue to refine myself to contribute to the development of this field.

choices.jepang: お世辞(せじ)は結構(けっこう)です。大切(たいせつ)なのは結果(けっか)ですから、学位(がくい)取得後(しゅとくご)の実績(じっせき)で証明(しょうめい)してください。
choices.romaji: Oseji wa kekkou desu. Taisetsu na no wa kekka desu kara, gakui shutokugo no jisseki de shoumei shite kudasai.
choices.arti: There is no need for pleasantries. What matters are results, so prove it through achievements after obtaining your degree.

choices.jepang: その言葉(ことば)を聞(き)いて、指導者(しどうしゃ)冥利(みょうり)に尽(つ)きる思(おも)いです。あなたの今後(こんご)の活躍(かつやく)を心(こころ)より期待(きたい)しておりますし、いつでも相談(そうだん)に応(おう)じましょう。
choices.romaji: Sono kotoba wo kiite, shidousha myouri ni tsukiru omoi desu. Anata no kongo no katsuyaku wo kokoro yori kitai shite orimasu shi, itsudemo soudan ni oujimashou.
choices.arti: Hearing those words, I feel this is the greatest happiness as a supervisor. I deeply hope for your future success, and I will always be available for consultation.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'this is the greatest happiness as a supervisor; I deeply hope for your future success' because it responds to the student's gratitude with warmth and ongoing support. Meanwhile, 'there is no need for pleasantries; prove it through achievements' is too cold and fails to appreciate the sincere emotional moment at the end of their shared academic journey.

conversation.speaker: 指導教員(しどうきょういん)・佐藤(さとう)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 論文(ろんぶん)の結論(けつろん)部分(ぶぶん)において、研究(けんきゅう)の限界(げんかい)を正直(しょうじき)に記述(きじゅつ)することもまた、学術(がくじゅつ)的(てき)誠実(せいじつ)さの表(あらわ)れです。研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)たるもの、自(みずか)らの研究(けんきゅう)の射程(しゃてい)を冷静(れいせい)に見極(みきわ)めるべきです。
question.romaji: Ronbun no ketsuron bubun ni oite, kenkyuu no genkai wo shoujiki ni kijutsu suru koto mo mata, gakujutsuteki seijitsu sa no araware desu.
question.arti: In the thesis conclusion, honestly describing the limitations of the research is also a reflection of academic integrity. As a researcher, one must be able to view the scope of one's own research calmly.

choices.jepang: 限界(げんかい)を認(みと)めると、論文(ろんぶん)全体(ぜんたい)の価値(かち)が下(さ)がってしまうのではないでしょうか。あえて記載(きさい)しないほうがよいかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Genkai wo mitomeru to, ronbun zentai no kachi ga sagatte shimau no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: If we acknowledge limitations, won't the overall value of the thesis decrease? I believe it is better not to include them.

choices.jepang: ご指導(しどう)を踏(ふ)まえ、第(だい)六章(ろくしょう)に「本研究(ほんけんきゅう)の限界(げんかい)と今後(こんご)の展望(てんぼう)」の節(せつ)を新(あら)たに設(もう)けます。
choices.romaji: Go shidou wo fumae, dai roku shou ni 'hon kenkyuu no genkai to kongo no tenbou' no setsu wo arata ni moukemasu.
choices.arti: Based on your guidance, Professor, I will create a new section titled 'Limitations of This Study and Future Prospects' in Chapter Six.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'creating a new section on limitations and future prospects' demonstrates academic integrity, matching 「たるもの」. Meanwhile, 'better not to include them' contradicts the principle of academic honesty -- concealing limitations actually damages credibility.

conversation.speaker: 大学院生(だいがくいんせい)・中村(なかむら)

question.jepang: 先生(せんせい)、長(なが)い道(みち)のりでしたが、ついに博士(はくし)論文(ろんぶん)の最終稿(さいしゅうこう)が完成(かんせい)いたしました。先生(せんせい)のご指導(しどう)なくしては到底(とうてい)成(な)し遂(と)げられなかったことです。この分野(ぶんや)の発展(はってん)に寄与(きよ)できるよう、今後(こんご)とも精進(しょうじん)してまいります。
question.romaji: Sensei, nagai michinori deshita ga, tsui ni hakushi ronbun no saishuukou ga kansei itashimashita.
question.arti: Professor, this long journey has finally reached its destination; the final draft of the doctoral dissertation is complete. Without your guidance, this would have been impossible. I will continue striving to contribute to the development of this field.

choices.jepang: 完成(かんせい)したからといって安心(あんしん)するのは早(はや)いですよ。審査(しんさ)で不合格(ふごうかく)になる可能性(かのうせい)も十分(じゅうぶん)にありますからね。
choices.romaji: Kansei shita kara to itte anshin suru no wa hayai desu yo. Shinsa de fugoukaku ni naru kanousei mo juubun ni arimasu kara ne.
choices.arti: Don't be too relieved just because it is finished. The possibility of failing the evaluation is quite high, you know.

choices.jepang: よくぞここまで辿(たど)り着(つ)きましたね。あなたの研究(けんきゅう)が学界(がっかい)に新(あたら)たな地平(ちへい)を切(き)り開(ひら)くことを願(ねが)ってやみません。今後(こんご)のご活躍(かつやく)を心(こころ)より祈念(きねん)しております。
choices.romaji: Yokuzo koko made tadoritsukimashita ne. Anata no kenkyuu ga gakkai ni aratana chihei wo kirihiraku koto wo negatte yamimasen.
choices.arti: Congratulations on reaching this point. I ceaselessly hope that your research will open new horizons for the academic world. I wish you success from the bottom of my heart.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appropriately uses 「願ってやまない」 to express sincere hopes at the end of an academic journey. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer is too negative and lacks empathy at a moment that should be full of appreciation.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: 二国間(にこくかん)条約(じょうやく)の締結(ていけつ)ともなると、双方(そうほう)の国益(こくえき)を慎重(しんちょう)に勘案(かんあん)しなければなりません。
question.romaji: Nikoku kan jouyaku no teiketsu to mo naru to, souhou no kokueki wo shinchou ni kanan shinakereba narimasen.
question.arti: When it comes to signing a bilateral treaty, the national interests of both parties must be considered with the utmost care.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の通(とお)りです。ただ、条約(じょうやく)の締結(ていけつ)は形式的(けいしきてき)な手続(てつづ)きに過(す)ぎませんので、細部(さいぶ)の検討(けんとう)は後回(あとまわ)しにしてもよいかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no toori desu. Tada, jouyaku no teiketsu wa keishiki teki na tetsuzuki ni sugimasen no de, saibu no kentou wa atomawashi ni shite mo yoi ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: As you have indicated. However, signing a treaty is merely a formal procedure, so I believe delaying the detailed examination would be acceptable.

choices.jepang: 全(まった)くもってその通(とお)りです。特(とく)に経済(けいざい)条項(じょうこう)については、両国(りょうこく)の産業(さんぎょう)構造(こうぞう)の違(ちが)いを十分(じゅうぶん)に反映(はんえい)させる必要(ひつよう)がございます。
choices.romaji: Mattaku motte sono toori desu. Toku ni keizai joukou ni tsuite wa, ryoukoku no sangyou kouzou no chigai wo juubun ni hanei saseru hitsuyou ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Particularly regarding economic clauses, it is necessary to adequately reflect the differences in industrial structures between both nations.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'economic clauses need to adequately reflect the differences in industrial structures' because it responds with identification of a concrete issue to be addressed in a bilateral treaty. Meanwhile, 'signing a treaty is merely a formal procedure' is gravely misguided -- a bilateral treaty is not a mere formality, and treating details lightly is a fatal mistake in diplomacy.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: わが国(くに)の代表団(だいひょうだん)は、相手国(あいてこく)の圧力(あつりょく)をものともせず、核心(かくしん)となる主権(しゅけん)問題(もんだい)では一歩(いっぽ)も譲(ゆず)りませんでした。
question.romaji: Waga kuni no daihyoudan wa, aiteko ku no atsuryoku wo mono to mo sezu, kakushin to naru shuken mondai de wa ippo mo yuzurimasendeshita.
question.arti: Our nation's delegation, undeterred by pressure from the opposing nation, did not yield an inch on the core sovereignty issue.

choices.jepang: その毅然(きぜん)とした態度(たいど)こそが、国際(こくさい)交渉(こうしょう)における信頼(しんらい)の礎(いしずえ)となります。妥協(だきょう)なき姿勢(しせい)が長期的(ちょうきてき)な外交(がいこう)関係(かんけい)を安定(あんてい)させるのです。
choices.romaji: Sono kizen to shita taido koso ga, kokusai koushou ni okeru shinrai no ishizue to narimasu. Dakyou naki shisei ga choukit eki na gaikou kankei wo antei saseru no desu.
choices.arti: That firm stance is the foundation of trust in international negotiations. That uncompromising position stabilizes long-term diplomatic relations.

choices.jepang: その毅然(きぜん)たる交渉(こうしょう)姿勢(しせい)は賞賛(しょうさん)に値(あたい)します。一方(いっぽう)で、戦略的(せんりゃくてき)な妥協点(だきょうてん)を見(み)極(きわ)める柔軟性(じゅうなんせい)も外交(がいこう)の要(かなめ)であることを忘(わす)れてはなりません。
choices.romaji: Sono kizen taru koushou shisei wa shousan ni atai shimasu. Ippou de, senryaku teki na dakyou ten wo mikiwameru juunan sei mo gaikou no kaname de aru koto wo wasurete wa narimasen.
choices.arti: That firm negotiating stance is commendable. On the other hand, the flexibility to find strategic compromise points is also an essence of diplomacy that must not be forgotten.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that firm stance is commendable, but flexibility to find strategic compromise is also essential to diplomacy' because it provides balanced appreciation with an important reminder about diplomacy. Meanwhile, 'uncompromising position stabilizes long-term relations' is too absolute -- 'uncompromising' is actually dangerous in complex diplomatic relations.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: 相手国(あいてこく)の代表(だいひょう)は、今(いま)すぐにでも合意(ごうい)書(しょ)に署名(しょめい)せんばかりの様子(ようす)でした。
question.romaji: Aiteko ku no daihyou wa, ima sugu ni demo goui sho ni shomei sen bakari no yousu deshita.
question.arti: The representatives of the opposing nation appeared as if about to sign the agreement document.

choices.jepang: それは吉兆(きっちょう)ですね。相手国(あいてこく)が急(いそ)いでいるということは、わが国(くに)も同(おな)じ速度(そくど)で署名(しょめい)を進(すす)めるべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sore wa kicchoo desu ne. Aiteko ku ga isoide iru to iu koto wa, waga kuni mo onaji sokudo de shomei wo susumeru beki deshou.
choices.arti: That is a good sign. Since the opposing nation is rushing, our nation should also accelerate the signing at the same pace.

choices.jepang: その焦(あせ)りが交渉(こうしょう)上(じょう)の弱点(じゃくてん)となり得(う)ます。わが国(くに)はその機(き)を逃(のが)さず、より有利(ゆうり)な条件(じょうけん)を引(ひ)き出(だ)す交渉(こうしょう)を展開(てんかい)すべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sono aseri ga koushou jou no jakuten to nari emasu. Waga kuni wa sono ki wo nogasazu, yori yuuri na jouken wo hikidasu koushou wo tenkai subekideshoo.
choices.arti: That haste could be a weakness in negotiations. Our nation should seize this opportunity and advance negotiations for more favorable terms.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that haste could be a weakness; our nation should secure more favorable terms' because it identifies a strategic opportunity from the opponent's weak position. Meanwhile, 'our nation should also accelerate the signing' is wrong -- in diplomacy, when the other party is rushing, that is precisely the time to be patient to obtain better conditions.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: 完全(かんぜん)な合意(ごうい)とはいかないまでも、両国(りょうこく)の基本(きほん)的(てき)な立場(たちば)の共通(きょうつう)点(てん)を確認(かくにん)することはできました。
question.romaji: Kanzen na goui to wa ikanai made mo, ryoukoku no kihonteki na tachiba no kyoutsuuten wo kakunin suru koto wa dekimashita.
question.arti: Although we may not have reached a full agreement, we successfully confirmed the common ground between both nations' fundamental positions.

choices.jepang: それは交渉(こうしょう)の第一歩(だいいっぽ)として十分(じゅうぶん)な成果(せいか)です。この共通(きょうつう)基盤(きばん)を足(あし)がかりに、次回(じかい)の協議(きょうぎ)へとつなげましょう。
choices.romaji: Sore wa koushou no daiippo to shite juubun na seika desu. Kono kyoutsu kiban wo ashigakari ni, jikai no kyougi he to tsunagemashou.
choices.arti: That is a sufficient result as a first step in negotiations. Let us use this common ground as a foundation for the next round of talks.

choices.jepang: 共通点(きょうつうてん)の確認(かくにん)のみでは不十分(ふじゅうぶん)です。完全(かんぜん)な合意(ごうい)なき交渉(こうしょう)は失敗(しっぱい)とみなされるべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Kyoutsuu ten no kakunin nomi de wa fujuubun desu. Kanzen na goui naki koushou wa shippai to minasareru beki deshou.
choices.arti: Merely confirming common ground is insufficient. Negotiations without a full agreement should be considered a failure.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that is a sufficient result as a first step; let us use this as a foundation for the next round' because it appreciates realistic incremental progress in diplomacy. Meanwhile, 'negotiations without a full agreement should be considered a failure' is unrealistic -- international diplomacy rarely achieves a full agreement at once, and identifying common ground is already valuable progress.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: この協定(きょうてい)の締結(ていけつ)は、地域(ちいき)全体(ぜんたい)の安全保障(あんぜんほしょう)体制(たいせい)に変革(へんかく)をもたらさずにはおかないでしょう。
question.romaji: Kono kyoutei no teiketsu wa, chiiki zentai no anzenhoshou taisei ni henkaku wo motarasa zu ni wa okana i deshou.
question.arti: The signing of this treaty will undoubtedly bring changes to the security framework of the entire region.

choices.jepang: 同感(どうかん)です。近隣(きんりん)諸国(しょこく)への事前(じぜん)の根回(ねまわ)しと情報(じょうほう)共有(きょうゆう)を怠(おこた)らないよう、外交(がいこう)ルートを通(とお)じた調整(ちょうせい)を急(いそ)ぎたいと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Doukan desu. Kinrin shokoku he no jizen no nemawashi to jouhou kyouyuu wo okotaranai you, gaikou ruuto wo toujita chousei wo isogi tai to omoimasu.
choices.arti: I agree. I would like to promptly make adjustments through diplomatic channels so as not to be remiss in lobbying and sharing information with neighboring countries first.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。ゆえに、締結(ていけつ)に際(さい)しては関係(かんけい)各国(かっこく)との綿密(めんみつ)な事前(じぜん)協議(きょうぎ)を経(へ)ることが肝要(かんよう)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Yueni, teiketsu ni saishite wa kankei kakkoku to no menmitsu na jizen kyougi wo heru koto ga kanyou ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: You are absolutely right. Therefore, I believe it is crucial to undergo thorough consultation with the relevant nations before signing.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'it is crucial to undergo thorough consultation with relevant nations before signing' because it responds with appropriate and formal procedural steps. Meanwhile, 'I would like to promptly make adjustments through diplomatic channels' although containing a similar idea, its tone is too hasty and insufficiently formal for high-level diplomatic context.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: 相手国(あいてこく)の突然(とつぜん)の政変(せいへん)により、交渉(こうしょう)の全面(ぜんめん)的(てき)な再開(さいかい)を余儀(よぎ)なくされることになりました。
question.romaji: Aiteko ku no totsuzen no seihen ni yori, koushou no zenmen teki na saikai wo yogi naku sareru koto ni narimashita.
question.arti: Due to a sudden change of government in the opposing nation, we were forced to restart negotiations from the very beginning.

choices.jepang: それは困(こま)りましたね。しかし、前政権(ぜんせいけん)との合意(ごうい)内容(ないよう)をそのまま新政権(しんせいけん)に引(ひ)き継(つ)がせるよう強(つよ)く要求(ようきゅう)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Sore wa komarimashita ne. Shikashi, zen seiken to no goui naiyou wo sono mama shin seiken ni hikitsugaseru you tsuyoku youkyuu subeki desu.
choices.arti: That is indeed troublesome. However, we must firmly demand that the new government inherit the content of the agreement with the previous government as is.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に遺憾(いかん)ながら、それが外交(がいこう)の現実(げんじつ)というものです。新(あたら)たな政権(せいけん)との関係(かんけい)構築(こうちく)を最優先(さいゆうせん)課題(かだい)として取(と)り組(く)む必要(ひつよう)があります。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni ikan nagara, sore ga gaikou no genjitsu to iu mono desu. Aratana seiken to no kankei kouchiku wo saiyuusen kadai to shite tori kumu hitsuyou ga arimasu.
choices.arti: It is truly regrettable, but such is the reality of diplomacy. We need to prioritize building relationships with the new government.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'such is the reality of diplomacy; we need to prioritize building relationships with the new government' because it responds with pragmatism -- accepting reality and focusing on next steps. Meanwhile, 'we must demand the new government inherit the previous agreement as is' is naive and unrealistic -- in international diplomacy, changes of government often alter policy directions.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: 外交(がいこう)交渉(こうしょう)においては、いかなる困難(こんなん)な局面(きょくめん)であれ、対話(たいわ)の糸(いと)を断(た)ち切(き)ってはなりません。それが外交(がいこう)ならではの原則(げんそく)です。
question.romaji: Gaikou koushou ni oite wa, ikanaru konnan na kyokumen de are, taiwa no ito wo tachikitte wa narimasen. Sore ga gaikou nara de wa no gensoku desu.
question.arti: In diplomatic negotiations, no matter how difficult the situation, one must never sever the thread of dialogue. That is the quintessential principle of diplomacy.

choices.jepang: 原則(げんそく)としてはその通(とお)りですが、相手国(あいてこく)が極端(きょくたん)な立場(たちば)を取(と)る場合(ばあい)は、対話(たいわ)を打(う)ち切(き)り制裁(せいさい)に踏(ふ)み切(き)るべきです。
choices.romaji: Gensoku to shite wa sono toori desu ga, aiteko ku ga kyokutan na tachiba wo toru baai wa, taiwa wo uchikiri seisai ni fumikiru beki desu.
choices.arti: As a principle it is correct, but if the opposing nation takes an extreme position, we should cut off dialogue and impose sanctions.

choices.jepang: ご卓見(たっけん)です。水面下(すいめんか)での非公式(ひこうしき)チャンネルの維持(いじ)も、外交(がいこう)における重要(じゅうよう)な手段(しゅだん)と心得(こころえ)ております。
choices.romaji: Go takken desu. Suimenka de no hikoushiki channeru no iji mo, gaikou ni okeru juuyou na shudan to kokoroete orimasu.
choices.arti: A most insightful view. I also understand that maintaining unofficial back-channels beneath the surface is an important diplomatic tool.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'maintaining unofficial back-channels is also an important diplomatic tool' because it demonstrates deep understanding of diplomatic practice by referring to back-channel diplomacy. Meanwhile, 'we should cut off dialogue and impose sanctions' directly contradicts the principle of 「ならではの」 just emphasized -- diplomacy precisely requires keeping dialogue open.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: 大臣(だいじん)、多国間(たこくかん)協議(きょうぎ)の場(ば)において、わが国(くに)の提案(ていあん)は各国(かっこく)の代表(だいひょう)に深(ふか)い感銘(かんめい)を与(あた)えずにはおかなかったようです。
question.romaji: Daijin, takokukan kyougi no ba ni oite, waga kuni no teian wa kakkoku no daihyou ni fukai kanmei wo ataezu ni wa okanakatta you desu.
question.arti: Minister, at the multilateral consultation forum, it appears that our nation's proposal has made a profound impression on the representatives of each country.

choices.jepang: 各国(かっこく)の賛同(さんどう)を得(え)たのであれば、我(わ)が国(くに)が主導権(しゅどうけん)を握(にぎ)り、一方的(いっぽうてき)に条件(じょうけん)を決(き)めてしまうのがよいでしょう。
choices.romaji: Kakkoku no sandou wo eta no de areba, wagakuni ga shudouken wo nigiri, ippouteki ni jouken wo kimete shimau no ga yoi deshou.
choices.arti: If agreement has been obtained from each country, our nation should seize leadership and unilaterally determine the terms.

choices.jepang: それは心強(こころづよ)い報告(ほうこく)です。この勢(いきお)いを活(い)かし、次(つぎ)の段階(だんかい)では具体的(ぐたいてき)な実施(じっし)計画(けいかく)の策定(さくてい)へと進(すす)めてまいりましょう。
choices.romaji: Sore wa kokorozuyoi houkoku desu. Kono ikioi wo ikashi, tsugi no dankai de wa gutaiteki na jisshi keikaku no sakutei he to susumete mairimasyou.
choices.arti: That is encouraging news. Let us capitalize on this momentum, and at the next stage, advance to formulating a concrete implementation plan.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'let us capitalize on this momentum and advance to formulating a concrete implementation plan' because it shows realistic next steps in multilateral diplomacy. Meanwhile, 'our nation should unilaterally determine the terms' contradicts the principles of multilateralism -- unilateral action would destroy the newly built trust.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: 外交官(がいこうかん)たるもの、個人的(こじんてき)な感情(かんじょう)を超(こ)えて、国家(こっか)の利益(りえき)を最前線(さいぜんせん)で守(まも)る使命(しめい)を帯(お)びていることを常(つね)に自覚(じかく)しなければなりません。
question.romaji: Gaikouken taru mono, kojinteki na kanjou wo koete, kokka no rieki wo saizensen de mamoru shimei wo obite iru koto wo tsune ni jikaku shinakereba narimasen.
question.arti: As a diplomat, one must always be aware of the mission to transcend personal feelings and protect national interests at the front lines.

choices.jepang: 大臣(だいじん)のご指摘(してき)は理解(りかい)しますが、外交官(がいこうかん)も人間(にんげん)ですから、時(とき)には感情(かんじょう)に従(したが)って判断(はんだん)することも必要(ひつよう)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Daijin no go shiteki wa rikai shimasu ga, gaikouken mo ningen desu kara, toki ni wa kanjou ni shitagatte handan suru koto mo hitsuyou de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: I understand what you indicate, Minister, but diplomats are also human; isn't it sometimes necessary to make decisions following one's feelings?

choices.jepang: 肝(きも)に銘(めい)じます。今回(こんかい)の交渉(こうしょう)においても、その心構(こころがま)えを忘(わす)れることなく、一意専心(いちいせんしん)に任務(にんむ)を遂行(すいこう)してまいります。
choices.romaji: Kimo ni meijimasu. Konkai no koushou ni oite mo, sono kokorogamae wo wasureru koto naku, ichiisensin ni ninmu wo suikou shite mairimasu.
choices.arti: I shall take that to heart. In the current negotiations as well, without forgetting that mental preparedness, I will carry out my duties wholeheartedly.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'I shall take that to heart and carry out my duties wholeheartedly' because it responds to 「たるもの」 with appropriate professional commitment. Meanwhile, 'sometimes it is necessary to make decisions following one's feelings' contradicts the principle of diplomatic professionalism just emphasized by the superior.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: 最終的(さいしゅうてき)に、両国(りょうこく)の首脳会談(しゅのうかいだん)を経(へ)て、歴史的(れきしてき)な共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)が発表(はっぴょう)されました。この成果(せいか)を両国(りょうこく)の友好(ゆうこう)関係(かんけい)のさらなる深化(しんか)につなげてやみたいものです。
question.romaji: Saishuteki ni, ryoukoku no shunou kaidan wo hete, rekishiteki na kyoudou seimei ga happyou saremashita. Kono seika wo ryoukoku no yuukou kankei no saranaru shinka ni tsunagete yamitai mono desu.
question.arti: Finally, through a summit between both nations, a historic joint statement has been announced. I earnestly wish to connect this achievement to further deepening of friendly relations between the two countries.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に画期的(かっきてき)な成果(せいか)です。この共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)を礎(いしずえ)に、各(かく)分野(ぶんや)での協力(きょうりょく)関係(かんけい)を着実(ちゃくじつ)に構築(こうちく)してまいりましょう。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni kakkiteki na seika desu. Kono kyoudou seimei wo ishizue ni, kaku bunya de no kyouryoku kankei wo chakujitsu ni kouchiku shite mairimasyou.
choices.arti: A truly revolutionary achievement. Let us use this joint statement as a foundation to gradually build cooperative relations across various fields.

choices.jepang: 共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)は所詮(しょせん)紙切(かみき)れに過(す)ぎません。重要(じゅうよう)なのは実際(じっさい)の行動(こうどう)であり、声明(せいめい)だけで満足(まんぞく)すべきではありません。
choices.romaji: Kyoudou seimei wa shosen kamikire ni sugimasen. Juuyou na no wa jissai no koudou de ari, seimei dake de manzoku subeki de wa arimasen.
choices.arti: A joint statement is ultimately just a piece of paper. What matters is concrete action, and we should not be satisfied with just a statement.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'let us use this joint statement as a foundation to build cooperative relations across various fields' because it appreciates the diplomatic achievement while directing toward concrete next steps. Meanwhile, 'a joint statement is just a piece of paper' is too cynical and fails to appreciate the long and complex diplomatic process that has been undertaken.

conversation.speaker: 外務大臣(がいむだいじん)・高橋(たかはし)

question.jepang: 外交(がいこう)における信頼(しんらい)構築(こうちく)は、一朝一夕(いっちょういっせき)に成(な)し遂(と)げられるものではなく、たゆまぬ対話(たいわ)の積(つ)み重(かさ)ねを通(つう)じてのみ実現(じつげん)し得(う)るものです。
question.romaji: Gaikou ni okeru shinrai kouchiku wa, icchooisseki ni nashitogererareru mono de wa naku, tayumanu taiwa no tsumikasane wo tsuujite nomi jitsugen shi uru mono desu.
question.arti: Building trust in diplomacy cannot be achieved overnight; it can only be realized through the accumulation of ceaseless dialogue.

choices.jepang: まさに仰(おっしゃ)る通(とお)りです。特(とく)に多国間(たこくかん)外交(がいこう)においては、各国(かっこく)の利害(りがい)が複雑(ふくざつ)に絡(から)み合(あ)うため、忍耐強(にんたいづよ)い交渉(こうしょう)が不可欠(ふかけつ)です。
choices.romaji: Masa ni ossharu toori desu. Toku ni takokukan gaikou ni oite wa, kakkoku no rigai ga fukuzatsu ni karamiau tame, nintaizuyoi koushou ga fukaketsu desu.
