choices.arti: You are absolutely right. Especially in multilateral diplomacy, where the interests of each nation are intricately interconnected, patient negotiation is indispensable.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かにそうですが、時(とき)には強硬(きょうこう)な姿勢(しせい)で短期間(たんきかん)に結果(けっか)を出(だ)すことも外交(がいこう)の一手(いって)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni sou desu ga, toki ni wa kyoukou na shisei de tankikan ni kekka wo dasu koto mo gaikou no itte de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: That is true, but sometimes taking a hard stance to achieve results in a short time is also a diplomatic strategy, isn't it?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds by adding analytical depth about multilateral diplomacy. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer contradicts the message about the importance of gradual dialogue and patience.

conversation.speaker: 外交官(がいこうかん)・渡辺(わたなべ)

question.jepang: 最終的(さいしゅうてき)に、両国(りょうこく)の首脳会談(しゅのうかいだん)を経(へ)て、歴史的(れきしてき)な共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)が発表(はっぴょう)されました。この成果(せいか)が両国(りょうこく)関係(かんけい)の礎(いしずえ)となることを願(ねが)ってやみません。
question.romaji: Saishuteki ni, ryoukoku no shunou kaidan wo hete, rekishiteki na kyoudou seimei ga happyou saremashita. Kono seika ga ryoukoku kankei no ishizue to naru koto wo negatte yamimasen.
question.arti: Finally, through a summit between both nations, a historic joint statement has been announced. I ceaselessly hope that this achievement will become the foundation of relations between the two countries.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に画期的(かっきてき)な成果(せいか)です。この共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)を礎(いしずえ)に、各(かく)分野(ぶんや)での協力(きょうりょく)関係(かんけい)を着実(ちゃくじつ)に構築(こうちく)してまいりましょう。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni kakkiteki na seika desu. Kono kyoudou seimei wo ishizue ni, kaku bunya de no kyouryoku kankei wo chakujitsu ni kouchiku shite mairimasyou.
choices.arti: A truly revolutionary achievement. Let us use this joint statement as a foundation to gradually build cooperative relations across various fields.

choices.jepang: 共同(きょうどう)声明(せいめい)は所詮(しょせん)紙切(かみき)れに過(す)ぎません。重要(じゅうよう)なのは実際(じっさい)の行動(こうどう)です。
choices.romaji: Kyoudou seimei wa shosen kamikire ni sugimasen. Juuyou na no wa jissai no koudou desu.
choices.arti: A joint statement is ultimately just a piece of paper. What matters is concrete action.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the diplomatic achievement and directs toward concrete steps. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer is too cynical toward the long and complex diplomatic process.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・小林(こばやし)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 存在(そんざい)論的(ろんてき)な議論(ぎろん)ともなると、言語(げんご)そのものの限界(げんかい)と向(む)き合(あ)わざるを得(え)ません。
question.romaji: Sonzairon teki na giron to mo naru to, gengo sono mono no genkai to mukiawa zaruo emasen.
question.arti: When it comes to ontological debate, we cannot help but confront the limitations of language itself.

choices.jepang: まさに言語(げんご)の限界(げんかい)こそがウィトゲンシュタインの語(かた)りえないものの核心(かくしん)ですね。それゆえに沈黙(ちんもく)もまた哲学的(てつがくてき)言明(げんめい)たり得(う)るのです。
choices.romaji: Masa ni gengo no genkai koso ga Wittgenstein no katarienai mono no kakushin desu ne. Soreyueni chinmoku mo mata tetsugakuteki genmei tari uru no desu.
choices.arti: It is precisely the limitations of language that are the essence of Wittgenstein's 'what cannot be said.' Therefore, silence too can be a philosophical statement.

choices.jepang: 言語(げんご)の限界(げんかい)については、より精密(せいみつ)な語彙(ごい)を開発(かいはつ)することで克服(こくふく)できると考(かんが)えます。技術(ぎじゅつ)の進歩(しんぽ)がその解決(かいけつ)策(さく)を提供(ていきょう)してくれるでしょう。
choices.romaji: Gengo no genkai ni tsuite wa, yori seimitsu na goi wo kaihatsu suru koto de kokufuku dekiru to kangaemasu. Gijutsu no shinpo ga sono kaiketsu saku wo teikyou shite kureru deshou.
choices.arti: Regarding the limitations of language, I believe they can be overcome by developing more precise vocabulary. Advances in technology will provide the solution.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'limitations of language are the essence of Wittgenstein; silence too can be a philosophical statement' because it responds within an appropriate philosophical framework and substantively expands the discussion. Meanwhile, 'they can be overcome by developing more precise vocabulary; technology will provide the solution' treats the limitations of language as a technical problem -- this disregards the profound philosophical dimension of the ontological issue.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・木村(きむら)

question.jepang: デカルトは、あらゆる懐疑(かいぎ)をものともせず、コギト・エルゴ・スムという不動(ふどう)の真理(しんり)に到達(とうたつ)しました。
question.romaji: Dekaruto wa, arayuru kaigi wo mono to mo sezu, Kogito Erugo Sumu to iu fudou no shinri ni toutatsushimashita.
question.arti: Descartes, undeterred by all doubt, arrived at the unshakable truth of Cogito Ergo Sum.

choices.jepang: ただ、そのコギトの確立(かくりつ)は、思惟(しい)する主体(しゅたい)を前提(ぜんてい)とする点(てん)で循環(じゅんかん)論法(ろんぽう)の疑(うたが)いを払拭(ふっしょく)し切(き)れないという批判(ひはん)も根強(ねづよ)くございます。
choices.romaji: Tada, sono Kogito no kakuritsu wa, shiyu suru shutai wo zentei to suru ten de junkan ronpou no utagai wo fusshoku shikirenai to iu hihan mo nezuyoku gozaimasu.
choices.arti: However, there is a deep-rooted criticism that the establishment of the Cogito cannot fully dispel the suspicion of circular reasoning, as it presupposes a thinking subject.

choices.jepang: しかし、そのコギトの確実性(かくじつせい)自体(じたい)も後(のち)にフッサールやハイデガーによって問(と)い直(なお)され、主体(しゅたい)概念(がいねん)の根本的(こんぽんてき)な再考(さいこう)を迫(せま)られることになりました。
choices.romaji: Shikashi, sono Kogito no kakujitsusei jitai mo nochi ni Husserl ya Heidegger ni yotte toinaosare, shutai gainen no konponteki na saikou wo semerare ru koto ni narimashita.
choices.arti: However, the certainty of the Cogito itself was later re-questioned by Husserl and Heidegger, forcing a fundamental review of the concept of the subject.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the establishment of the Cogito cannot fully dispel the suspicion of circular reasoning' because it introduces an appropriate and specific philosophical critique -- the problem of circularity in the Cogito argument -- which elegantly continues the discussion. Meanwhile, 'the Cogito was later questioned by Husserl and Heidegger' although valid, jumps too far to another era without directly responding to the point about Descartes' success.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・小林(こばやし)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: ニーチェの文章(ぶんしょう)は、読者(どくしゃ)の価値観(かちかん)を根底(こんてい)から覆(くつがえ)さんばかりの破壊力(はかいりょく)を持(も)っておりますね。
question.romaji: Niiche no bunshoo wa, dokusha no kachikan wo kontei kara kutsusgaesen bakari no hakairyoku wo motte orimasu ne.
question.arti: Nietzsche's writings possess a destructive force as if they would shatter the reader's value system from its very roots.

choices.jepang: そうですね。ただ、ニーチェの思想(しそう)は過激(かげき)すぎるため、哲学(てつがく)的(てき)な厳密(げんみつ)さに欠(か)け、学術的(がくじゅつてき)な価値(かち)は低(ひく)いと言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Tada, Niiche no shisou wa kageki sugiru tame, tetsugakuteki na genmitsu sa ni kake, gakujutsuteki na kachi wa hikui to iwa zaruo emasen.
choices.arti: Indeed. However, Nietzsche's thought is too radical, lacks philosophical rigor, and cannot help but be deemed of low academic value.

choices.jepang: その破壊(はかい)こそが創造(そうぞう)への道(みち)だとニーチェは論(ろん)じましたね。偶像(ぐうぞう)の黄昏(たそがれ)における「哲学(てつがく)するとは鎚(つち)を手(て)に持(も)つことだ」という言葉(ことば)はその精髄(せいずい)でしょう。
choices.romaji: Sono hakai koso ga souzou he no michi da to Niiche wa ronjimashita ne. Guuzou no tasogare ni okeru 'tetsugaku suru to wa tsuchi wo te ni motsu koto da' to iu kotoba wa sono seizui deshou.
choices.arti: Nietzsche argued that it is precisely through destruction that the path to creation lies. The words in 'Twilight of the Idols' that 'to philosophize means to wield a hammer' are the essence of this.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'destruction is the path to creation; to philosophize means to wield a hammer' because it develops the idea within Nietzsche's own framework and references a specific work. Meanwhile, 'Nietzsche's thought is too radical, of low academic value' is gravely misguided -- Nietzsche is one of the most influential philosophers in history, and dismissing him demonstrates a fundamental misunderstanding.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・木村(きむら)

question.jepang: 絶対的(ぜったいてき)な真理(しんり)の確立(かくりつ)とはいかないまでも、実践的(じっせんてき)な生(せい)の指針(ししん)となりうる哲学(てつがく)こそが現代(げんだい)には求(もと)められているのではないでしょうか。
question.romaji: Zettaiteki na shinri no kakuritsu to wa ikanai made mo, jissenteki na sei no shishin to nari uru tetsugaku koso ga gendai ni wa motomerare te iru no de wa nai deshou ka.
question.arti: Even if we may not establish absolute truth, isn't it a philosophy that can serve as a practical guide for living that the modern era needs?

choices.jepang: 同感(どうかん)です。哲学(てつがく)が実用的(じつようてき)であるためには、難解(なんかい)な専門(せんもん)用語(ようご)を廃(はい)し、誰(だれ)でもわかりやすい言葉(ことば)で語(かた)れるよう単純化(たんじゅんか)すべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Doukan desu. Tetsugaku ga jitsuyouteki de aru tame ni wa, nankai na senmon yougo wo hai shi, dare demo wakari yasui kotoba de katareru you tanjunka subeki deshou.
choices.arti: I agree. For philosophy to be practical, difficult technical terminology should be eliminated and simplified so it can be expressed in language everyone understands.

choices.jepang: プラグマティズムの系譜(けいふ)に連(つら)なるご見解(けんかい)ですね。ジェームズやデューイが志向(しこう)した、生(い)きた経験(けいけん)に根差(ねざ)す哲学(てつがく)の重要性(じゅうようせい)を改(あらた)めて問(と)い直(なお)す必要(ひつよう)があるかもしれません。
choices.romaji: Puragumateizumu no keifu ni tsuranaru go kenkai desu ne. Jeemuzu ya Dyuui ga shikou shita, ikita keiken ni nezasu tetsugaku no juuyousei wo aratamete toinaosu hitsuyou ga aru kamo shiremasen.
choices.arti: That is a view that falls within the line of pragmatism. Perhaps we need to re-examine the importance of philosophy rooted in lived experience as advocated by James and Dewey.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'a view within the line of pragmatism; we need to re-examine philosophy rooted in lived experience' because it connects the idea with a substantively relevant philosophical tradition. Meanwhile, 'technical terminology should be eliminated and simplified' oversimplifies philosophy excessively -- eliminating technical terminology removes the depth needed for a meaningful guide to life.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・小林(こばやし)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: ハイデガーの存在(そんざい)と時間(じかん)は、西洋(せいよう)哲学(てつがく)の伝統(でんとう)を根本(こんぽん)から揺(ゆ)さぶらずにはおかない問題提起(もんだいていき)を含(ふく)んでいます。
question.romaji: Haidegaa no Sonzai to Jikan wa, seiyou tetsugaku no dentou wo konpon kara yusa burazu ni wa okana i mondai teiki wo fukunde imasu.
question.arti: Heidegger's 'Being and Time' contains problems that will undoubtedly shake the Western philosophical tradition from its roots.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かに、現存在(げんそんざい)概念(がいねん)による主体(しゅたい)の脱中心化(だつちゅうしんか)は、デカルト以来(いらい)の認識論(にんしきろん)的(てき)主体(しゅたい)の優位(ゆうい)を根底(こんてい)から問(と)い直(なお)すものでした。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni, genson zai gainen ni yoru shutai no datsu chuusin ka wa, Dekaruto irai no ninshikironteki shutai no yuui wo kontei kara toinaosu mono deshita.
choices.arti: Indeed, the de-centralization of the subject through the concept of Dasein re-questions from its roots the dominance of the epistemological subject since Descartes.

choices.jepang: その通(とお)りです。特(とく)に現存在(げんそんざい)の時間性(じかんせい)という概念(がいねん)は、永遠(えいえん)不変(ふへん)の真理(しんり)を追求(ついきゅう)してきた形而上学(けいじじょうがく)の根拠(こんきょ)そのものを問(と)いに付(ふ)すものでありましょう。
choices.romaji: Sono toori desu. Toku ni genson zai no jikansei to iu gainen wa, eien fuhen no shinri wo tsuikyuu shite kita keijijougaku no konkyo sono mono wo toi ni fusu mono de ari mashou.
choices.arti: Absolutely. In particular, the concept of Dasein's temporality questions the very foundation of metaphysics that has pursued eternal and unchanging truth.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Dasein's temporality questions the very foundation of metaphysics pursuing eternal truth' because it identifies the specific aspect that is most groundbreaking with deep and precise analysis. Meanwhile, 'de-centralization of the subject through Dasein questions epistemological dominance since Descartes' although valid, does not develop the argument further and stops at a commonly known assertion.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・木村(きむら)

question.jepang: 予期(よき)せぬ体調(たいちょう)不良(ふりょう)により、今回(こんかい)の国際(こくさい)哲学(てつがく)会議(かいぎ)での基調(きちょう)講演(こうえん)の辞退(じたい)を余儀(よぎ)なくされてしまいました。
question.romaji: Yoki senu taichou furyou ni yori, konkai no kokusai tetsugaku kaigi de no kichou kouen no jitai wo yogi naku sarete shimaimashita.
question.arti: Due to unexpected health conditions, I was forced to withdraw from the keynote speech at this international philosophy conference.

choices.jepang: それは致(いた)し方(かた)ないことです。体調(たいちょう)が優(すぐ)れないのであれば、今後(こんご)は学術(がくじゅつ)活動(かつどう)を一切(いっさい)控(ひか)えることをお勧(すす)めします。
choices.romaji: Sore wa itashikata nai koto desu. Taichou ga sugurenai no de areba, kongo wa gakujutsu katsudou wo issai hikaeru koto wo osusume shimasu.
choices.arti: That cannot be helped. If your health is poor, I would suggest discontinuing all academic activities going forward.

choices.jepang: それは誠(まこと)に残念(ざんねん)なことでございます。ただ、ご回復(かいふく)の後(のち)に論文(ろんぶん)として発表(はっぴょう)される形(かたち)で、そのご知見(ちけん)を広(ひろ)く世(よ)に問(と)うてはいかがでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Sore wa makoto ni zannen na koto de gozaimasu. Tada, go kaifuku no nochi ni ronbun to shite happyou sareru katachi de, sono go chiken wo hiroku yo ni toute wa ikaga deshou ka.
choices.arti: That is truly regrettable. However, after you recover, how about publishing those views as a paper to disseminate them to the world?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'after recovery, how about publishing those views as a paper' because it provides sincere sympathy along with a constructive suggestion that values the intellectual contribution. Meanwhile, 'discontinuing all academic activities going forward' is grossly disproportionate -- suggesting a complete stop only because of one illness is an entirely unempathetic response.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・小林(こばやし)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: 東洋(とうよう)哲学(てつがく)、とりわけ禅(ぜん)の思想(しそう)は、論理的(ろんりてき)分析(ぶんせき)をもってしてもなお捉(とら)えきれない直観的(ちょっかんてき)な真理(しんり)を指(さ)し示(しめ)していると言(い)えましょう。
question.romaji: Touyou tetsugaku, toriwake Zen no shisou wa, ronriteki bunseki wo motte shite mo nao torae kirenai chokkanteki na shinri wo sashi shimeshite iru to iemasyou.
question.arti: Eastern philosophy, particularly Zen thought, could be said to point toward intuitive truths that even logical analysis cannot fully capture.

choices.jepang: ただ、禅(ぜん)の思想(しそう)は学術的(がくじゅつてき)な厳密(げんみつ)さを欠(か)いておりますので、西洋(せいよう)哲学(てつがく)と同列(どうれつ)に論(ろん)じることは難(むずか)しいのではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Tada, Zen no shisou wa gakujutsuteki na genmitsu sa wo kaite orimasu no de, seiyou tetsugaku to douretsu ni ronjiru koto wa muzukashii no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: However, Zen thought lacks academic rigor, so it is difficult to discuss it on equal terms with Western philosophy, isn't it?

choices.jepang: 興味深(きょうみぶか)いご指摘(してき)です。西田(にしだ)幾多郎(きたろう)の「純粋(じゅんすい)経験(けいけん)」の概念(がいねん)は、まさに東西(とうざい)の哲学(てつがく)的(てき)伝統(でんとう)を架橋(かきょう)する試(こころ)みであったと評(ひょう)せましょう。
choices.romaji: Kyoumibuka i go shiteki desu. Nishida Kitarou no 'junsui keiken' no gainen wa, masani touzai no tetsugakuteki dentou wo kakyou suru kokoromi de atta to hyou semasyou.
choices.arti: A most fascinating point. Nishida Kitaro's concept of 'pure experience' can be evaluated as an attempt to bridge Eastern and Western philosophical traditions.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'Nishida Kitaro's concept of pure experience can be evaluated as an attempt to bridge Eastern and Western traditions' because it connects Zen thought with a Japanese philosopher who academically endeavored to bridge both traditions. Meanwhile, 'Zen thought lacks academic rigor' shows a bias toward Western traditions and disregards the philosophical depth of Zen that has been widely recognized.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・木村(きむら)

question.jepang: 現代(げんだい)の哲学者(てつがくしゃ)たるもの、抽象的(ちゅうしょうてき)な思弁(しべん)に終始(しゅうし)するのではなく、現実(げんじつ)社会(しゃかい)の諸問題(しょもんだい)にも積極的(せっきょくてき)に関与(かんよ)すべきではないでしょうか。
question.romaji: Gendai no tetsugakusha taru mono, chuushouteki na shiben ni shuushi suru no de wa naku, genjitsu shakai no shomondai ni mo sekkyokuteki ni kanyo subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
question.arti: As modern philosophers, shouldn't we not only engage in abstract speculation but also actively involve ourselves in various real-world social issues?

choices.jepang: サルトルが提唱(ていしょう)した「アンガジュマン」の精神(せいしん)ですね。哲学(てつがく)が象牙(ぞうげ)の塔(とう)に閉(と)じこもることは、知識人(ちしきじん)としての責任(せきにん)放棄(ほうき)に等(ひと)しいかもしれません。
choices.romaji: Sarutoru ga teishou shita 'angajuman' no seishin desu ne. Tetsugaku ga zouge no tou ni tojikomoru koto wa, chishikijin to shite no sekinin houki ni hitoshii kamo shiremasen.
choices.arti: That is the spirit of 'engagement' championed by Sartre. Philosophy that confines itself to an ivory tower may be tantamount to abandoning the responsibility of intellectuals.

choices.jepang: 哲学(てつがく)の本質(ほんしつ)は純粋(じゅんすい)な思索(しさく)にあります。社会(しゃかい)問題(もんだい)への関与(かんよ)は政治家(せいじか)やジャーナリストの仕事(しごと)であって、哲学者(てつがくしゃ)がそれに加(くわ)わる必要(ひつよう)はございません。
choices.romaji: Tetsugaku no honshitsu wa junsui na shisaku ni arimasu. Shakai mondai he no kanyo wa seijika ya jaanarisuto no shigoto de atte, tetsugakusha ga sore ni kuwawaru hitsuyou wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: The essence of philosophy lies in pure contemplation. Involvement in social issues is the work of politicians and journalists; philosophers need not participate.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'that is the spirit of engagement championed by Sartre; philosophy in an ivory tower may be abandoning intellectual responsibility' because it connects the argument to a relevant philosophical tradition. Meanwhile, 'philosophers need not participate in social issues' contradicts 「たるもの」 which emphasizes responsibility -- many great philosophers have been actively involved in social issues.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・小林(こばやし)教授(きょうじゅ)

question.jepang: AI時代(じだい)における意識(いしき)の問題(もんだい)は、哲学(てつがく)の根幹(こんかん)を揺(ゆ)るがさずにはおかないテーマとなっておりますね。機械(きかい)に意識(いしき)が宿(やど)り得(う)るか否(いな)か、これは哲学(てつがく)ならではの問(と)いです。
question.romaji: AI jidai ni okeru ishiki no mondai wa, tetsugaku no konkan wo yurugasazu ni wa okanai teema to natte orimasu ne. Kikai ni ishiki ga yadori uru ka ina ka, kore wa tetsugaku nara de wa no toi desu.
question.arti: The problem of consciousness in the AI era has become a theme that will undoubtedly shake the foundations of philosophy. Whether consciousness can emerge in machines is a quintessentially philosophical question.

choices.jepang: 意識(いしき)の問題(もんだい)は脳(のう)科学(かがく)の進歩(しんぽ)が解決(かいけつ)するでしょう。哲学(てつがく)がこの問題(もんだい)に関与(かんよ)する時代(じだい)は終(お)わりつつあると思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Ishiki no mondai wa noukagaku no shinpo ga kaiketsu suru deshou. Tetsugaku ga kono mondai ni kanyo suru jidai wa owaritsutsu aru to omoimasu.
choices.arti: The consciousness problem will be solved by advances in neuroscience. I believe the era when philosophy is involved in this issue is ending.

choices.jepang: チャーマーズの「ハードプロブレム」が示(しめ)すように、主観的(しゅかんてき)体験(たいけん)の本質(ほんしつ)は、いかなる物理的(ぶつりてき)説明(せつめい)をもってしても汲(く)み尽(つ)くしがたいものがございます。
choices.romaji: Chaamaazu no 'haado purobusemu' ga shimesu you ni, shukanteki taiken no honshitsu wa, ikanaru butsuri teki setsumei wo motte shite mo kumitsukushi gatai mono ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: As demonstrated by Chalmers' 'Hard Problem,' the essence of subjective experience possesses something that cannot be fully traced by any physical explanation.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'as demonstrated by Chalmers' Hard Problem, subjective experience cannot be fully traced by physical explanation' because it references a highly relevant contemporary philosophical concept. Meanwhile, 'the era when philosophy is involved in consciousness is ending' ignores the fact that advances in AI make philosophical questions about consciousness more pressing, not less relevant.

conversation.speaker: 哲学者(てつがくしゃ)・木村(きむら)

question.jepang: 本日(ほんじつ)の対話(たいわ)を通(つう)じて、哲学(てつがく)が人類(じんるい)の知的(ちてき)営(いとな)みにおいて果(は)たす役割(やくわり)の大(おお)きさを改(あらた)めて認識(にんしき)いたしました。哲学(てつがく)の灯(ひ)が消(き)えることなく受(う)け継(つ)がれることを願(ねが)ってやみません。
question.romaji: Honjitsu no taiwa wo tsuujite, tetsugaku ga jinrui no chiteki itonami ni oite hatasu yakuwari no ookisa wo aratamete ninshiki itashimashita. Tetsugaku no hi ga kieru koto naku uketsugareru koto wo negatte yamimasen.
question.arti: Through today's dialogue, I have once again realized the immense role of philosophy in the intellectual activities of humanity. I ceaselessly hope that the flame of philosophy continues to be passed on without being extinguished.

choices.jepang: そうですね。ただ、哲学(てつがく)は一部(いちぶ)の専門家(せんもんか)のためだけのものですから、一般(いっぱん)社会(しゃかい)への普及(ふきゅう)を意識(いしき)する必要(ひつよう)はないでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Tada, tetsugaku wa ichibu no senmonka no tame dake no mono desu kara, ippan shakai he no fukyuu wo ishiki suru hitsuyou wa nai deshou.
choices.arti: Indeed. However, since philosophy is only for a select few experts, there is no need to think about spreading it to the general public.

choices.jepang: 私(わたくし)もまた、本日(ほんじつ)の対話(たいわ)に深(ふか)い知的(ちてき)喜(よろこ)びを覚(おぼ)えました。ソクラテス以来(いらい)の対話(たいわ)の伝統(でんとう)こそが、哲学(てつがく)の生命(せいめい)線(せん)であり続(つづ)けるのでしょう。
choices.romaji: Watakushi mo mata, honjitsu no taiwa ni fukai chiteki yorokobi wo oboema shita. Sokuratesu irai no taiwa no dentou koso ga, tetsugaku no seimeisen de ari tsuzukeru no deshou.
choices.arti: I too felt profound intellectual joy from today's dialogue. The tradition of dialogue since Socrates will forever remain the lifeline of philosophy.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'the tradition of dialogue since Socrates will forever remain the lifeline of philosophy' because it responds to 「願ってやまない」 with a connection to the most fundamental philosophical tradition -- Socratic dialogue. Meanwhile, 'philosophy is only for a select few, no need to spread it' contradicts the spirit of the discussion emphasizing the importance of philosophy for life and society at large.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka Kyoju

question.jepang: 遺伝子(いでんし)工学(こうがく)ともなると、その倫理的(りんりてき)な問題(もんだい)は避(さ)けて通(とお)れないのではないでしょうか。
question.romaji: Idenshi kougaku to mo naru to, sono rinriteki na mondai wa sakete toorenai no dewa nai deshou ka.
question.arti: When it comes to genetic engineering, ethical issues are unavoidable, aren't they?

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通りです。遺伝子(いでんし)操作(そうさ)は人類(じんるい)の未来(みらい)を左右(さゆう)しかねない技術(ぎじゅつ)であり、慎重(しんちょう)な議論(ぎろん)を要(よう)します。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Idenshi sousa wa jinrui no mirai wo sayou shikanenai gijutsu de ari, shinchyou na giron wo you shimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Genetic manipulation is a technology that can determine the future of humanity, requiring careful discussion.

choices.jepang: ええ、しかし遺伝子(いでんし)工学(こうがく)の進歩(しんぽ)は止(と)められませんから、倫理(りんり)より利便性(りべんせい)を優先(ゆうせん)すべきでしょう。
choices.romaji: Ee, shikashi idenshi kougaku no shinpo wa tomeraremasen kara, rinri yori riben-sei wo yuusen subeki deshou.
choices.arti: Yes, but the advancement of genetic engineering cannot be stopped, so we should prioritize convenience over ethics.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'genetic manipulation can determine the future of humanity, requiring careful discussion' because it responds appropriately and seriously to the ethical issue. Meanwhile, 'we should prioritize convenience over ethics' disregards the ethical dimension that is the very core of the question about genetic engineering.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto Kenkyuuin

question.jepang: 確(たし)かに、ゲノム編集(へんしゅう)技術(ぎじゅつ)はあらゆる困難(こんなん)をものともせず急速(きゅうそく)に発展(はってん)してきました。しかし、その恩恵(おんけい)は全人類(ぜんじんるい)に等(ひと)しく行(い)き渡(わた)るのでしょうか。
question.romaji: Tashika ni, genoumu henshuu gijutsu wa arayuru konnan wo mono to mo sezu kyuusoku ni hatten shite kimashita. Shikashi, sono onkei wa zen jinrui ni hitoshiku ikiwataru no deshou ka.
question.arti: Indeed, genome editing technology has advanced rapidly without being deterred by any obstacles. However, will its benefits reach all of humanity equally?

choices.jepang: その懸念(けねん)はもっともです。技術(ぎじゅつ)へのアクセスの不平等(ふびょうどう)が倫理的(りんりてき)問題(もんだい)をさらに複雑(ふくざつ)にしているのは否定(ひてい)しがたい事実(じじつ)です。
choices.romaji: Sono kenen wa mottomo desu. Gijutsu he no akusesu no fubyoudou ga rinriteki mondai wo sara ni fukuzatsu ni shite iru no wa hitei shigatai jijitsu desu.
choices.arti: That concern is very reasonable. It is undeniable that inequality in access to technology further complicates ethical issues.

choices.jepang: 残念(ざんねん)ながら、現状(げんじょう)では先進国(せんしんこく)と途上国(とじょうこく)の格差(かくさ)が広(ひろ)がるばかりで、平等(びょうどう)な恩恵(おんけい)は望(のぞ)み薄(うす)いと言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。
choices.romaji: Zannen nagara, genjou dewa senshinkoku to tojou koku no kakusa ga hirogaru bakari de, byoudou na onkei wa nozomi usui to iwazaru wo emasen.
choices.arti: Unfortunately, given current conditions, the gap between developed and developing nations continues to widen, and we are forced to acknowledge that equal distribution of benefits is difficult to expect.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'inequality in access to technology further complicates ethical issues' because it builds a nuanced argument about the relationship between technology access and ethics. Meanwhile, 'equal distribution of benefits is difficult to expect' is too pessimistic and does not directly address the ethical aspect of the question.

conversation.speaker: Tanaka Kyoju

question.jepang: デザイナーベビーの問題(もんだい)にしても、親(おや)が子(こ)の遺伝子(いでんし)を選択(せんたく)できるとなると、優生思想(ゆうせいしそう)の再来(さいらい)を招(まね)きかねないと危惧(きぐ)されています。
question.romaji: Dezainaa bebi no mondai ni shite mo, oya ga ko no idenshi wo sentaku dekiru to naru to, yuusei shisou no sairan wo manekikanenai to kigu sarete imasu.
question.arti: Regarding designer babies as well, if parents can choose their children's genes, there are concerns about a revival of eugenic thinking.

choices.jepang: それは杞憂(きゆう)に過(す)ぎません。科学(かがく)の進歩(しんぽ)を歴史(れきし)的(てき)な過(あやま)ちと結(むす)びつけるのは短絡(たんらく)的(てき)な発想(はっそう)です。
choices.romaji: Sore wa kiyu ni sugimansen. Kagaku no shinpo wo rekishiteki na ayamachi to musubitsukeru no wa tanrakuteki na hasso desu.
choices.arti: That is merely an excessive concern. Connecting scientific progress with historical mistakes is overly narrow thinking.

choices.jepang: まさに。その懸念(けねん)を払拭(ふっしょく)するためにも、国際的(こくさいてき)な規制(きせい)の枠組(わくぐ)みを早急(そうきゅう)に整備(せいび)することが不可欠(ふかけつ)です。
choices.romaji: Masa ni. Sono kenen wo fusshoku suru tame ni mo, kokusaiteki na kisei no wakugumi wo soukyuu ni seibi suru koto ga fukaketsu desu.
choices.arti: Exactly right. Precisely to dispel such concerns, establishing an international regulatory framework as soon as possible is non-negotiable.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer is 'establishing an international regulatory framework as soon as possible is non-negotiable' because it provides a concrete solution to eugenic concerns. Meanwhile, 'that is merely excessive concern; connecting science with historical mistakes is narrow thinking' dismisses a historically valid concern without a strong basis for the argument.

conversation.speaker: Yamamoto Kenkyuuin

question.jepang: 研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)たるもの、科学的(かがくてき)な真理(しんり)の追究(ついきゅう)のみならず、その社会的(しゃかいてき)影響(えいきょう)に対(たい)する責任(せきにん)も負(お)わなければならないと考(かんが)えます。

question.jepang: 遺伝子(いでんし)治療(ちりょう)においては、体細胞(たいさいぼう)遺伝子(いでんし)治療(ちりょう)と生殖(せいしょく)細胞系列(さいぼうけいれつ)の編集(へんしゅう)を明確(めいかく)に区別(くべつ)すべきであり、後者(こうしゃ)についてはより慎重(しんちょう)な姿勢(しせい)が求(もと)められると考(かんが)えます。

choices.arti: Exactly right. To determine the balance between risk and benefit, the establishment of interdisciplinary ethics committees and continuous monitoring systems is essential.

question.romaji: Zaisei kinshuku seisaku wa, tankiteki na zaisei saiken ni wa kiyo suru mono no, keizai seicho wo sogai shi, kekka to shite shakaiteki jakusha ni fukinkou na futan wo shiiru keikou ga aru to shiteki sarete imasu.

choices.jepang: なるほど、ただし財政規律(ざいせいきりつ)の維持(いじ)なくして持続的(じぞくてき)な経済成長(けいざいせいちょう)はあり得(え)ませんので、多少(たしょう)の犠牲(ぎせい)はやむを得(え)ないものと思(おも)われます。
choices.romaji: Naruhodo, tadashi zaisei kiritsu no iji naku shite jizokuteki na keizai seicho wa ariemaseno de, tashou no gisei wa yamu wo enai mono to omowaremasu.
choices.arti: I see, but without maintaining fiscal discipline there can be no sustainable economic growth, so some sacrifice seems unavoidable.

choices.jepang: その分析(ぶんせき)は鋭(するど)いと存(ぞん)じます。ギリシャの事例(じれい)が如実(にょじつ)に示(しめ)すように、過度(かど)な緊縮(きんしゅく)は経済(けいざい)の悪循環(あくじゅんかん)を招(まね)き、社会的(しゃかいてき)安定(あんてい)をも損(そこ)なうことがあります。
choices.romaji: Sono bunseki wa surudoi to zonjimasu. Girisha no jirei ga nyojitsu ni shimesu you ni, kado na kinshuku wa keizai no akujunkan wo maneki, shakaiteki antei wo mo sokonau koto ga arimasu.
choices.arti: I believe that analysis is extremely incisive. As clearly demonstrated by the case of Greece, excessive austerity can trigger a vicious economic cycle and even undermine social stability.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports the argument with a concrete example. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer downplays the negative impact on vulnerable groups.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki Ekonomisuto

question.jepang: 量的緩和(りょうてきかんわ)政策(せいさく)は景気刺激(けいきしげき)にはなるものの、資産(しさん)格差(かくさ)の拡大(かくだい)をもたらさずにはおかない副作用(ふくさよう)があるとの批判(ひはん)も根強(ねづよ)くあります。
question.romaji: Ryouteki kanwa seisaku wa keiki shigeki ni wa naru mono no, shisan kakusa no kakudai wo motarasazu ni wa okanai fukusayou ga aru to no hihan mo nezuyoku arimasu.
question.arti: Quantitative easing policies certainly serve as economic stimulus, but the criticism that such policies inevitably widen the asset gap also remains strong.

choices.jepang: まさに看過(かんか)し難(がた)い問題(もんだい)です。政策(せいさく)の便益(べんえき)が広(ひろ)く社会全体(しゃかいぜんたい)に及(およ)ぶよう、再分配(さいぶんぱい)メカニズムとの連携(れんけい)が不可欠(ふかけつ)と言(い)えましょう。
choices.romaji: Masa ni kanka shi gatai mondai desu. Seisaku no beneki ga hiroku shakai zentai ni oyobu you, saibunpai mekanizumu to no renkei ga fukaketsu to iemasyou.
choices.arti: This is indeed an issue that cannot be ignored. It could be said that linkage with redistribution mechanisms is essential for the benefits of the policy to reach all of society broadly.

choices.jepang: その批判(ひはん)は一概(いちがい)に否定(ひてい)できません。低金利(ていきんり)環境(かんきょう)下(か)では、資産(しさん)を保有(ほゆう)する富裕層(ふゆうそう)がより大(おお)きな恩恵(おんけい)を受(う)ける構造(こうぞう)が固定化(こていか)される恐(おそ)れがあります。
choices.romaji: Sono hihan wa ichigai ni hitei dekimasen. Tei kinri kankyou-ka dewa, shisan wo hoyuu suru fuyuusou ga yori ooki na onkei wo ukeru kouzou ga koteika sareru osore ga arimasu.
choices.arti: That criticism cannot be simply dismissed. In a low interest rate environment, there are concerns that the structure where the wealthy who own assets benefit disproportionately will become further entrenched.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer offers a concrete solution through redistribution, matching 「ずにはおかない」. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer only describes the problem without a solution.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura Anarisuto

question.arti: When we think in accordance with existing economic policies, there is the reality that theoretical correctness and political feasibility do not always align.

choices.arti: I understand your point, but since political compromise inevitably degrades economic policy quality, economists should maintain theoretical purity.

choices.jepang: 鋭(するど)いご指摘(してき)です。経済学者(けいざいがくしゃ)たるもの、純粋(じゅんすい)な理論(りろん)の追究(ついきゅう)のみならず、現実(げんじつ)の制約(せいやく)の中(なか)で最善(さいぜん)の政策(せいさく)を模索(もさく)する姿勢(しせい)が求(もと)められます。

choices.arti: An incisive point. As an economist, the attitude of not merely pursuing pure theory but also seeking the best policy within the constraints of reality is required.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer balances theory and practice, matching 「たるもの」. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer contradicts the essence of the question emphasizing the need to reconcile theory with political reality.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki Ekonomisuto

question.jepang: デジタル通貨(つうか)の普及(ふきゅう)は、既存(きそん)の金融(きんゆう)システムに変革(へんかく)を余儀(よぎ)なくさせると同時(どうじ)に、プライバシーや監視社会(かんししゃかい)という新(あら)たな懸念(けねん)も生(う)み出(だ)しています。
question.romaji: Dejitaru tsuuka no fukyuu wa, kison no kin'yuu shisutemu ni henkaku wo yogi naku saseru to douji ni, puraibashii ya kanshi shakai to iu aratana kenen mo umidashite imasu.
question.arti: The spread of digital currencies forces a transformation of existing financial systems while simultaneously giving rise to new concerns about privacy and surveillance society.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に重要(じゅうよう)な視点(してん)です。技術革新(ぎじゅつかくしん)の恩恵(おんけい)を享受(きょうじゅ)しつつ、個人(こじん)の自由(じゆう)と権利(けんり)を守(まも)るための制度的(せいどてき)な枠組(わくぐ)みを並行(へいこう)して整備(せいび)することが肝要(かんよう)です。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni juuyou na shiten desu. Gijutsu kakushin no onkei wo kyouju shi tsutsu, kojin no jiyuu to kenri wo mamoru tame no seidoteki na wakugumi wo heikou shite seibi suru koto ga kanyou desu.
choices.arti: This is a truly important perspective. What is most important is simultaneously preparing institutional frameworks to protect individual freedoms and rights while enjoying the benefits of technological innovation.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。中央銀行(ちゅうおうぎんこう)デジタル通貨(つうか)の導入(どうにゅう)は効率性(こうりつせい)をもたらす一方(いっぽう)で、国家(こっか)による金融(きんゆう)監視(かんし)が強化(きょうか)される可能性(かのうせい)も排除(はいじょ)できません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Chuuou ginkou dejitaru tsuuka no dounyuu wa kouritsusei wo motarasu ippou de, kokka ni yoru kin'yuu kanshi ga kyouka sareru kanousei mo haijo dekimasen.
choices.arti: Absolutely. The introduction of central bank digital currencies brings efficiency, but the possibility of strengthened state financial surveillance also cannot be dismissed.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer offers a holistic solution, matching 「を余儀なくされる」. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer focuses too much on a single aspect without offering balance.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura Anarisuto

question.jepang: 総括(そうかつ)しますと、持続可能(じぞくかのう)な経済発展(けいざいはってん)のためには、成長(せいちょう)と公正(こうせい)、効率(こうりつ)と安定(あんてい)という一見(いっけん)相反(あいはん)する目標(もくひょう)を如何(いか)に調和(ちょうわ)させるかが、現代経済学(げんだいけいざいがく)の最大(さいだい)の課題(かだい)と言(い)えるでしょう。
question.romaji: Soukatsu shimasu to, jizoku kanou na keizai hatten no tame ni wa, seicho to kousei, kouritsu to antei to iu ikken aihan suru mokuhyou wo ika ni chyouwa saseru ka ga, gendai keizaigaku no saidai no kadai to ieru deshou.
question.arti: To summarize, the greatest challenge of the modern economy could be said to be how to harmonize seemingly contradictory goals -- growth and equity, efficiency and stability -- for sustainable economic development.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の通(とお)りです。しかし現実(げんじつ)には完全(かんぜん)な調和(ちょうわ)は不可能(ふかのう)ですから、どちらかを犠牲(ぎせい)にするトレードオフは避(さ)けられないと考(かんが)えます。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no toori desu. Shikashi genjitsu ni wa kanzen na chyouwa wa fukanou desu kara, dochira ka wo gisei ni suru toreedoofu wa sakerarenai to kangaemasu.
choices.arti: You are right. However, in reality, perfect harmony is impossible, so I believe trade-offs that sacrifice one are unavoidable.

choices.jepang: 卓見(たっけん)です。この緊張関係(きんちょうかんけい)を生産的(せいさんてき)に解消(かいしょう)していくことこそが、経済学者(けいざいがくしゃ)と政策立案者(せいさくりつあんしゃ)に課(か)せられた使命(しめい)と言(い)えましょう。
choices.romaji: Takken desu. Kono kinchou kankei wo seisanteki ni kaishou shite iku koto koso ga, keizaigakusha to seisaku ritsuansha ni kaserareta shimei to iemasyou.
choices.arti: A most insightful view. It could be said that this is the mission entrusted to economists and policymakers: to resolve these tensions productively.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer builds a constructive conclusion. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer is too pessimistic.

conversation.speaker: Suzuki Ekonomisuto

question.jepang: インフレーションの制御(せいぎょ)においては、中央銀行(ちゅうおうぎんこう)の独立性(どくりつせい)が損(そこ)なわれてはならないという原則(げんそく)は、いかなる政治的(せいじてき)圧力(あつりょく)をものともせず堅持(けんじ)されるべきです。
question.romaji: Infureeshon no seigyo ni oite wa, chuuou ginkou no dokuritsusei ga sokonawarete wa naranai to iu gensoku wa, ikanaru seijiteki atsuryoku wo mono to mo sezu kenji sareru beki desu.
question.arti: In inflation control, the principle that central bank independence must not be compromised should be maintained undeterred by any political pressure.

choices.jepang: 中央銀行(ちゅうおうぎんこう)の独立性(どくりつせい)は理想(りそう)ですが、民主的(みんしゅてき)な正統性(せいとうせい)を欠(か)くとの批判(ひはん)もあり、ある程度(ていど)の政治的(せいじてき)関与(かんよ)は避(さ)けられないでしょう。
choices.romaji: Chuuou ginkou no dokuritsusei wa risou desu ga, minshuteki na seitousei wo kaku to no hihan mo ari, aru teido no seijiteki kanyo wa sakerarenai deshou.
choices.arti: Central bank independence is ideal, but there is also criticism that it lacks democratic legitimacy, so a degree of political involvement is unavoidable.

choices.jepang: 全(まった)くその通(とお)りです。歴史的(れきしてき)に見(み)ても、政治(せいじ)が金融(きんゆう)政策(せいさく)に介入(かいにゅう)した結果(けっか)、ハイパーインフレを招(まね)いた事例(じれい)は枚挙(まいきょ)にいとまがありません。
choices.romaji: Mattaku sono toori desu. Rekishiteki ni mite mo, seiji ga kin'yuu seisaku ni kainyuu shita kekka, haipaainfure wo maneita jirei wa maikyo ni itoma ga arimasen.
choices.arti: Entirely correct. Historically as well, examples where political intervention in monetary policy triggered hyperinflation are too numerous to count.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer supports the principle of independence with historical evidence, matching 「をものともせず」. Meanwhile, the incorrect answer undermines a fundamental principle of inflation control.

conversation.speaker: Nakamura Anarisuto

question.jepang: グローバル・サプライチェーンの脆弱性(ぜいじゃくせい)が露呈(ろてい)した今(いま)、各国(かっこく)は経済安全保障(けいざいあんぜんほしょう)の観点(かんてん)から、サプライチェーンの再構築(さいこうちく)を余儀(よぎ)なくされています。
question.romaji: Guroobaruq sapurai cheen no zeijaku sei ga rotei shita ima, kakkoku wa keizai anzen hoshou no kanten kara, sapurai cheen no saikouchiku wo yogi naku sarete imasu.
question.arti: Now that the vulnerability of global supply chains has been exposed, each nation is forced to restructure supply chains from the perspective of economic security.

choices.jepang: サプライチェーンの問題(もんだい)は一時的(いちじてき)なものに過(す)ぎません。グローバル化(か)の流(なが)れは止(と)められませんから、従来(じゅうらい)の体制(たいせい)に戻(もど)るべきです。
choices.romaji: Sapurai cheen no mondai wa ichijiteki na mono ni sugimasen. Guroobaruka no nagare wa tomeraremasen kara, juurai no taisei ni modoru beki desu.
choices.arti: Supply chain issues are only temporary. The tide of globalization cannot be stopped, so we should return to the old system.
