question.arti: When it comes to this special edition, the quality of submitted papers is far higher, necessitating the application of even stricter peer review standards.

choices.jepang: そうですね。特集号(とくしゅうごう)だからこそ、査読(さどく)の基準(きじゅん)を少(すこ)し緩(ゆる)めて多(おお)くの論文(ろんぶん)を掲載(けいさい)すべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Sou desu ne. Tokushuu gou da kara koso, sadoku no kijun wo sukoshi yurumete ooku no ronbun wo keisai subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: That's true. Precisely because it is a special edition, shouldn't review standards be somewhat relaxed to publish more papers?

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。学術(がくじゅつ)誌(し)の信頼性(しんらいせい)を維持(いじ)すべく、査読(さどく)プロセスには一切(いっさい)の妥協(だきょう)があってはなりません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Gakujutsu shi no shinraisei wo iji subeku, sadoku purosesu ni wa issai no dakyou ga atte wa narimasen.
choices.arti: Absolutely. To maintain the credibility of the academic journal, there must be absolutely no compromise in the peer review process.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses the pattern 「～べく」 (in order to) showing determination to maintain high standards, matching 「ともなると」 which emphasizes the significant consequences of a special edition. The incorrect answer proposes relaxing standards, contradicting the strict context presented.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: この論文(ろんぶん)の著者(ちょしゃ)は、先行(せんこう)研究(けんきゅう)の批判(ひはん)をものともせず、独自(どくじ)の理論(りろん)を展開(てんかい)しておりますが、方法論(ほうほうろん)に疑義(ぎぎ)がございます。
question.romaji: Kono ronbun no chosha wa, senkou kenkyuu no hihan wo mono to mo sezu, dokuji no riron wo tenkai shite orimasu ga, houhouron ni gigi ga gozaimasu.
question.arti: The author of this paper developed an original theory undeterred by criticism of prior research, but there are doubts about the methodology.

choices.jepang: 独創性(どくそうせい)は評価(ひょうか)に値(あたい)しますが、方法論(ほうほうろん)の厳密(げんみつ)さなくして、いかなる理論(りろん)も学術的(がくじゅつてき)妥当性(だとうせい)を持(も)ち得(え)ません。修正(しゅうせい)を求(もと)める所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Dokusousei wa hyouka ni atai shimasu ga, houhouron no genmitsusa naku shite, ikanaru riron mo gakujutsuteki datousei wo mochi emasen. Shuusei wo motomeru shozon desu.
choices.arti: Originality is commendable, but without methodological rigor, no theory can possess academic validity. I intend to request a revision.

choices.jepang: 独創的(どくそうてき)な理論(りろん)であれば、方法論(ほうほうろん)の問題(もんだい)は些細(ささい)なことですので、そのまま掲載(けいさい)を承認(しょうにん)してよいかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Dokusouteki na riron de areba, houhouron no mondai wa sasai na koto desu no de, sono mama keisai wo shounin shite yoi ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: If the theory is original, methodological issues are trivial, so I believe it can be directly approved for publication.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～なくして」 (without) and 「～所存です」 (intend to) showing that methodology is an inseparable foundation of academic validity. The incorrect answer ignores the importance of methodology, a fundamental principle of peer review.

conversation.speaker: 山本(やまもと)編集長(へんしゅうちょう)

question.jepang: 査読者(さどくしゃ)ともあろう方(かた)が、利益(りえき)相反(そうはん)を申告(しんこく)せずに審査(しんさ)に臨(のぞ)むとは、学術(がくじゅつ)倫理(りんり)に反(はん)する行為(こうい)と言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。
question.romaji: Sadokusha to mo arou kata ga, rieki souhan wo shinkoku sezu ni shinsa ni nozomu to wa, gakujutsu rinri ni han suru koui to iwazaru wo emasen.
question.arti: A person who is supposed to be a reviewer failed to report a conflict of interest and proceeded with the evaluation; that can only be called a violation of academic ethics.

choices.jepang: 利益(りえき)相反(そうはん)の有無(うむ)は個人(こじん)の判断(はんだん)に委(ゆだ)ねればよいのではないでしょうか。過度(かど)な規制(きせい)は査読(さどく)の効率(こうりつ)を妨(さまた)げます。
choices.romaji: Rieki souhan no umu wa kojin no handan ni yudanereba yoi no de wa nai deshou ka. Kado na kisei wa sadoku no kouritsu wo samatagemasu.
choices.arti: Shouldn't the presence or absence of a conflict of interest be left to personal judgment? Excessive regulation actually hinders peer review efficiency.

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に遺憾(いかん)極(きわ)まりない事態(じたい)です。当該(とうがい)査読(さどく)結果(けっか)を無効(むこう)とし、新(あら)たな審査員(しんさいん)を選任(せんにん)すべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni ikan kiwamarinai jitai desu. Tougai sadoku kekka wo mukou to shi, arata na shinsain wo sennin subeki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: This is a situation that is truly deeply regrettable. I believe the review results should be invalidated and a new reviewer appointed.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～極まりない」 (extremely) to express the seriousness of the ethical violation, matching 「ともあろう方が」 which emphasizes that someone in such a respected position should not have done this. The incorrect answer trivializes the importance of conflict of interest declarations.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の投稿(とうこう)論文(ろんぶん)には、実験(じっけん)データの再現性(さいげんせい)に疑問(ぎもん)を禁(きん)じ得(え)ない箇所(かしょ)がいくつか見受(みう)けられました。
question.romaji: Konkai no toukou ronbun ni wa, jikken deeta no saigensei ni gimon wo kin ji enai kasho ga ikutsuka miukeraremashita.
question.arti: In the paper submitted this time, there are several sections that inevitably raise doubts about the reproducibility of the experimental data.

choices.jepang: データの再現性(さいげんせい)に即(そく)して検証(けんしょう)を行(おこな)い、著者(ちょしゃ)に追加(ついか)実験(じっけん)の実施(じっし)を要請(ようせい)するのが妥当(だとう)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Deeta no saigensei ni soku shite kenshou wo okonai, chosha ni tsuika jikken no jisshi wo yousei suru no ga datou ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I believe verification based on data reproducibility should be conducted, and the author should be asked to perform additional experiments.

choices.jepang: 再現性(さいげんせい)の問題(もんだい)は分野(ぶんや)によって異(こと)なりますので、著者(ちょしゃ)の主張(しゅちょう)をそのまま受(う)け入(い)れてもよいのではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Saigensei no mondai wa bunya ni yotte kotonarimasu no de, chosha no shuchou wo sono mama ukeirete mo yoi no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Since reproducibility issues vary by field, wouldn't it be acceptable to take the author's claims at face value?

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～に即して」 (based on) showing a rigorous scientific approach to data doubts, aligned with 「～を禁じ得ない」 expressing unavoidable doubt. The incorrect answer is too lenient in accepting claims without verification.

conversation.speaker: 山本(やまもと)編集長(へんしゅうちょう)

question.jepang: この研究(けんきゅう)分野(ぶんや)の発展(はってん)は、厳格(げんかく)な査読(さどく)制度(せいど)なくしては成(な)し遂(と)げられなかったと言(い)っても過言(かごん)ではありません。
question.romaji: Kono kenkyuu bunya no hatten wa, genkaku na sadoku seido naku shite wa nashi togerarenakatta to itte mo kagon de wa arimasen.
question.arti: It is no exaggeration to say that the development of this research field could not have been achieved without a rigorous peer review system.

choices.jepang: 査読(さどく)制度(せいど)は形式的(けいしきてき)なものに過(す)ぎず、研究(けんきゅう)の発展(はってん)は研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)個人(こじん)の努力(どりょく)によるものにほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Sadoku seido wa keishikiteki na mono ni sugi zu, kenkyuu no hatten wa kenkyuusha kojin no doryoku ni yoru mono ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: The peer review system is merely a formality; research development is nothing other than the result of individual researchers' efforts.

choices.jepang: まさにその通(とお)りです。査読(さどく)を通(つう)じた建設的(けんせつてき)な批評(ひひょう)こそが、学問(がくもん)の質(しつ)を担保(たんぽ)する唯一(ゆいいつ)の仕組(しく)みと申(もう)せましょう。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sono toori desu. Sadoku wo tsuujita kensetsuteki na hihyou koso ga, gakumon no shitsu wo tanpo suru yuiitsu no shikumi to mousemasyou.
choices.arti: That is indeed true. Constructive criticism through peer review is the sole mechanism that guarantees academic quality.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer aligns with 「なくしては成し遂げられなかった」 emphasizing the crucial role of peer review. The incorrect answer misuses 「にほかならない」 to diminish peer review's role, when the context actually extols it.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: 一秒(いちびょう)たりとも査読(さどく)の締切(しめきり)を延長(えんちょう)することは認(みと)められないという編集(へんしゅう)方針(ほうしん)は、いささか厳(きび)しすぎるのではないでしょうか。
question.romaji: Ichi byou tari to mo sadoku no shimekiri wo enchou suru koto wa mitomerarenai to iu henshuu houshin wa, isasaka kibishisugiru no de wa nai deshou ka.
question.arti: An editorial policy that does not allow even a second's extension of the peer review deadline -- isn't that somewhat too strict?

choices.jepang: ご懸念(けねん)はごもっともです。ただし、学術(がくじゅつ)出版(しゅっぱん)の適時性(てきじせい)を確保(かくほ)するには、締切(しめきり)の厳守(げんしゅ)は不可避(ふかひ)でございます。
choices.romaji: Go kenen wa gomottomo desu. Tadashi, gakujutsu shuppan no tekijisei wo kakuho suru ni wa, shimekiri no genshu wa fukahi de gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Your concern is very reasonable. However, to ensure timely academic publication, adherence to deadlines is unavoidable.

choices.jepang: 確(たし)かに厳(きび)しいですね。査読者(さどくしゃ)の負担(ふたん)を考(かんが)えれば、締切(しめきり)は自由(じゆう)に設定(せってい)できるようにすべきです。
choices.romaji: Tashika ni kibishii desu ne. Sadokusha no futan wo kangaereba, shimekiri wa jiyuu ni settei dekiru you ni subeki desu.
choices.arti: It is indeed strict. Considering the burden on reviewers, deadlines should be freely adjustable.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer wisely addresses the doubt about 「たりとも」 (not even... a single), acknowledging the concern while explaining the rational reason behind the strict policy. The incorrect answer is too lax and ignores the importance of time discipline in academic publication.

conversation.speaker: 山本(やまもと)編集長(へんしゅうちょう)

question.jepang: 当(とう)ジャーナルならではの二重(にじゅう)盲検(もうけん)査読(さどく)方式(ほうしき)は、公正性(こうせいせい)の観点(かんてん)から他誌(たし)の模範(もはん)となっております。
question.romaji: Tou jaanaru nara de wa no nijuu mouken sadoku houshiki wa, kouseisei no kanten kara tashi no mohan to natte orimasu.
question.arti: The double-blind peer review system that is the hallmark of our journal has become a model for other journals from the perspective of fairness.

choices.jepang: 二重(にじゅう)盲検(もうけん)方式(ほうしき)は時代遅(じだいおく)れであり、むしろオープンレビューに移行(いこう)すべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Nijuu mouken houshiki wa jidai okure de ari, mushiro oopun rebyuu ni ikou subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: The double-blind system is outdated; shouldn't we switch to open review?

choices.jepang: その公正(こうせい)な制度(せいど)があればこそ、投稿者(とうこうしゃ)からの信頼(しんらい)も厚(あつ)く、優秀(ゆうしゅう)な論文(ろんぶん)が集(あつ)まるのでしょう。
choices.romaji: Sono kousei na seido ga areba koso, toukousha kara no shinrai mo atsuku, yuushuu na ronbun ga atsumaru no deshou.
choices.arti: Precisely because of that fair system, trust from contributors is high and quality papers keep coming in.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the uniqueness of 「ならではの」 of the journal's system by explaining its positive impact. The incorrect answer rejects the system without considering its proven advantages.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: 査読者(さどくしゃ)たるもの、個人的(こじんてき)な偏見(へんけん)を排(はい)し、純粋(じゅんすい)に学術的(がくじゅつてき)見地(けんち)から論文(ろんぶん)を評価(ひょうか)せねばなりません。
question.romaji: Sadokusha taru mono, kojinteki na henken wo hai shi, junsui ni gakujutsuteki kenchi kara ronbun wo hyouka seneba narimasen.
question.arti: As a true reviewer, one must set aside personal biases and evaluate papers purely from an academic perspective.

choices.jepang: 完全(かんぜん)な客観性(きゃっかんせい)を維持(いじ)することは現実的(げんじつてき)に不可能(ふかのう)ですので、ある程度(ていど)の主観(しゅかん)は許容(きょよう)されるべきです。
choices.romaji: Kanzen na kyakkansei wo iji suru koto wa genjitsuteki ni fukanou desu no de, aru teido no shukan wa kyoyou sareru beki desu.
choices.arti: Since maintaining perfect objectivity is realistically impossible, a certain degree of subjectivity should be tolerated.

choices.jepang: まさにその通(とお)りです。学術(がくじゅつ)の公正性(こうせいせい)は、査読者(さどくしゃ)一人(ひとり)一人(ひとり)の誠実(せいじつ)さをおいて他(ほか)に担保(たんぽ)する術(すべ)はございません。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sono toori desu. Gakujutsu no kouseisei wa, sadokusha hitori hitori no seijitsusa wo oite hoka ni tanpo suru sube wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: That is indeed true. There is no other way to guarantee academic fairness than through the honesty of each individual reviewer.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～をおいて他に～ない」 (there is no other than) to emphasize the central role of reviewer integrity, matching 「たるもの」 which affirms a reviewer's fundamental responsibility. The incorrect answer weakens the objectivity standard, a core principle of peer review.

conversation.speaker: 山本(やまもと)編集長(へんしゅうちょう)

question.jepang: この論文(ろんぶん)の結論(けつろん)は、従来(じゅうらい)の通説(つうせつ)からすれば極(きわ)めて挑戦的(ちょうせんてき)であり、学界(がっかい)に波紋(はもん)を広(ひろ)げずにはおかないでしょう。
question.romaji: Kono ronbun no ketsuron wa, juurai no tsuusetsu kara sureba kiwamete chousenteki de ari, gakkai ni hamon wo hirogeru zu ni wa okanai deshou.
question.arti: The conclusions of this paper, viewed from conventional theory, are highly challenging and will undoubtedly create ripples in the academic world.

choices.jepang: 挑戦的(ちょうせんてき)な結論(けつろん)ほど慎重(しんちょう)な検証(けんしょう)が求(もと)められます。追加(ついか)データの提出(ていしゅつ)を条件(じょうけん)に掲載(けいさい)を承認(しょうにん)するのが賢明(けんめい)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Chousenteki na ketsuron hodo shinchou na kenshou ga motomeraremasu. Tsuika deeta no teishutsu wo jouken ni keisai wo shounin suru no ga kenmei ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: The more challenging the conclusions, the more careful the verification required. I believe it would be prudent to approve publication on condition that additional data is submitted.

choices.jepang: 挑戦的(ちょうせんてき)な論文(ろんぶん)は読者(どくしゃ)の興味(きょうみ)を引(ひ)きますので、検証(けんしょう)なしでもすぐに掲載(けいさい)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Chousenteki na ronbun wa dokusha no kyoumi wo hikimasu no de, kenshou nashi de mo sugu ni keisai subeki desu.
choices.arti: Challenging papers attract reader interest, so they should be published immediately even without verification.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「からすれば」 (viewed from the perspective of) and 「ずにはおかない」 (will undoubtedly) with an appropriate scientific attitude: acknowledging potential impact while emphasizing the need for further verification. The incorrect answer prioritizes popularity over scientific rigor.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: 剽窃(ひょうせつ)は学術(がくじゅつ)の世界(せかい)において最(もっと)もあるまじき行為(こうい)であり、いかなる弁明(べんめい)も許(ゆる)されるべきではございません。
question.romaji: Hyousetsu wa gakujutsu no sekai ni oite mottomo aru majiki koui de ari, ikanaru benmei mo yurusareru beki de wa gozaimasen.
question.arti: Plagiarism is the most impermissible act in academia, and no defense of any kind should be allowed.

choices.jepang: 場合(ばあい)によっては意図(いと)せぬ類似(るいじ)もございますので、一概(いちがい)に剽窃(ひょうせつ)と断(だん)ずるのは早計(そうけい)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Baai ni yotte wa ito senu ruiji mo gozaimasu no de, ichigai ni hyousetsu to dan zuru no wa soukei de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Depending on the case, there are also unintentional similarities, so isn't it premature to immediately conclude it is plagiarism?

choices.jepang: 全(まった)く同感(どうかん)です。学術的(がくじゅつてき)誠実(せいじつ)さを損(そこ)なう行為(こうい)は、研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)としての資格(しかく)を問(と)われるべき重大(じゅうだい)な違反(いはん)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Mattaku doukan desu. Gakujutsuteki seijitsusa wo sokonau koui wa, kenkyuusha to shite no shikaku wo towareru beki juudai na ihan ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: I fully agree. An act that damages academic integrity is nothing other than a serious violation that calls into question one's qualifications as a researcher.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (is nothing other than) to affirm that plagiarism is a serious violation, aligned with 「あるまじき」 (must never occur) expressing absolute prohibition. The incorrect answer attempts to soften the seriousness of plagiarism.

conversation.speaker: 山本(やまもと)編集長(へんしゅうちょう)

question.jepang: 査読(さどく)プロセスの透明性(とうめいせい)を高(たか)めることなしに、学術(がくじゅつ)出版(しゅっぱん)への社会的(しゃかいてき)信頼(しんらい)を回復(かいふく)することはできないと考(かんが)えております。
question.romaji: Sadoku purosesu no touumeisei wo takameru koto nashi ni, gakujutsu shuppan e no shakaiteki shinrai wo kaifuku suru koto wa dekinai to kangaete orimasu.
question.arti: I believe that without enhancing the transparency of the peer review process, it is impossible to restore public trust in academic publishing.

choices.jepang: 透明性(とうめいせい)を高(たか)めるとかえって査読者(さどくしゃ)が萎縮(いしゅく)し、率直(そっちょく)な批評(ひひょう)ができなくなるのではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Touumeisei wo takameru to kaette sadokusha ga ishuku shi, socchoku na hihyou ga dekinaku naru no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: If transparency is enhanced, reviewers will feel constrained and unable to provide frank criticism, won't they?

choices.jepang: ご慧眼(けいがん)に敬服(けいふく)いたします。段階的(だんかいてき)にオープンレビューの要素(ようそ)を導入(どうにゅう)し、透明性(とうめいせい)と匿名性(とくめいせい)の均衡(きんこう)を図(はか)る所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Go keigan ni keifuku itashimasu. Dankaiteki ni oopun rebyuu no youso wo dounyuu shi, touumeisei to tokumeise no kinkou wo hakaru shozon desu.
choices.arti: I admire your incisive perspective. I intend to gradually introduce elements of open review and seek a balance between transparency and anonymity.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「ことなしに～できない」 (without... cannot) with a concrete solution using 「所存です」 (intend to), showing commitment to balanced improvement of transparency. The incorrect answer only highlights risks without offering a solution.

conversation.speaker: 佐藤(さとう)審査員(しんさいん)

question.jepang: 若手(わかて)研究者(けんきゅうしゃ)の論文(ろんぶん)であれ、ベテランの論文(ろんぶん)であれ、査読(さどく)基準(きじゅん)は一律(いちりつ)に適用(てきよう)されるべきものと心得(こころえ)ております。
question.romaji: Wakate kenkyuusha no ronbun de are, beteran no ronbun de are, sadoku kijun wa ichiritsu ni tekiyou sareru beki mono to kokoroete orimasu.
question.arti: Whether papers by young researchers or papers by senior researchers, I understand that peer review standards must be applied uniformly.

choices.jepang: それは理想(りそう)ですが、現実(げんじつ)には大御所(おおごしょ)の論文(ろんぶん)に厳(きび)しい指摘(してき)をするのは難(むずか)しいものです。
choices.romaji: Sore wa risou desu ga, genjitsu ni wa oogosho no ronbun ni kibishii shiteki wo suru no wa muzukashii mono desu.
choices.arti: That is the ideal, but in reality it is difficult to give harsh criticism to papers by prominent figures.

choices.jepang: その姿勢(しせい)こそが学術(がくじゅつ)の公正性(こうせいせい)を支(ささ)える礎(いしずえ)です。著者(ちょしゃ)の経歴(けいれき)に左右(さゆう)されない審査(しんさ)こそ、我々(われわれ)の使命(しめい)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Sono shisei koso ga gakujutsu no kouseisei wo sasaeru ishizue desu. Chosha no keireki ni sayuu sarenai shinsa koso, wareware no shimei ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: That attitude is the very foundation supporting academic fairness. Evaluation uninfluenced by an author's track record is nothing other than our mission.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (is nothing other than) to affirm the mission of fairness, matching 「であれ～であれ」 (whether... or) emphasizing equal treatment. The incorrect answer acknowledges unfairness in practice without attempting to correct it.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の多国間(たこくかん)貿易(ぼうえき)交渉(こうしょう)ともなると、各国(かっこく)の利害(りがい)が複雑(ふくざつ)に絡(から)み合(あ)い、合意(ごうい)形成(けいせい)は容易(ようい)ではございません。
question.romaji: Konkai no takokukan boueki koushou to mo naru to, kakkoku no rigai ga fukuzatsu ni karami ai, goui keisei wa youi de wa gozaimasen.
question.arti: When it comes to this multilateral trade negotiation, the interests of each country are intricately interconnected, and building consensus is no easy task.

choices.jepang: 各国(かっこく)の利害(りがい)は大(おお)して変(か)わりませんので、前回(ぜんかい)と同(おな)じ方式(ほうしき)で進(すす)めればよいかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Kakkoku no rigai wa taishite kawarimasen no de, zenkai to onaji houshiki de susumereba yoi ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: The interests of each country are not very different, so I believe we can proceed in the same way as before.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。だからこそ、互(たが)いの譲歩(じょうほ)なくして合意(ごうい)には至(いた)り得(え)ないという認識(にんしき)のもと、粘(ねば)り強(づよ)い交渉(こうしょう)に臨(のぞ)む所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Dakara koso, tagai no jouho naku shite goui ni wa itari enai to iu ninshiki no moto, nebari zuyoi koushou ni nozomu shozon desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Precisely because of that, with the awareness that consensus cannot be reached without mutual compromise, I intend to face negotiations with persistence.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer combines 「なくして～ない」 (without... cannot) and 「所存です」 (intend to) showing deep understanding of multilateral negotiation complexity, matching 「ともなると」. The incorrect answer oversimplifies an inherently complex situation.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 貴国(きこく)は関税(かんぜい)障壁(しょうへき)をものともせず、積極的(せっきょくてき)に市場(しじょう)開放(かいほう)を推進(すいしん)してこられましたが、今回(こんかい)は農産物(のうさんぶつ)分野(ぶんや)でも同様(どうよう)のご姿勢(しせい)でしょうか。
question.romaji: Kikoku wa kanzei shouheki wo mono to mo sezu, sekkyokuteki ni shijou kaihou wo suishin shite koraremashita ga, konkai wa nousanbutsu bunya de mo douyou no go shisei deshou ka.
question.arti: Your country has actively promoted market opening undeterred by tariff barriers. Will you take the same stance on agricultural products this time?

choices.jepang: 農産物(のうさんぶつ)分野(ぶんや)に関(かん)しましては、国内(こくない)産業(さんぎょう)の保護(ほご)と市場(しじょう)開放(かいほう)の均衡(きんこう)を図(はか)るべく、段階的(だんかいてき)な自由化(じゆうか)を提案(ていあん)する所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Nousanbutsu bunya ni kan shimashite wa, kokunai sangyou no hogo to shijou kaihou no kinkou wo hakaru beku, dankaiteki na jiyuuka wo teian suru shozon desu.
choices.arti: Regarding agricultural products, in order to seek a balance between domestic industry protection and market opening, I intend to propose phased liberalization.

choices.jepang: 農産物(のうさんぶつ)は国民(こくみん)生活(せいかつ)に直結(ちょっけつ)しますので、一切(いっさい)の譲歩(じょうほ)は認(みと)められません。この件(けん)は交渉(こうしょう)の対象外(たいしょうがい)です。
choices.romaji: Nousanbutsu wa kokumin seikatsu ni chokketsu shimasu no de, issai no jouho wa mitomeraremasen. Kono ken wa koushou no taishougai desu.
choices.arti: Agricultural products directly affect people's livelihoods, so no compromise is acceptable. This is outside the scope of negotiations.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 (in order to) showing a balanced diplomatic approach to 「をものともせず」, offering a phased solution. The incorrect answer is too rigid and refuses negotiation entirely, contradicting the spirit of trade diplomacy.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 新興(しんこう)国(こく)の急速(きゅうそく)な経済(けいざい)成長(せいちょう)は、既存(きそん)の貿易(ぼうえき)秩序(ちつじょ)を根底(こんてい)から覆(くつがえ)さずにはおかない勢(いきお)いでございます。
question.romaji: Shinkou koku no kyuusoku na keizai seichou wa, kison no boueki chitsujo wo kontei kara kutsugaesu zu ni wa okanai ikioi de gozaimasu.
question.arti: The rapid economic growth of developing nations has momentum that will undoubtedly transform the existing trade order from its foundations.

choices.jepang: 新興(しんこう)国(こく)の成長(せいちょう)は一時的(いちじてき)なものに過(す)ぎませんので、既存(きそん)の枠組(わくぐ)みを変更(へんこう)する必要(ひつよう)はないかと思(おも)われます。
choices.romaji: Shinkou koku no seichou wa ichijiteki na mono ni sugimasen no de, kison no wakugumi wo henkou suru hitsuyou wa nai ka to omowaremasu.
choices.arti: Growth in developing nations is only temporary, so I believe there is no need to change the existing framework.

choices.jepang: その通(とお)りです。既存(きそん)の枠組(わくぐ)みに固執(こしつ)することなく、新(あら)たな国際(こくさい)経済(けいざい)秩序(ちつじょ)の構築(こうちく)に向(む)けて柔軟(じゅうなん)に対応(たいおう)すべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Sono toori desu. Kison no wakugumi ni koshitsu suru koto naku, arata na kokusai keizai chitsujo no kouchiku ni mukete juunan ni taiou subeki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Indeed so. Without being fixated on the existing framework, I believe we must respond flexibly toward building a new international economic order.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「ずにはおかない」 (will undoubtedly) with an adaptive and visionary attitude, acknowledging that change is inevitable. The incorrect answer underestimates significant global trends and clings too much to the status quo.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 知的(ちてき)財産権(ざいさんけん)の保護(ほご)に関(かん)しましては、先進国(せんしんこく)からすれば当然(とうぜん)の要求(ようきゅう)でしょうが、途上国(とじょうこく)にとっては技術(ぎじゅつ)移転(いてん)の障壁(しょうへき)となりかねません。
question.romaji: Chiteki zaisanken no hogo ni kan shimashite wa, senshinkoku kara sureba touzen no youkyuu deshou ga, tojoukoku ni totte wa gijutsu iten no shouheki to narikane masen.
question.arti: Regarding intellectual property protection, from the perspective of developed nations it is certainly a reasonable demand, but for developing nations it can become an obstacle to technology transfer.

choices.jepang: 知的(ちてき)財産権(ざいさんけん)は絶対的(ぜったいてき)なものであり、途上国(とじょうこく)の事情(じじょう)を考慮(こうりょ)する余地(よち)はございません。
choices.romaji: Chiteki zaisanken wa zettaiteki na mono de ari, tojoukoku no jijou wo kouryo suru yochi wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: Intellectual property rights are absolute; there is no room to consider the circumstances of developing nations.

choices.jepang: 双方(そうほう)の立場(たちば)を尊重(そんちょう)し、技術(ぎじゅつ)移転(いてん)の促進(そくしん)と知的(ちてき)財産権(ざいさんけん)の保護(ほご)を両立(りょうりつ)させる枠組(わくぐ)みを模索(もさく)すべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Souhou no tachiba wo sonchou shi, gijutsu iten no sokushin to chiteki zaisanken no hogo wo ryouritsu saseru wakugumi wo mosaku subeki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I believe we must respect the positions of both sides and seek a framework that can balance the promotion of technology transfer with intellectual property protection.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to the 「からすれば」 (from the perspective of) with a diplomatic attitude seeking balanced interests. The incorrect answer is too absolutist and ignores the reality of the gap between developed and developing nations.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 自由(じゆう)貿易(ぼうえき)協定(きょうてい)の締結(ていけつ)は、両国(りょうこく)の経済(けいざい)発展(はってん)に資(し)するものにほかならず、一刻(いっこく)も早(はや)い合意(ごうい)が望(のぞ)まれます。
question.romaji: Jiyuu boueki kyoutei no teiketsu wa, ryoukoku no keizai hatten ni shi suru mono ni hoka narazu, ikkoku mo hayai goui ga nozomaremasu.
question.arti: The signing of a free trade agreement is nothing other than something that contributes to the economic development of both nations, and the earliest possible consensus is strongly desired.

choices.jepang: ご高説(こうせつ)ごもっともです。ただし、拙速(せっそく)な合意(ごうい)はかえって国益(こくえき)を損(そこ)ないかねませんので、慎重(しんちょう)かつ迅速(じんそく)に交渉(こうしょう)を進(すす)めたく存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Go kousetsu gomottomo desu. Tadashi, sessoku na goui wa kaette kokueki wo sokonai kanemasen no de, shinchou katsu jinsoku ni koushou wo susumetaku zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Your view is very reasonable. However, a hasty consensus could actually harm national interests, so I wish to advance negotiations both carefully and swiftly.

choices.jepang: 自由(じゆう)貿易(ぼうえき)協定(きょうてい)は大国(たいこく)にのみ有利(ゆうり)ですので、我(わ)が国(くに)としては参加(さんか)を見送(みおく)るべきかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Jiyuu boueki kyoutei wa taikoku ni nomi yuuri desu no de, waga kuni to shite wa sanka wo miokuru beki ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Free trade agreements only benefit large nations, so I believe our country should not participate.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer accepts the premise of 「にほかならず」 (nothing other than) but adds a nuance of prudent caution in the negotiation process. The incorrect answer rejects the basic premise without strong grounds and departs from the spirit of negotiation.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 環境(かんきょう)基準(きじゅん)の統一(とういつ)は、持続(じぞく)可能(かのう)な貿易(ぼうえき)を実現(じつげん)するうえで避(さ)けて通(とお)れない課題(かだい)であり、一日(いちにち)たりとも対応(たいおう)を遅(おく)らせてはなりません。
question.romaji: Kankyou kijun no touitsu wa, jizoku kanou na boueki wo jitsugen suru ue de sakete toorenai kadai de ari, ichi nichi tari to mo taiou wo okurasete wa narimasen.
question.arti: The unification of environmental standards is an unavoidable issue in realizing sustainable trade, and addressing it must not be delayed even a single day.

choices.jepang: 環境(かんきょう)基準(きじゅん)は各国(かっこく)の主権(しゅけん)に関(かか)わる問題(もんだい)ですので、統一(とういつ)は非現実的(ひげんじつてき)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Kankyou kijun wa kakkoku no shuken ni kakawaru mondai desu no de, touitsu wa higenjitsuteki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Environmental standards are a matter of each nation's sovereignty, so unification is unrealistic in my view.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の緊急性(きんきゅうせい)は十分(じゅうぶん)に認識(にんしき)しております。各国(かっこく)の発展(はってん)段階(だんかい)に即(そく)した柔軟(じゅうなん)な基準(きじゅん)設定(せってい)を提案(ていあん)いたします。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no kinkyuusei wa juubun ni ninshiki shite orimasu. Kakkoku no hatten dankai ni soku shita juunan na kijun settei wo teian itashimasu.
choices.arti: I am fully aware of the urgency you have conveyed. I propose setting flexible standards tailored to the development stage of each country.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～に即した」 (tailored to) to respond to the urgency of 「たりとも～てはならない」 (must not even a single) with a practical solution considering the realities of various nations. The incorrect answer refuses unification efforts without offering an alternative.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 多国間(たこくかん)交渉(こうしょう)ならではの難(むずか)しさは、二国間(にこくかん)交渉(こうしょう)とは比較(ひかく)にならないほど複雑(ふくざつ)であり、各(かく)代表団(だいひょうだん)の忍耐(にんたい)が試(ため)されます。
question.romaji: Takokukan koushou nara de wa no muzukashisa wa, nikokukan koushou to wa hikaku ni naranai hodo fukuzatsu de ari, kaku daihyoudan no nintai ga tamesaremasu.
question.arti: The difficulties unique to multilateral negotiations are far more complex than bilateral ones, and the patience of every delegation is tested.

choices.jepang: 多国間(たこくかん)交渉(こうしょう)は非効率(ひこうりつ)ですので、二国間(にこくかん)交渉(こうしょう)に切(き)り替(か)えた方(ほう)がよいのではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Takokukan koushou wa hikouritsu desu no de, nikokukan koushou ni kirikaeta hou ga yoi no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Multilateral negotiations are inefficient; wouldn't it be better to switch to bilateral negotiations?

choices.jepang: まさにそうです。しかしながら、多国間(たこくかん)の枠組(わくぐ)みでこそ達成(たっせい)し得(う)る包括的(ほうかつてき)な合意(ごうい)がございます。困難(こんなん)をおそれず、粘(ねば)り強(づよ)く臨(のぞ)みましょう。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sou desu. Shikashi nagara, takokukan no wakugumi de koso tassei shi uru houkatsuteki na goui ga gozaimasu. Konnan wo osorezu, nebari zuyoku nozomimashou.
choices.arti: That is indeed true. However, there are comprehensive consensuses achievable only within a multilateral framework. Let us face the difficulties fearlessly and with persistence.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer acknowledges the uniqueness of 「ならではの」 multilateral negotiations and sees them as an opportunity to achieve what cannot be achieved bilaterally. The incorrect answer gives up on complexity without seeing its advantages.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 交渉官(こうしょうかん)たるもの、自国(じこく)の利益(りえき)を最大化(さいだいか)しつつも、国際(こくさい)社会(しゃかい)全体(ぜんたい)の繁栄(はんえい)を見据(みす)えた視野(しや)を持(も)たねばなりません。
question.romaji: Koushoukan taru mono, jikoku no rieki wo saidaika shitsutsu mo, kokusai shakai zentai no han'ei wo misue ta shiya wo motaneba narimasen.
question.arti: As a true negotiator, one must possess a far-sighted vision for the prosperity of the entire international community while maximizing one's own national interests.

choices.jepang: 理想(りそう)は理解(りかい)できますが、現実(げんじつ)の交渉(こうしょう)では自国(じこく)の利益(りえき)を最優先(さいゆうせん)にすべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Risou wa rikai dekimasu ga, genjitsu no koushou de wa jikoku no rieki wo saiyuusen ni subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: The ideal can be understood, but in actual negotiations, shouldn't national interests be prioritized?

choices.jepang: 全(まった)くその通(とお)りです。短期的(たんきてき)な国益(こくえき)に固執(こしつ)するあまり、長期的(ちょうきてき)な国際(こくさい)協調(きょうちょう)を損(そこ)なうことは、結局(けっきょく)は自国(じこく)の利益(りえき)をも毀損(きそん)することにほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Mattaku sono toori desu. Tankiteki na kokueki ni koshitsu suru amari, choukiteki na kokusai kyouchou wo sokonau koto wa, kekkyoku wa jikoku no rieki wo mo kison suru koto ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Clinging too much to short-term national interests to the point of damaging long-term international cooperation is ultimately nothing other than harming one's own national interests as well.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (nothing other than) to emphasize that short-term egoism actually harms oneself, matching 「たるもの」 demanding broad vision from a negotiator. The incorrect answer is too narrow and contradicts the spirit of multilateral diplomacy.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 今次(こんじ)の交渉(こうしょう)における最大(さいだい)の争点(そうてん)は、デジタル貿易(ぼうえき)のルール策定(さくてい)をおいて他(ほか)にないと言(い)えましょう。
question.romaji: Konji no koushou ni okeru saidai no souten wa, dejitaru boueki no ruuru sakutei wo oite hoka ni nai to iemasyou.
question.arti: It could be said that the most critical issue in this negotiation is none other than the formulation of digital trade rules.

choices.jepang: デジタル貿易(ぼうえき)は新(あたら)しい分野(ぶんや)ですので、ルール策定(さくてい)は時期尚早(じきしょうそう)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Dejitaru boueki wa atarashii bunya desu no de, ruuru sakutei wa jiki shousou de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Digital trade is still a new field; isn't rule formulation still premature?

choices.jepang: 同感(どうかん)でございます。データの越境(えっきょう)移転(いてん)とプライバシー保護(ほご)の両立(りょうりつ)こそが、今次(こんじ)交渉(こうしょう)の成否(せいひ)を左右(さゆう)する鍵(かぎ)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Doukan de gozaimasu. Deeta no ekkyou iten to puraibashii hogo no ryouritsu koso ga, konji koushou no seihi wo sayuu suru kagi ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I agree. I believe the balance between cross-border data transfer and privacy protection is the key that determines the success of this negotiation.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「をおいて他にない」 (none other than) by identifying the specific key issue in digital trade. The incorrect answer underestimates the urgency of an issue already declared as the top priority.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の合意(ごうい)文書(ぶんしょ)の草案(そうあん)には、各国(かっこく)の感情(かんじょう)を刺激(しげき)せずにはおかない表現(ひょうげん)がいくつか含(ふく)まれております。
question.romaji: Konkai no goui bunsho no souan ni wa, kakkoku no kanjou wo shigeki sezu ni wa okanai hyougen ga ikutsuka fukumarete orimasu.
question.arti: In the current draft agreement document, there are several expressions that will undoubtedly touch the sensitivities of each country.

choices.jepang: 文言(もんごん)の修正(しゅうせい)に関(かん)しましては、各国(かっこく)の立場(たちば)に即(そく)した表現(ひょうげん)に改(あらた)めるべく、文言(もんごん)検討(けんとう)小委員会(しょういいんかい)の設置(せっち)を提案(ていあん)いたします。
choices.romaji: Mongon no shuusei ni kan shimashite wa, kakkoku no tachiba ni soku shita hyougen ni aratameru beku, mongon kentou shou iinkai no secchi wo teian itashimasu.
choices.arti: Regarding wording revisions, I propose forming a wording review sub-committee to modify expressions in accordance with each country's position.

choices.jepang: 文言(もんごん)の問題(もんだい)は些末(さまつ)なことですので、内容(ないよう)さえ合意(ごうい)できれば、表現(ひょうげん)はそのままでよいかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Mongon no mondai wa samatsu na koto desu no de, naiyou sae goui dekireba, hyougen wa sono mama de yoi ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Wording issues are trivial; as long as the content can be agreed upon, the expressions can be left as they are.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～に即した」 (in accordance with) and 「～べく」 (in order to) showing diplomatic sensitivity toward 「ずにはおかない」. In diplomacy, the nuance of wording is critically important. The incorrect answer underestimates the importance of diplomatic language.

conversation.speaker: 中村(なかむら)大使(たいし)

question.jepang: 大使(たいし)ともあろう方(かた)が、非公式(ひこうしき)の場(ば)で相手国(あいてこく)の通商(つうしょう)政策(せいさく)を公然(こうぜん)と批判(ひはん)されたとは、外交儀礼(がいこうぎれい)に反(はん)すると言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。
question.romaji: Taishi to mo arou kata ga, hikoushiki no ba de aitekoku no tsuushou seisaku wo kouzen to hihan sareta to wa, gaikou girei ni han suru to iwazaru wo emasen.
question.arti: A person who is supposed to be an ambassador openly criticized another country's trade policies at an informal forum; that can only be called a violation of diplomatic etiquette.

choices.jepang: 非公式(ひこうしき)の場(ば)ですので、ある程度(ていど)の率直(そっちょく)な意見(いけん)交換(こうかん)は許容(きょよう)されるべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Hikoushiki no ba desu no de, aru teido no socchoku na iken koukan wa kyoyou sareru beki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Since it was an informal forum, shouldn't frank exchanges of opinion to a certain extent be tolerated?

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に遺憾(いかん)極(きわ)まりない事態(じたい)です。公式(こうしき)・非公式(ひこうしき)を問(と)わず、外交(がいこう)の場(ば)における言動(げんどう)には細心(さいしん)の注意(ちゅうい)を払(はら)うべきです。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni ikan kiwamarinai jitai desu. Koushiki hikoushiki wo towazu, gaikou no ba ni okeru gendou ni wa saishin no chuui wo harau beki desu.
choices.arti: This is a situation that is truly deeply regrettable. Whether at formal or informal forums, full attention must be given to every action in the diplomatic arena.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「極まりない」 (extremely) to express the seriousness of the diplomatic etiquette violation, matching 「ともあろう方が」 emphasizing the respected position that should maintain composure. The incorrect answer downplays the diplomatic violation.

conversation.speaker: 李(り)首席(しゅせき)交渉官(こうしょうかん)

question.jepang: 相互(そうご)の信頼(しんらい)関係(かんけい)なくして、いかなる貿易(ぼうえき)協定(きょうてい)も持続的(じぞくてき)な効力(こうりょく)を持(も)ち得(え)ないことは、歴史(れきし)が証明(しょうめい)しております。
question.romaji: Sougo no shinrai kankei naku shite, ikanaru boueki kyoutei mo jizokuteki na kouryoku wo mochi enai koto wa, rekishi ga shoumei shite orimasu.
question.arti: That without mutual trust, no trade agreement can have sustainable binding force -- this has been proven by history.

choices.jepang: 信頼(しんらい)関係(かんけい)よりも法的(ほうてき)拘束力(こうそくりょく)の方(ほう)が重要(じゅうよう)です。契約(けいやく)さえしっかりしていれば問題(もんだい)ありません。
choices.romaji: Shinrai kankei yori mo houteki kousokuryoku no hou ga juuyou desu. Keiyaku sae shikkari shite ireba mondai arimasen.
choices.arti: Legal binding force is more important than mutual trust. As long as the contract is strong, there is no problem.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。本(ほん)交渉(こうしょう)を通(つう)じて築(きず)かれる信頼(しんらい)こそが、協定(きょうてい)の実効性(じっこうせい)を担保(たんぽ)する最(もっと)も重要(じゅうよう)な基盤(きばん)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Hon koushou wo tsuujite kizukareru shinrai koso ga, kyoutei no jikkousei wo tanpo suru mottomo juuyou na kiban ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: Absolutely. The trust built through these negotiations is nothing other than the most important foundation guaranteeing the effectiveness of the agreement.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (nothing other than) to affirm the importance of trust, matching 「なくして～得ない」 (without... cannot) as the premise of the argument. The incorrect answer relies too much on legal mechanisms and ignores the human dimension of diplomacy.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)改正(かいせい)の議論(ぎろん)ともなると、国民(こくみん)一人(ひとり)一人(ひとり)の基本的(きほんてき)人権(じんけん)に関(かか)わる問題(もんだい)であり、極(きわ)めて慎重(しんちょう)な審議(しんぎ)が求(もと)められます。
question.romaji: Kenpou kaisei no giron to mo naru to, kokumin hitori hitori no kihonteki jinken ni kakawaru mondai de ari, kiwamete shinchou na shingi ga motomeraremasu.
question.arti: When it comes to discussing constitutional amendment, this is a matter concerning the fundamental rights of every citizen, requiring extremely careful deliberation.

choices.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)改正(かいせい)は時代(じだい)の要請(ようせい)ですので、迅速(じんそく)に進(すす)めるべきではないでしょうか。国民的(こくみんてき)議論(ぎろん)は後(あと)からでも構(かま)いません。
choices.romaji: Kenpou kaisei wa jidai no yousei desu no de, jinsoku ni susumeru beki de wa nai deshou ka. Kokuminteki giron wa ato kara de mo kamaimasen.
choices.arti: Constitutional amendment is a demand of the times; shouldn't it be carried out quickly? National discussion can come later.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。立憲主義(りっけんしゅぎ)の根幹(こんかん)に関(かか)わる以上(いじょう)、拙速(せっそく)な改正(かいせい)は断(だん)じて避(さ)けねばなりません。国民的(こくみんてき)合意(ごうい)の形成(けいせい)こそが最優先(さいゆうせん)です。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Rikkenshugi no konkan ni kakawaru ijou, sessoku na kaisei wa dan jite sakeneba narimasen. Kokuminteki goui no keisei koso ga saiyuusen desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Since it concerns the foundations of constitutionalism, hasty amendment must be absolutely avoided. Building national consensus is the top priority.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「ともなると」 appropriately, acknowledging the serious weight of constitutional amendment and emphasizing the need for caution and national consensus. The incorrect answer disregards the fundamental principle of deliberative democracy in constitutional amendment.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 反対(はんたい)派(は)の猛烈(もうれつ)な批判(ひはん)をものともせず、改正(かいせい)案(あん)の上程(じょうてい)を推進(すいしん)する覚悟(かくご)でございますが、先生(せんせい)のご見解(けんかい)をお聞(き)かせください。
question.romaji: Hantai ha no mouretsu na hihan wo mono to mo sezu, kaisei an no joutei wo suishin suru kakugo de gozaimasu ga, sensei no go kenkai wo okikase kudasai.
question.arti: I am prepared to push forward the amendment bill undeterred by harsh criticism from the opposition. Please share your views.

choices.jepang: 反対(はんたい)意見(いけん)を無視(むし)して改正(かいせい)を進(すす)めることは、民主主義(みんしゅしゅぎ)の原則(げんそく)に反(はん)する行為(こうい)と言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。少数(しょうすう)意見(いけん)の尊重(そんちょう)なくして、真(しん)の民主的(みんしゅてき)合意(ごうい)は成(な)り立(た)ちません。
choices.romaji: Hantai iken wo mushi shite kaisei wo susumeru koto wa, minshu shugi no gensoku ni han suru koui to iwazaru wo emasen. Shousuuu iken no sonchou naku shite, shin no minshuteki goui wa nari tachimasen.
choices.arti: Ignoring opposition views and pressing ahead with amendment can only be called an act violating democratic principles. Without respecting minority opinions, true democratic consensus will not be achieved.

choices.jepang: 多数決(たすうけつ)で決(き)まるものですから、反対(はんたい)派(は)の意見(いけん)にこだわる必要(ひつよう)はないかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Tasuu ketsu de kimaru mono desu kara, hantai ha no iken ni kodawaru hitsuyou wa nai ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Since it is decided by majority vote, I believe there is no need to be fixated on the opposition's views.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「なくして～ない」 (without... cannot) to emphasize the importance of dialogue with the opposition. Although the question uses 「をものともせず」 positively, the wise answer reminds of democratic principles. The incorrect answer reduces democracy to mere majoritarianism.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)提案(ていあん)されている改正(かいせい)条項(じょうこう)は、表現(ひょうげん)の自由(じゆう)を制限(せいげん)しかねず、国民(こくみん)の危機感(ききかん)を煽(あお)らずにはおかない内容(ないよう)です。
question.romaji: Konkai teian sarete iru kaisei joukou wa, hyougen no jiyuu wo seigen shikanezu, kokumin no kikikan wo aorazu ni wa okanai naiyou desu.
question.arti: The proposed amendment article has the potential to restrict freedom of expression, and its content will undoubtedly provoke a sense of crisis among the citizenry.

choices.jepang: 表現(ひょうげん)の自由(じゆう)は公共(こうきょう)の秩序(ちつじょ)のために制限(せいげん)されるべきものですので、特(とく)に問題(もんだい)はないかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Hyougen no jiyuu wa koukyou no chitsujo no tame ni seigen sareru beki mono desu no de, toku ni mondai wa nai ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: Freedom of expression should indeed be restricted for public order, so I believe there is no particular problem.

choices.jepang: 極(きわ)めて憂慮(ゆうりょ)すべき事態(じたい)です。基本的(きほんてき)人権(じんけん)の制約(せいやく)は、必要(ひつよう)最小限(さいしょうげん)の範囲(はんい)に留(とど)めるべきであり、この条項(じょうこう)は比例(ひれい)原則(げんそく)に即(そく)して再検討(さいけんとう)されるべきです。
choices.romaji: Kiwamete yuuryo subeki jitai desu. Kihonteki jinken no seiyaku wa, hitsuyou saishlougen no han'i ni todomeru beki de ari, kono joukou wa hirei gensoku ni soku shite saikentou sareru beki desu.
choices.arti: This is an extremely concerning situation. Restrictions on fundamental rights must be limited to the minimum possible scope, and this article must be reviewed based on the principle of proportionality.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～に即して」 (based on) the principle of proportionality to respond to the concern of 「ずにはおかない」 about restricting freedom. The incorrect answer too readily accepts restrictions on fundamental rights without proportionality considerations.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 国会(こっかい)議員(ぎいん)ともあろう者(もの)が、憲法(けんぽう)の基本(きほん)原理(げんり)を理解(りかい)せずに改正(かいせい)案(あん)に賛成(さんせい)するとは、まさにあるまじき行為(こうい)です。
question.romaji: Kokkai giin to mo arou mono ga, kenpou no kihon genri wo rikai sezu ni kaisei an ni sansei suru to wa, masa ni aru majiki koui desu.
question.arti: A person who is supposed to be a member of parliament approved the amendment bill without understanding the basic principles of the constitution; that is truly an act that should never occur.

choices.jepang: 政治(せいじ)の世界(せかい)では党(とう)の方針(ほうしん)に従(したが)うのが当然(とうぜん)ですので、個人(こじん)の理解(りかい)は二(に)の次(つぎ)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Seiji no sekai de wa tou no houshin ni shitagau no ga touzen desu no de, kojin no rikai wa ni no tsugi de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: In the world of politics, following party policy is normal, so isn't personal understanding of secondary importance?

choices.jepang: 全(まった)く同感(どうかん)です。国民(こくみん)の付託(ふたく)を受(う)けた議員(ぎいん)たるもの、憲法(けんぽう)の精神(せいしん)を深(ふか)く理解(りかい)したうえで、良心(りょうしん)に基(もと)づいた判断(はんだん)をなすべきです。
choices.romaji: Mattaku doukan desu. Kokumin no futaku wo uketa giin taru mono, kenpou no seishin wo fukaku rikai shita ue de, ryoushin ni motozuita handan wo nasu beki desu.
choices.arti: I fully agree. As a member of parliament entrusted with a mandate from the people, one must make decisions based on conscience after deeply understanding the spirit of the constitution.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「たるもの」 (as one who is) to affirm the moral responsibility of parliamentarians, matching 「ともあろう者が」 and 「あるまじき」 expressing disappointment at the violation of ethical standards. The incorrect answer prioritizes party loyalty over constitutional responsibility.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)の平和(へいわ)主義(しゅぎ)条項(じょうこう)は、戦後(せんご)日本(にほん)の国際的(こくさいてき)信頼(しんらい)の礎(いしずえ)にほかならず、安易(あんい)な改正(かいせい)は国際(こくさい)社会(しゃかい)からの信頼(しんらい)を損(そこ)ないかねません。
question.romaji: Kenpou no heiwa shugi joukou wa, sengo nihon no kokusaiteki shinrai no ishizue ni hoka narazu, an'i na kaisei wa kokusai shakai kara no shinrai wo sokonaikane masen.
question.arti: The pacifism article of the constitution is nothing other than the foundation of international trust in postwar Japan, and reckless amendment could damage trust from the international community.

choices.jepang: 国際(こくさい)情勢(じょうせい)の変化(へんか)に鑑(かんが)みれば、平和(へいわ)主義(しゅぎ)条項(じょうこう)はもはや時代遅(じだいおく)れであり、全面的(ぜんめんてき)に見直(みなお)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Kokusai jousei no henka ni kangamireba, heiwa shugi joukou wa mohaya jidai okure de ari, zenmenteki ni minaosu beki desu.
choices.arti: Given the changing international situation, the pacifism article is outdated and should be comprehensively reviewed.

choices.jepang: ご慧眼(けいがん)の通(とお)りです。平和(へいわ)主義(しゅぎ)の理念(りねん)を堅持(けんじ)しつつ、現実(げんじつ)の安全(あんぜん)保障(ほしょう)環境(かんきょう)にも対応(たいおう)し得(う)る解釈(かいしゃく)の幅(はば)を模索(もさく)すべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Go keigan no toori desu. Heiwa shugi no rinen wo kenji shitsutsu, genjitsu no anzen hoshou kankyou ni mo taiou shi uru kaishaku no haba wo mosaku subeki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: As your incisive view suggests. I believe that while maintaining the pacifist principle, we must seek room for interpretation that can also respond to the realities of the security environment.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer accepts the premise of 「にほかならず」 about the importance of pacifism and offers a nuanced approach balancing principles with reality. The incorrect answer is too radical in wanting to eliminate a well-established constitutional foundation.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)改正(かいせい)の国民(こくみん)投票(とうひょう)においては、一票(いっぴょう)たりとも軽視(けいし)されてはならず、全(すべ)ての有権者(ゆうけんしゃ)に十分(じゅうぶん)な情報(じょうほう)が提供(ていきょう)されるべきです。
question.romaji: Kenpou kaisei no kokumin touhyou ni oite wa, ippyou tari to mo keishi sarete wa narazu, subete no yuukensha ni juubun na jouhou ga teikyou sareru beki desu.
question.arti: In a constitutional amendment referendum, not a single vote must be underestimated, and adequate information must be provided to all voters.

choices.jepang: 国民(こくみん)投票(とうひょう)は形式的(けいしきてき)なものですので、情報(じょうほう)提供(ていきょう)にそれほど労力(ろうりょく)をかける必要(ひつよう)はないかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Kokumin touhyou wa keishikiteki na mono desu no de, jouhou teikyou ni sorehodo rouryoku wo kakeru hitsuyou wa nai ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: A referendum is merely a formality, so I believe there is no need to devote much effort to information provision.

choices.jepang: まさにその通(とお)りです。主権者(しゅけんしゃ)たる国民(こくみん)が十分(じゅうぶん)な判断(はんだん)材料(ざいりょう)を得(え)られるよう、賛否(さんぴ)双方(そうほう)の論点(ろんてん)を公平(こうへい)に周知(しゅうち)する所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sono toori desu. Shukensha taru kokumin ga juubun na handan zairyou wo erareru you, sanpi souhou no ronten wo kouhei ni shuuchi suru shozon desu.
choices.arti: That is indeed true. I intend to disseminate pro and con arguments fairly so that the people, as holders of sovereignty, receive adequate materials for consideration.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「たる」 (as) and 「所存です」 to show commitment to the principle of 「たりとも～てはならない」 about the importance of every vote. The incorrect answer trivializes the fundamental democratic process of a constitutional referendum.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 日本国(にほんこく)憲法(けんぽう)ならではの平和(へいわ)的(てき)生存権(せいぞんけん)の概念(がいねん)は、世界(せかい)の憲法(けんぽう)学(がく)においても類(るい)を見(み)ない独自(どくじ)の理念(りねん)でございます。
question.romaji: Nihon koku kenpou nara de wa no heiwateki seizonken no gainen wa, sekai no kenpou gaku ni oite mo rui wo minai dokuji no rinen de gozaimasu.
question.arti: The concept of the right to peaceful living, a hallmark of the Japanese Constitution, is a unique principle unparalleled in constitutional studies worldwide.

choices.jepang: 独自(どくじ)の理念(りねん)であるからこそ、国際(こくさい)標準(ひょうじゅん)に合(あ)わせて修正(しゅうせい)すべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Dokuji no rinen de aru kara koso, kokusai hyoujun ni awasete shuusei subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Precisely because it is unique, shouldn't it be revised to align with international standards?

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。その独自性(どくじせい)こそが日本(にほん)の国際的(こくさいてき)アイデンティティを形作(かたちづく)り、平和(へいわ)構築(こうちく)における日本(にほん)の発言力(はつげんりょく)の源泉(げんせん)となっております。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Sono dokujisei koso ga nihon no kokusaiteki aidentiti wo katachizukuri, heiwa kouchiku ni okeru nihon no hatsugenryoku no gensen to natte orimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. That uniqueness shapes Japan's international identity and serves as a source of Japan's influence in peacebuilding.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the 「ならではの」 (unique to) aspect by explaining its strategic value for Japan's international position. The incorrect answer seeks to eliminate a uniqueness that is actually a strength.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)たるもの、政治的(せいじてき)中立性(ちゅうりつせい)を保(たも)ち、純粋(じゅんすい)に学術的(がくじゅつてき)見地(けんち)から憲法(けんぽう)解釈(かいしゃく)を論(ろん)ずべきではないでしょうか。
question.romaji: Kenpou gakusha taru mono, seijiteki chuuritsusei wo tamochi, junsui ni gakujutsuteki kenchi kara kenpou kaishaku wo ron zubeki de wa nai deshou ka.
question.arti: As a true constitutional law scholar, shouldn't one maintain political neutrality and discuss constitutional interpretation purely from an academic perspective?

choices.jepang: 学者(がくしゃ)も一市民(いちしみん)ですので、政治的(せいじてき)立場(たちば)を明確(めいかく)にして発言(はつげん)する自由(じゆう)がございます。
choices.romaji: Gakusha mo ichi shimin desu no de, seijiteki tachiba wo meikaku ni shite hatsugen suru jiyuu ga gozaimasu.
choices.arti: Academics are also citizens, so they have the freedom to clarify political positions and speak out.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)はごもっともです。ただし、学術的(がくじゅつてき)知見(ちけん)に基(もと)づく憲法(けんぽう)解釈(かいしゃく)を社会(しゃかい)に提示(ていじ)することは、学者(がくしゃ)の社会的(しゃかいてき)責任(せきにん)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki wa gomottomo desu. Tadashi, gakujutsuteki chiken ni motozuku kenpou kaishaku wo shakai ni teiji suru koto wa, gakusha no shakaiteki sekinin ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: Your suggestion is very reasonable. However, presenting constitutional interpretations to the public based on academic knowledge is nothing other than an academic's social responsibility.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (nothing other than) to affirm that academic contribution is not partisan but a responsibility, responding to 「たるもの」 in a balanced manner. The incorrect answer blurs the line between political opinion and academic analysis.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の改正(かいせい)案(あん)における緊急(きんきゅう)事態(じたい)条項(じょうこう)は、行政権(ぎょうせいけん)の過度(かど)な集中(しゅうちゅう)を招(まね)きかねず、権力(けんりょく)分立(ぶんりつ)の原則(げんそく)からすれば極(きわ)めて問題(もんだい)がございます。
question.romaji: Konkai no kaisei an ni okeru kinkyuu jitai joukou wa, gyouseiken no kado na shuuchuu wo maneki kanezu, kenryoku bunritsu no gensoku kara sureba kiwamete mondai ga gozaimasu.
question.arti: The emergency powers article in the current amendment bill could result in excessive concentration of executive power, and from the principle of separation of powers, this is highly problematic.

choices.jepang: 緊急(きんきゅう)時(じ)には迅速(じんそく)な対応(たいおう)が求(もと)められますので、権力(けんりょく)の集中(しゅうちゅう)はやむを得(え)ないことです。
choices.romaji: Kinkyuu ji ni wa jinsoku na taiou ga motomeraremasu no de, kenryoku no shuuchuu wa yamu wo enai koto desu.
choices.arti: In emergencies, rapid response is needed, so concentration of power is unavoidable.

choices.jepang: ご懸念(けねん)を禁(きん)じ得(え)ません。緊急(きんきゅう)事態(じたい)においても司法(しほう)審査(しんさ)の機能(きのう)を維持(いじ)し、行政権(ぎょうせいけん)の濫用(らんよう)を防(ふせ)ぐ歯止(はど)めを設(もう)けるべきです。
choices.romaji: Go kenen wo kin ji emasen. Kinkyuu jitai ni oite mo shihou shinsa no kinou wo iji shi, gyouseiken no ran'you wo fusegu hadome wo moukeru beki desu.
choices.arti: I cannot help but feel concerned. Even in emergencies, judicial oversight functions must be maintained and mechanisms to check executive power abuse must be established.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～を禁じ得ない」 (cannot help but) to express genuine concern about the analysis from 「からすれば」 (from the perspective of). The incorrect answer too readily accepts concentration of power without oversight mechanisms.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 国民(こくみん)主権(しゅけん)の原理(げんり)に即(そく)して考(かんが)えれば、憲法(けんぽう)改正(かいせい)の最終的(さいしゅうてき)な決定権(けっていけん)は国民(こくみん)にあり、国会(こっかい)はその発議(はつぎ)機関(きかん)に過(す)ぎません。
question.romaji: Kokumin shuken no genri ni soku shite kangaereba, kenpou kaisei no saishuu tekina ketteiken wa kokumin ni ari, kokkai wa sono hatsugi kikan ni sugimasen.
question.arti: Thinking based on the principle of popular sovereignty, the final decision on constitutional amendment rests with the people, and parliament is merely a proposing body.

choices.jepang: その通(とお)りです。しかしながら、発議(はつぎ)機関(きかん)としての国会(こっかい)の責任(せきにん)もまた重大(じゅうだい)であり、十分(じゅうぶん)な審議(しんぎ)なくして発議(はつぎ)することは許(ゆる)されません。
choices.romaji: Sono toori desu. Shikashi nagara, hatsugi kikan to shite no kokkai no sekinin mo mata juudai de ari, juubun na shingi naku shite hatsugi suru koto wa yurusaremasen.
choices.arti: Indeed so. However, parliament's responsibility as a proposing body is also very significant, and proposing without adequate deliberation cannot be permitted.

choices.jepang: 国民(こくみん)投票(とうひょう)は煩雑(はんざつ)な手続(てつづ)きですので、国会(こっかい)の議決(ぎけつ)のみで改正(かいせい)を完了(かんりょう)できるようにすべきです。
choices.romaji: Kokumin touhyou wa hanzatsu na tetsuzuki desu no de, kokkai no giketsu nomi de kaisei wo kanryou dekiru you ni subeki desu.
choices.arti: Referendums are a complicated procedure, so amendments should be completable with parliamentary decisions alone.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to 「に即して」 (based on) by adding a layer of parliamentary responsibility using 「なくして～ない」. The incorrect answer removes the fundamental element of popular sovereignty from the amendment process.

conversation.speaker: 藤原(ふじわら)憲法(けんぽう)学者(がくしゃ)

question.jepang: 改正(かいせい)反対(はんたい)派(は)の懸念(けねん)は、立憲主義(りっけんしゅぎ)を守(まも)らんとする切実(せつじつ)な思(おも)いからにほかならないことを、与党(よとう)は深(ふか)く認識(にんしき)すべきです。
question.romaji: Kaisei hantai ha no kenen wa, rikkenshugi wo mamoran to suru setsujitsu na omoi kara ni hoka naranai koto wo, yotou wa fukaku ninshiki subeki desu.
question.arti: The ruling party must deeply recognize that the concerns of amendment opponents are nothing other than a sincere desire to protect constitutionalism.

choices.jepang: 反対(はんたい)派(は)は単(たん)に政治的(せいじてき)な理由(りゆう)で反対(はんたい)しているだけですので、その意見(いけん)を過度(かど)に重視(じゅうし)する必要(ひつよう)はございません。
choices.romaji: Hantai ha wa tan ni seijiteki na riyuu de hantai shite iru dake desu no de, sono iken wo kado ni juushi suru hitsuyou wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: Opponents are merely opposing for political reasons, so there is no need to pay too much attention to their opinions.

choices.jepang: ご慧眼(けいがん)に敬服(けいふく)いたします。建設的(けんせつてき)な対話(たいわ)を通(つう)じて双方(そうほう)の懸念(けねん)を解消(かいしょう)すべく、超党派(ちょうとうは)の協議(きょうぎ)の場(ば)を設(もう)ける所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Go keigan ni keifuku itashimasu. Kensetsuteki na taiwa wo tsuujite souhou no kenen wo kaishou subeku, choutouha no kyougi no ba wo moukeru shozon desu.
choices.arti: I admire your incisive perspective. In order to address the concerns of both sides through constructive dialogue, I intend to establish a cross-party consultative forum.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 (in order to) and 「所存です」 (intend to) to respond to 「にほかならない」 with concrete bridge-building action. The incorrect answer delegitimizes opposition concerns that have been stated as sincere.

conversation.speaker: 渡辺(わたなべ)議員(ぎいん)

question.jepang: 憲法(けんぽう)改正(かいせい)という国家(こっか)百年(ひゃくねん)の大計(たいけい)は、一世代(いちせだい)の利害(りがい)を超(こ)え、将来(しょうらい)世代(せだい)の権利(けんり)をも見据(みす)えたものでなくてはなりません。
question.romaji: Kenpou kaisei to iu kokka hyakunen no taikei wa, ichi sedai no rigai wo koe, shourai sedai no kenri wo mo misue ta mono de nakute wa narimasen.
question.arti: The grand plan called constitutional amendment spanning a hundred years must transcend the interests of a single generation and also consider the rights of future generations.

choices.jepang: 将来(しょうらい)のことは将来(しょうらい)の世代(せだい)が決(き)めればよいのですから、現在(げんざい)の必要性(ひつようせい)に応(おう)じて改正(かいせい)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Shourai no koto wa shourai no sedai ga kimereba yoi no desu kara, genzai no hitsuyousei ni oujite kaisei subeki desu.
choices.arti: Future matters should be decided by future generations; what matters is that the amendment meets current needs.

choices.jepang: まさに至言(しげん)でございます。世代間(せだいかん)の公正(こうせい)という視座(しざ)なくしては、真(しん)に持続(じぞく)可能(かのう)な憲法(けんぽう)秩序(ちつじょ)は構築(こうちく)し得(え)ないと確信(かくしん)いたします。
choices.romaji: Masa ni shigen de gozaimasu. Sedaikan no kousei to iu shiza naku shite wa, shin ni jizoku kanou na kenpou chitsujo wa kouchiku shi enai to kakushin itashimasu.
choices.arti: Those are truly wise words. I am convinced that without a perspective of intergenerational justice, a truly sustainable constitutional order cannot be built.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「なくしては～得ない」 (without... cannot) to affirm the importance of a long-term perspective in constitutional amendment. The incorrect answer demonstrates a presentist attitude that ignores responsibility to future generations.

conversation.speaker: 高橋(たかはし)都市(とし)計画(けいかく)部長(ぶちょう)

question.jepang: 今回(こんかい)の再開発(さいかいはつ)計画(けいかく)ともなると、数万(すうまん)人(にん)の住民(じゅうみん)の生活(せいかつ)に直接(ちょくせつ)影響(えいきょう)を及(およ)ぼすため、あらゆる側面(そくめん)から慎重(しんちょう)に検討(けんとう)せねばなりません。
question.romaji: Konkai no saikaihatsu keikaku to mo naru to, suuman nin no juumin no seikatsu ni chokusetsu eikyou wo oyobosu tame, arayuru sokumen kara shinchou ni kentou seneba narimasen.
question.arti: When it comes to this redevelopment plan, as it directly impacts the lives of tens of thousands of residents, it must be examined carefully from every angle.

choices.jepang: 住民(じゅうみん)への影響(えいきょう)は最小限(さいしょうげん)ですので、計画(けいかく)通(どお)りに迅速(じんそく)に進(すす)めるべきかと思(おも)います。
choices.romaji: Juumin e no eikyou wa saishlougen desu no de, keikaku doori ni jinsoku ni susumeru beki ka to omoimasu.
choices.arti: The impact on residents is minimal, so I believe it should proceed quickly according to plan.

choices.jepang: おっしゃる通(とお)りです。住民(じゅうみん)の声(こえ)を丁寧(ていねい)に拾(ひろ)い上(あ)げるべく、パブリックコメントの期間(きかん)を十分(じゅうぶん)に確保(かくほ)する所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Ossharu toori desu. Juumin no koe wo teinei ni hiroi ageru beku, paburikku komento no kikan wo juubun ni kakuho suru shozon desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. In order to listen carefully to residents' voices, I intend to ensure an adequate public comment period.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 (in order to) and 「所存です」 (intend to) showing commitment to public participation, matching 「ともなると」 emphasizing the project's significant impact. The incorrect answer downplays the clearly significant impact on residents.

conversation.speaker: 木村(きむら)住民(じゅうみん)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 住民(じゅうみん)は立(た)ち退(の)きの不安(ふあん)をものともせず、再開発(さいかいはつ)の恩恵(おんけい)を信(しん)じて協力(きょうりょく)してまいりましたが、約束(やくそく)されていた補償(ほしょう)が履行(りこう)されておりません。
question.romaji: Juumin wa tachinoki no fuan wo mono to mo sezu, saikaihatsu no onkei wo shinjite kyouryoku shite mairimashita ga, yakusoku sarete ita hoshou ga rikou sarete orimasen.
question.arti: Residents cooperated while undeterred by the anxiety of eviction, trusting in the benefits of redevelopment, yet the promised compensation has not been fulfilled.

choices.jepang: 補償(ほしょう)の遅延(ちえん)は予算(よさん)の関係(かんけい)でやむを得(え)ない面(めん)もございますが、住民(じゅうみん)の方々(かたがた)のご心配(しんぱい)は杞憂(きゆう)に過(す)ぎません。
choices.romaji: Hoshou no chien wa yosan no kankei de yamu wo enai men mo gozaimasu ga, juumin no katagata no go shinpai wa kiyuu ni sugimasen.
choices.arti: Compensation delays have unavoidable aspects due to budget issues, but residents' concerns are merely unfounded fears.

choices.jepang: 住民(じゅうみん)の皆様(みなさま)のご協力(きょうりょく)に深(ふか)く感謝(かんしゃ)申(もう)し上(あ)げます。補償(ほしょう)の履行(りこう)は行政(ぎょうせい)としての責務(せきむ)にほかならず、早急(さっきゅう)に対応(たいおう)いたします。
choices.romaji: Juumin no minasama no go kyouryoku ni fukaku kansha moushi agemasu. Hoshou no rikou wa gyousei to shite no sekimu ni hoka narazu, sakkyuu ni taiou itashimasu.
choices.arti: I express my deepest gratitude for all residents' cooperation. Fulfilling compensation is nothing other than the government's obligation, and it will be addressed immediately.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならず」 (nothing other than) to affirm government responsibility for promised compensation, honoring the courage of residents shown through 「をものともせず」. The incorrect answer dismisses residents' legitimate concerns.

conversation.speaker: 高橋(たかはし)都市(とし)計画(けいかく)部長(ぶちょう)

question.jepang: この地域(ちいき)の歴史的(れきしてき)景観(けいかん)の喪失(そうしつ)は、住民(じゅうみん)に深(ふか)い悲(かな)しみを禁(きん)じ得(え)ないものであり、再開発(さいかいはつ)計画(けいかく)には景観(けいかん)保全(ほぜん)の視点(してん)が不可欠(ふかけつ)です。
question.romaji: Kono chiiki no rekishiteki keikan no soushitsu wa, juumin ni fukai kanashimi wo kin ji enai mono de ari, saikaihatsu keikaku ni wa keikan hozen no shiten ga fukaketsu desu.
question.arti: The loss of the area's historic landscape inevitably evokes deep sadness among residents, and the perspective of landscape preservation is crucial in redevelopment plans.

choices.jepang: 景観(けいかん)保全(ほぜん)と都市(とし)開発(かいはつ)の両立(りょうりつ)は現実的(げんじつてき)に不可能(ふかのう)ですので、開発(かいはつ)を優先(ゆうせん)すべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Keikan hozen to toshi kaihatsu no ryouritsu wa genjitsuteki ni fukanou desu no de, kaihatsu wo yuusen subeki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Since balancing landscape preservation and urban development is realistically impossible, I believe development must take priority.

choices.jepang: 歴史的(れきしてき)景観(けいかん)に即(そく)した建築(けんちく)デザインガイドラインを策定(さくてい)し、新旧(しんきゅう)の調和(ちょうわ)を実現(じつげん)する計画(けいかく)を提案(ていあん)いたします。
choices.romaji: Rekishiteki keikan ni soku shita kenchiku dezain gaidorain wo sakutei shi, shinkyuu no chouwa wo jitsugen suru keikaku wo teian itashimasu.
choices.arti: I propose establishing architectural design guidelines in accordance with the historic landscape, and a plan that realizes harmony between old and new.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～に即した」 (in accordance with) to offer a concrete solution that respects the feelings of 「を禁じ得ない」 residents regarding the loss of historic landscape. The incorrect answer ignores important emotional and cultural aspects.

conversation.speaker: 木村(きむら)住民(じゅうみん)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 行政(ぎょうせい)の長(ちょう)ともあろう方(かた)が、住民(じゅうみん)説明会(せつめいかい)に一度(いちど)も出席(しゅっせき)されないとは、住民(じゅうみん)軽視(けいし)も甚(はなは)だしいと言(い)わざるを得(え)ません。
question.romaji: Gyousei no chou to mo arou kata ga, juumin setsumeikai ni ichi do mo shusseki sarenai to wa, juumin keishi mo hanahada shii to iwazaru wo emasen.
question.arti: A person who is supposed to be the head of government has never attended a single public explanation session for residents; that can only be called an outrageous neglect of citizens.

choices.jepang: 首長(しゅちょう)は多忙(たぼう)ですので、担当(たんとう)部署(ぶしょ)が対応(たいおう)すれば十分(じゅうぶん)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Shuchou wa tabou desu no de, tantou busho ga taiou sureba juubun de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: The regional head is busy, so isn't it sufficient if the relevant department handles it?

choices.jepang: 誠(まこと)に申(もう)し訳(わけ)ございません。行政(ぎょうせい)の責任者(せきにんしゃ)たるもの、住民(じゅうみん)と直接(ちょくせつ)向(む)き合(あ)うことこそが信頼(しんらい)回復(かいふく)の第一歩(だいいっぽ)です。次回(じかい)より必(かなら)ず出席(しゅっせき)いたします。
choices.romaji: Makoto ni moushiwake gozaimasen. Gyousei no sekininsha taru mono, juumin to chokusetsu mukiau koto koso ga shinrai kaifuku no daiippo desu. Jikai yori kanarazu shusseki itashimasu.
choices.arti: I sincerely apologize. As the person responsible for governance, directly facing residents is the first step toward restoring trust. I will certainly attend from the next meeting.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「たるもの」 (as one who is) to show acceptance of responsibility, responding to the criticism of 「ともあろう方が」 with a concrete commitment. The incorrect answer delegates responsibility and trivializes the importance of leadership presence.

conversation.speaker: 高橋(たかはし)都市(とし)計画(けいかく)部長(ぶちょう)

question.jepang: 持続(じぞく)可能(かのう)な都市(とし)開発(かいはつ)は、環境(かんきょう)への配慮(はいりょ)なくしては実現(じつげん)し得(え)ず、グリーンインフラの整備(せいび)が急務(きゅうむ)でございます。
question.romaji: Jizoku kanou na toshi kaihatsu wa, kankyou e no hairyo naku shite wa jitsugen shi ezu, guriin infura no seibi ga kyuumu de gozaimasu.
question.arti: Sustainable urban development cannot be realized without environmental consideration, and the development of green infrastructure is an urgent task.

choices.jepang: グリーンインフラは費用(ひよう)対(たい)効果(こうか)が低(ひく)いですので、従来型(じゅうらいがた)のインフラ整備(せいび)を優先(ゆうせん)すべきではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Guriin infura wa hiyou tai kouka ga hikui desu no de, juurai gata no infura seibi wo yuusen subeki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Green infrastructure has low cost-effectiveness; shouldn't we prioritize conventional infrastructure development?

choices.jepang: 全(まった)く同感(どうかん)です。都市(とし)緑化(りょっか)率(りつ)の数値(すうち)目標(もくひょう)を設定(せってい)し、開発(かいはつ)事業者(じぎょうしゃ)にも緑化(りょっか)義務(ぎむ)を課(か)すべく、条例(じょうれい)の改正(かいせい)を進(すす)める所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Mattaku doukan desu. Toshi ryokka ritsu no suuchi mokuhyou wo settei shi, kaihatsu jigyousha ni mo ryokka gimu wo kasubeku, jourei no kaisei wo susumeru shozon desu.
choices.arti: I fully agree. I intend to set numerical targets for the city's green coverage ratio and advance ordinance revisions to mandate greening for developers.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 (in order to) and 「所存です」 to respond to 「なくしては～得ない」 with concrete, measurable policy. The incorrect answer ignores environmental needs already stated as prerequisites for sustainable development.

conversation.speaker: 木村(きむら)住民(じゅうみん)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 防災(ぼうさい)対策(たいさく)は一日(いちにち)たりとも怠(おこた)ることは許(ゆる)されず、特(とく)に高齢者(こうれいしゃ)の避難(ひなん)経路(けいろ)の確保(かくほ)は最優先(さいゆうせん)事項(じこう)です。
question.romaji: Bousai taisaku wa ichi nichi tari to mo okotaru koto wa yurusarezu, toku ni koureisha no hinan keiro no kakuho wa saiyuusen jikou desu.
question.arti: Disaster prevention measures must not be neglected even for a single day, and securing evacuation routes for the elderly is the top priority.

choices.jepang: 高齢者(こうれいしゃ)の避難(ひなん)は家族(かぞく)の責任(せきにん)ですので、行政(ぎょうせい)がそこまで関与(かんよ)する必要(ひつよう)はないかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Koureisha no hinan wa kazoku no sekinin desu no de, gyousei ga soko made kan'yo suru hitsuyou wa nai ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: Elderly evacuation is a family responsibility, so I believe the government need not be involved to that extent.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の通(とお)りです。再開発(さいかいはつ)計画(けいかく)にバリアフリーの避難(ひなん)経路(けいろ)を組(く)み込(こ)み、高齢者(こうれいしゃ)の安全(あんぜん)を最優先(さいゆうせん)に確保(かくほ)いたします。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no toori desu. Saikaihatsu keikaku ni bariafurii no hinan keiro wo kumikomi, koureisha no anzen wo saiyuusen ni kakuho itashimasu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. We will integrate barrier-free evacuation routes into the redevelopment plan and secure the safety of the elderly as the top priority.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer responds to the urgency of 「たりとも～許されず」 with concrete action integrating elderly needs into planning. The incorrect answer shifts public responsibility to families.

conversation.speaker: 高橋(たかはし)都市(とし)計画(けいかく)部長(ぶちょう)

question.jepang: この地域(ちいき)ならではのコミュニティの絆(きずな)は、再開発(さいかいはつ)においても最大限(さいだいげん)に尊重(そんちょう)されるべきものと考(かんが)えております。
question.romaji: Kono chiiki nara de wa no komyuniti no kizuna wa, saikaihatsu ni oite mo saidaigen ni sonchou sareru beki mono to kangaete orimasu.
question.arti: The community bonds that are the hallmark of this area should be respected as much as possible even in redevelopment.

choices.jepang: コミュニティの絆(きずな)は時代(じだい)とともに自然(しぜん)に変化(へんか)するものですので、特別(とくべつ)な配慮(はいりょ)は不要(ふよう)ではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Komyuniti no kizuna wa jidai to tomo ni shizen ni henka suru mono desu no de, tokubetsu na hairyo wa fuyou de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Community bonds change naturally over time, so isn't special attention unnecessary?
