choices.romaji: Sangyoukai to no kyouchou mo taisetsu desu no de, aru teido no juunansei wo motaseru beki de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Cooperation with industry is also important, so shouldn't a certain degree of flexibility be provided?

choices.jepang: まさにその通(とお)りです。規制(きせい)当局(とうきょく)の独立性(どくりつせい)こそが国民(こくみん)の信頼(しんらい)の基盤(きばん)であり、科学的(かがくてき)根拠(こんきょ)に基(もと)づく判断(はんだん)をおいて他(ほか)に拠(よ)るべき基準(きじゅん)はございません。
choices.romaji: Masa ni sono toori desu. Kisei toukyoku no dokuritsusei koso ga kokumin no shinrai no kiban de ari, kagakuteki konkyo ni motozuku handan wo oite hoka ni yoru beki kijun wa gozaimasen.
choices.arti: Indeed so. The independence of the regulatory agency is the foundation of public trust, and there is no standard other than science-based evaluation to be followed.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「をおいて他に～ない」 (there is no other than) to affirm the supremacy of scientific evidence, matching 「にほかならず」 defining the regulatory mission. The incorrect answer compromises regulatory independence for industry interests.

conversation.speaker: 井上(いのうえ)製薬(せいやく)会社(がいしゃ)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 臨床(りんしょう)試験(しけん)の被験者(ひけんしゃ)保護(ほご)は一瞬(いっしゅん)たりとも疎(おろそ)かにしてはならず、インフォームドコンセントの徹底(てってい)は製薬(せいやく)企業(きぎょう)の絶対的(ぜったいてき)責務(せきむ)です。
question.romaji: Rinshou shiken no hikensha hogo wa isshun tari to mo orosoka ni shite wa narazu, infoomudo konsento no tettei wa seiyaku kigyou no zettaiteki sekimu desu.
question.arti: The protection of clinical trial subjects must not be neglected even for a second, and the thorough implementation of informed consent is an absolute obligation of pharmaceutical companies.

choices.jepang: インフォームドコンセントは形式的(けいしきてき)な手続(てつづ)きに過(す)ぎませんので、効率化(こうりつか)のために簡略化(かんりゃくか)してもよいのではないでしょうか。
choices.romaji: Infoomudo konsento wa keishikiteki na tetsuzuki ni sugimasen no de, kouritsuka no tame ni kanryakuka shite mo yoi no de wa nai deshou ka.
choices.arti: Informed consent is merely a formal procedure, so shouldn't it be simplified for efficiency?

choices.jepang: ご認識(にんしき)に深(ふか)く賛同(さんどう)いたします。被験者(ひけんしゃ)の権利(けんり)と尊厳(そんげん)を守(まも)ることこそが、倫理的(りんりてき)な臨床(りんしょう)研究(けんきゅう)の大前提(だいぜんてい)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Go ninshiki ni fukaku sandou itashimasu. Hikensha no kenri to songen wo mamoru koto koso ga, rinriteki na rinshou kenkyuu no daizentei ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: I strongly agree with your view. Protecting the rights and dignity of trial subjects is nothing other than the foremost prerequisite of ethical clinical research.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (nothing other than) to affirm that subject protection is an absolute prerequisite, matching 「たりとも～てはならない」 emphasizing obligation without exception. The incorrect answer trivializes informed consent, a pillar of research ethics.

conversation.speaker: 松田(まつだ)薬事(やくじ)審議官(しんぎかん)

question.jepang: 日本(にほん)の薬事(やくじ)規制(きせい)ならではの厳格(げんかく)さは、国際的(こくさいてき)にも高(たか)く評価(ひょうか)されており、医薬品(いやくひん)の品質(ひんしつ)管理(かんり)における世界(せかい)の模範(もはん)となっております。
question.romaji: Nihon no yakuji kisei nara de wa no genkakusa wa, kokusaiteki ni mo takaku hyouka sarete ori, iyakuhin no hinshitsu kanri ni okeru sekai no mohan to natte orimasu.
question.arti: The stringent pharmaceutical regulation that is the hallmark of Japan is highly evaluated internationally and has become a world model for drug quality management.

choices.jepang: 国際的(こくさいてき)な規制(きせい)調和(ちょうわ)の流(なが)れに鑑(かんが)み、日本(にほん)独自(どくじ)の基準(きじゅん)は廃止(はいし)して海外(かいがい)基準(きじゅん)に統一(とういつ)すべきです。
choices.romaji: Kokusaiteki na kisei chouwa no nagare ni kangami, nihon dokuji no kijun wa haishi shite kaigai kijun ni touitsu subeki desu.
choices.arti: Given the trend of international regulatory harmonization, Japan's unique standards should be eliminated and unified with foreign standards.

choices.jepang: その独自(どくじ)の強(つよ)みを堅持(けんじ)しつつ、国際(こくさい)規制(きせい)調和(ちょうわ)にも積極的(せっきょくてき)に貢献(こうけん)し、グローバルな医薬品(いやくひん)安全性(あんぜんせい)の向上(こうじょう)を図(はか)るべきかと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Sono dokuji no tsuyomi wo kenji shitsutsu, kokusai kisei chouwa ni mo sekkyokuteki ni kouken shi, gurooubaru na iyakuhin anzensei no koujou wo hakaru beki ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: I believe that while maintaining that unique strength, we should also actively contribute to international regulatory harmonization and enhance global drug safety.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer appreciates the 「ならではの」 (unique to) stringency of Japan's regulation while seeing opportunities for global contribution. The incorrect answer eliminates unique advantages for standardization that may not be superior.

conversation.speaker: 井上(いのうえ)製薬(せいやく)会社(がいしゃ)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 製薬(せいやく)企業(きぎょう)たるもの、利益(りえき)追求(ついきゅう)のみならず、医薬品(いやくひん)アクセスの公平性(こうへいせい)にも責任(せきにん)を負(お)うべきであると認識(にんしき)しております。
question.romaji: Seiyaku kigyou taru mono, rieki tsuikyuu nomi narazu, iyakuhin akusesu no kouheisei ni mo sekinin wo ou beki de aru to ninshiki shite orimasu.
question.arti: As a true pharmaceutical company, we recognize that we must be accountable not only for profit-seeking but also for equity in drug access.

choices.jepang: 企業(きぎょう)の第一(だいいち)義務(ぎむ)は株主(かぶぬし)への利益(りえき)還元(かんげん)ですので、アクセスの公平性(こうへいせい)は行政(ぎょうせい)が担(にな)うべき課題(かだい)です。
choices.romaji: Kigyou no daiichi gimu wa kabunushi e no rieki kangen desu no de, akusesu no kouheisei wa gyousei ga ninau beki kadai desu.
choices.arti: A company's primary obligation is returning profits to shareholders, so equitable access is a task that should be borne by the government.

choices.jepang: 極(きわ)めて重要(じゅうよう)なご認識(にんしき)です。途上国(とじょうこく)への医薬品(いやくひん)供給(きょうきゅう)と薬価(やっか)の適正化(てきせいか)は、製薬(せいやく)業界(ぎょうかい)全体(ぜんたい)の社会的(しゃかいてき)責任(せきにん)にほかなりません。
choices.romaji: Kiwamete juuyou na go ninshiki desu. Tojoukoku e no iyakuhin kyoukyuu to yakka no tekiseika wa, seiyaku gyoukai zentai no shakaiteki sekinin ni hoka narimasen.
choices.arti: That is a very important perspective. Drug supply to developing nations and fair drug pricing are nothing other than the social responsibility of the entire pharmaceutical industry.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「にほかならない」 (nothing other than) to affirm the social responsibility of the pharmaceutical industry, matching 「たるもの」 demanding high ethical standards. The incorrect answer separates corporate responsibility from health equity issues.

conversation.speaker: 松田(まつだ)薬事(やくじ)審議官(しんぎかん)

question.jepang: ジェネリック医薬品(いやくひん)の普及(ふきゅう)促進(そくしん)は、医療費(いりょうひ)削減(さくげん)の観点(かんてん)からすれば避(さ)けて通(とお)れない政策(せいさく)課題(かだい)であり、品質(ひんしつ)と安全性(あんぜんせい)の担保(たんぽ)が前提(ぜんてい)です。
question.romaji: Jenerikku iyakuhin no fukyuu sokushin wa, iryouhi sakugen no kanten kara sureba sakete toorenai seisaku kadai de ari, hinshitsu to anzensei no tanpo ga zentei desu.
question.arti: Promoting the spread of generic drugs, from the perspective of reducing medical costs, is a policy issue that cannot be avoided, and quality and safety assurance is its prerequisite.

choices.jepang: ジェネリック医薬品(いやくひん)は先発品(せんぱつひん)より品質(ひんしつ)が劣(おと)るという認識(にんしき)が根強(ねづよ)いですので、普及(ふきゅう)は困難(こんなん)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: Jenerikku iyakuhin wa senpatsuhin yori hinshitsu ga otoru to iu ninshiki ga nezuyoi desu no de, fukyuu wa konnan ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: The perception that generics are lower quality than branded drugs remains strong, so their spread is difficult in my view.

choices.jepang: ご指摘(してき)の通(とお)りです。品質(ひんしつ)基準(きじゅん)に即(そく)した厳格(げんかく)な検査(けんさ)体制(たいせい)を構築(こうちく)し、国民(こくみん)の信頼(しんらい)を得(え)ることこそが普及(ふきゅう)の鍵(かぎ)です。
choices.romaji: Go shiteki no toori desu. Hinshitsu kijun ni soku shita genkaku na kensa taisei wo kouchiku shi, kokumin no shinrai wo eru koto koso ga fukyuu no kagi desu.
choices.arti: Absolutely. Building a rigorous inspection system in accordance with quality standards and earning public trust is the key to their spread.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「に即した」 (in accordance with) to offer a concrete solution addressing the 「からすれば」 perspective, emphasizing that public trust is built through rigorous systems. The incorrect answer resigns to negative perceptions without attempting to address them.

conversation.speaker: 井上(いのうえ)製薬(せいやく)会社(がいしゃ)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 新型(しんがた)感染症(かんせんしょう)のパンデミック時(じ)には、ワクチン開発(かいはつ)の遅(おく)れが数(すう)万人(まんにん)の命(いのち)を奪(うば)わずにはおかないという現実(げんじつ)を、我々(われわれ)は痛感(つうかん)いたしました。
question.romaji: Shingata kansenshou no pandemikku ji ni wa, wakuchin kaihatsu no okure ga suu mannin no inochi wo ubawazu ni wa okanai to iu genjitsu wo, wareware wa tsuukan itashimashita.
question.arti: During the new infectious disease pandemic, we keenly felt the reality that delays in vaccine development would inevitably claim tens of thousands of lives.

choices.jepang: パンデミック時(じ)であっても通常(つうじょう)の審査(しんさ)手続(てつづ)きを省略(しょうりゃく)すべきではありません。安全性(あんぜんせい)は何(なに)があっても最優先(さいゆうせん)です。
choices.romaji: Pandemikku ji de atte mo tsuujou no shinsa tetsuzuki wo shouryaku subeki de wa arimasen. Anzensei wa nani ga atte mo saiyuusen desu.
choices.arti: Even during a pandemic, normal review procedures must not be bypassed. Safety is the top priority no matter what.

choices.jepang: その教訓(きょうくん)を踏(ふ)まえ、緊急(きんきゅう)使用(しよう)許可(きょか)制度(せいど)の整備(せいび)と平時(へいじ)からのワクチン開発(かいはつ)基盤(きばん)強化(きょうか)を両輪(りょうりん)として進(すす)めるべく、産官学(さんかんがく)連携(れんけい)の枠組(わくぐ)みを構築(こうちく)する所存(しょぞん)です。
choices.romaji: Sono kyoukun wo fumae, kinkyuu shiyou kyoka seido no seibi to heiji kara no wakuchin kaihatsu kiban kyouka wo ryourin to shite susumeru beku, sankangaku renkei no wakugumi wo kouchiku suru shozon desu.
choices.arti: Based on that lesson, I intend to build an industry-government-academia cooperation framework, advancing both the establishment of an emergency use authorization system and the strengthening of vaccine development foundations during peacetime as twin pillars.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 to respond to the reality of 「ずにはおかない」 with a systemic solution balancing speed and safety. The incorrect answer is too rigid and fails to learn from the pandemic experience about the need for emergency mechanisms.

conversation.speaker: 松田(まつだ)薬事(やくじ)審議官(しんぎかん)

question.jepang: AI技術(ぎじゅつ)を活用(かつよう)した創薬(そうやく)プロセスの革新(かくしん)は、従来(じゅうらい)の規制(きせい)の枠組(わくぐ)みを根本(こんぽん)から見直(みなお)すことなしには対応(たいおう)し得(え)ない課題(かだい)をもたらしております。
question.romaji: AI gijutsu wo katsuyou shita souyaku purosesu no kakushin wa, juurai no kisei no wakugumi wo konpon kara minaosu koto nashi ni wa taiou shi enai kadai wo motarashite orimasu.
question.arti: Innovation in AI-driven drug discovery brings challenges that cannot be addressed without fundamentally reviewing the conventional regulatory framework.

choices.jepang: AI創薬(そうやく)は未知(みち)の領域(りょういき)ですので、従来(じゅうらい)の規制(きせい)をそのまま適用(てきよう)すれば十分(じゅうぶん)かと存(ぞん)じます。
choices.romaji: AI souyaku wa michi no ryouiki desu no de, juurai no kisei wo sono mama tekiyou sureba juubun ka to zonjimasu.
choices.arti: AI drug discovery is still an unknown field, so I believe it is sufficient to apply conventional regulations as they are.

choices.jepang: 時代(じだい)の要請(ようせい)に応(おう)じた規制(きせい)改革(かいかく)は不可避(ふかひ)です。AIが生成(せいせい)した候補(こうほ)化合物(かごうぶつ)の評価(ひょうか)基準(きじゅん)を策定(さくてい)すべく、専門家(せんもんか)委員会(いいんかい)を早急(さっきゅう)に設置(せっち)いたします。
choices.romaji: Jidai no yousei ni oujita kisei kaikaku wa fukahi desu. AI ga seisei shita kouho kagoubutsu no hyouka kijun wo sakutei subeku, senmonka iinkai wo sakkyuu ni secchi itashimasu.
choices.arti: Regulatory reform keeping pace with the times is unavoidable. In order to develop evaluation standards for AI-generated candidate compounds, we will promptly establish an expert committee.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 to respond to the challenge of 「ことなしには～得ない」 with forward-looking concrete action. The incorrect answer fails to respond to the urgency of the paradigm shift brought by AI in drug discovery.

conversation.speaker: 井上(いのうえ)製薬(せいやく)会社(がいしゃ)代表(だいひょう)

question.jepang: 規制(きせい)当局(とうきょく)と製薬(せいやく)企業(きぎょう)の信頼(しんらい)関係(かんけい)なくしては、安全(あんぜん)かつ迅速(じんそく)な新薬(しんやく)供給(きょうきゅう)は実現(じつげん)し得(え)ず、最終的(さいしゅうてき)に不利益(ふりえき)を被(こうむ)るのは患者(かんじゃ)にほかなりません。
question.romaji: Kisei toukyoku to seiyaku kigyou no shinrai kankei naku shite wa, anzen katsu jinsoku na shinyaku kyoukyuu wa jitsugen shi ezu, saishuu tekini furieki wo koumuru no wa kanja ni hoka narimasen.
question.arti: Without mutual trust between regulatory agencies and pharmaceutical companies, the safe and swift provision of new drugs cannot be realized, and ultimately it is none other than patients who are harmed.

choices.jepang: 規制(きせい)当局(とうきょく)と企業(きぎょう)は本来(ほんらい)対立(たいりつ)関係(かんけい)にありますので、信頼(しんらい)関係(かんけい)の構築(こうちく)は非現実的(ひげんじつてき)です。
choices.romaji: Kisei toukyoku to kigyou wa honrai tairitsu kankei ni arimasu no de, shinrai kankei no kouchiku wa higenjitsuteki desu.
choices.arti: Regulatory agencies and companies are fundamentally in an adversarial relationship, so building mutual trust is unrealistic.

choices.jepang: 全(まった)く同感(どうかん)です。患者(かんじゃ)中心(ちゅうしん)の医療(いりょう)を実現(じつげん)すべく、規制(きせい)当局(とうきょく)と産業界(さんぎょうかい)が建設的(けんせつてき)な対話(たいわ)を重(かさ)ね、相互(そうご)理解(りかい)を深(ふか)めてまいりましょう。
choices.romaji: Mattaku doukan desu. Kanja chuushin no iryou wo jitsugen subeku, kisei toukyoku to sangyoukai ga kensetsuteki na taiwa wo kasane, sougo rikai wo fukamete mairimashou.
choices.arti: I fully agree. In order to realize patient-centered healthcare, let regulatory agencies and industry continue constructive dialogue and deepen mutual understanding.

conversation.explanation: The correct answer uses 「～べく」 to respond to 「なくしては～得ない」 and 「にほかならない」 with a collaborative vision placing patients at the center. The incorrect answer accepts an adversarial relationship as inevitable without trying to improve it.
